Eyebrow and eyelash loss simultaneously

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين شايفة كل حاجة ضعفين، خصوصاً لما بكون تعبانة. وبيجيلي تدلي في جفن عيني الشمال في الآخر اليوم. وبتعب من المضغ والكلام.
urgency_reason: Fatigable ptosis with bulbar symptoms is myasthenia gravis with risk of myasthenic crisis and respiratory failure.
recommended_action: See neurologist urgently; get AChR antibody, edrophonium test, CT chest for thymoma; monitor respiratory function.
red_flags: Difficulty breathing or swallowing liquids
Nasal voice worsening throughout day
Inability to keep eyes open

user_symptom_description: I've developed really painful cracks in both corners of my mouth that won't heal. My dentures have been slipping lately, my tongue looks red and smooth, and I feel very tired.
urgency_reason: Angular cheilitis with glossitis and fatigue suggests nutritional deficiency or anemia requiring investigation for underlying cause.
recommended_action: See GP for CBC, ferritin, B12, folate, and LFTs; if anemia confirmed, investigate source of iron loss especially GI bleeding.
red_flags: Very low hemoglobin causing breathlessness at rest
Black tarry stools
Difficulty swallowing from Plummer-Vinson syndrome

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بحس بألم وحرارة في رجلي الشمال وهي ورمانة وحمرا. ما فيش جرح. أنا شايل زيادة في الوزن وبقعد كتير.
urgency_reason: Spreading leg cellulitis with systemic signs requires antibiotic therapy and DVT must be excluded with Doppler ultrasound.
recommended_action: See GP urgently or go to urgent care for leg Doppler, CBC, and antibiotics; mark the border of erythema with a pen to track spread.
red_flags: Rapid spread beyond marked border within hours
Blistering or skin breakdown
Fever above 38.5°C with systemic toxicity

user_symptom_description: I've been having really intrusive thoughts about harming people even though I would never want to do that. These thoughts horrify me and I can't make them stop. I've been avoiding going out because of this.
urgency_reason: Ego-dystonic harm obsessions with avoidance indicate severe OCD requiring mental health evaluation; risk of worsening isolation.
recommended_action: See psychiatrist or psychologist within one week; CBT with ERP is highly effective; reassurance that ego-dystonic thoughts are a hallmark of OCD not intent.
red_flags: Any planned or impulsive action toward harm
Psychotic symptoms like commanding voices
Complete social paralysis

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وأنا بحس بألم في كتفي الأيمن وذراعي من فوق لما بحرك إيدي لجنب أو لفوق. بيصعب أعمل حاجات فوق دماغي. مش عندي رضة.
urgency_reason: Shoulder impingement is common and responds to conservative physiotherapy; no immediate danger but should be addressed for function.
recommended_action: See GP for shoulder X-ray and physiotherapy referral; avoid overhead lifting; NSAIDs for short-term pain relief; MRI if no improvement.
red_flags: Complete inability to raise arm
Night pain preventing sleep
Weakness suggesting full-thickness tear

user_symptom_description: My 7-year-old has had a limp for the past week. He says his hip hurts but sometimes the pain is in his knee. He had a cold two weeks ago. He doesn't look very ill.
extracted_symptoms: hip pain with referred knee pain
urgency_reason: Hip limp in a child requires Kocher criteria assessment to urgently exclude septic arthritis before assuming transient synovitis.
recommended_action: See pediatrician within 24 hours for CBC, CRP, ESR, and hip ultrasound; if Kocher criteria met, go to ER for aspiration.
red_flags: Fever above 38.5°C
Unable to bear any weight
CRP above 2 and WBC above 12,000

user_symptom_description: أنا 67 سنة وبقالي شهر وأنا بعاني من ضيق في التنفس بالليل وتورم في رجليا وبحس بإرهاق. عندي فشل كلوي مزمن وبروح للغسيل.
urgency_reason: Pulmonary edema in a dialysis patient requires emergency ultrafiltration; fluid overload can rapidly cause respiratory failure.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately for urgent dialysis session with ultrafiltration; cardiac evaluation and oxygen therapy needed.
red_flags: Oxygen saturation below 90%
Frothy pink sputum
Altered mental status from uremia

user_symptom_description: I've been feeling low and hopeless for the past 4 months. I've stopped going to the gym and lost interest in everything I used to love. I've been drinking more alcohol to cope.
urgency_reason: Depression with increasing alcohol use is a self-reinforcing cycle that escalates risk of suicidality and requires prompt dual management.
recommended_action: See GP or psychiatrist within one week; screen for suicidal ideation; address alcohol use as part of treatment plan; CBT and SSRI are first-line.
red_flags: Suicidal ideation or plan
Alcohol consumption exceeding 10 units daily
Inability to perform basic self-care

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بحس بألم وحرقان عند التبوّل وعندي إفرازات من تحت لونها أصفر وفيها رايحة. ألمي في بطني الأسفل برضو.
urgency_reason: Purulent discharge with pelvic pain suggests gonorrhea or PID; untreated gonorrhea causes infertility and systemic dissemination.
recommended_action: See GP or sexual health clinic urgently for cervical swabs; start ceftriaxone plus doxycycline for empirical dual coverage; notify partners.
red_flags: High fever with peritoneal signs
Positive pregnancy test
Signs of disseminated infection like joint pain and skin pustules

user_symptom_description: I have a painful, burning rash along the left side of my chest that wraps from my back to the front. It's been there for 5 days and looks like clusters of blisters. I can't wear a shirt because of the pain.
urgency_reason: Shingles requires antivirals within 72 hours of rash onset to reduce severity and prevent postherpetic neuralgia.
recommended_action: See GP today for antiviral prescription; determine if 72-hour window is still open; analgesics and possible gabapentin for neuropathic pain.
red_flags: Rash involving the nose tip or forehead
Eye pain or vision change
Immunocompromised status with spreading rash

user_symptom_description: أنا 72 سنة وبقالي شهرين بشوف خطوط متعرجة وبقعة داكنة في نص بصري في العين اليمين. مش فيه ألم بس البصر بقى مش زي زمان.
urgency_reason: Wet AMD with metamorphopsia requires urgent anti-VEGF injection within days to prevent irreversible central vision loss.
recommended_action: Go to ophthalmology urgently within 48 hours for OCT and possible anti-VEGF injection; cover unaffected eye and check with Amsler grid daily.
red_flags: Sudden dramatic worsening of distortion
New onset in the other eye
Loss of ability to read any text

user_symptom_description: My fingernails and toenails have been thickening and turning yellow-brown for the past year. They're crumbly at the edges and have separated from the nail bed in two of my toes. No pain.
urgency_reason: Fungal nail infection is not dangerous but requires nail clipping culture confirmation before starting systemic antifungals.
recommended_action: See dermatologist for nail clipping microscopy and culture; oral terbinafine for 3-6 months if confirmed; check for diabetes.
red_flags: Painful nail with surrounding redness suggesting paronychia
In diabetic patients, any nail pathology warrants earlier review
Signs of spreading fungal skin infection

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهرين أحس بألم في وسط صدري زي ما حاجة بتضغط عليه، وبيزيد لما بحس بضغط أو قلق. وبيجيلي ضيق في النفس بدون سبب واضح.
urgency_reason: Chest symptoms always require cardiac workup before attributing to anxiety; organic causes must be excluded.
recommended_action: See GP for ECG, troponin, and chest X-ray; if cardiac workup normal, refer to psychiatrist for anxiety management.
red_flags: Chest pain with exertion
Radiation to arm or jaw
Syncope during episode

user_symptom_description: I've been having severe pain in my upper right abdomen for 2 hours after eating a fatty meal. The pain is sharp and radiates to my right shoulder. I feel nauseated and have vomited twice.
urgency_reason: Acute RUQ pain with shoulder radiation after fatty meal is cholecystitis requiring urgent evaluation for surgical intervention.
recommended_action: Go to ER today for abdominal ultrasound, LFTs, lipase, and CBC; IV antibiotics and surgical consultation may be needed.
red_flags: High fever above 39°C with rigors
Yellow eyes developing (Charcot's triad)
Altered consciousness with fever and jaundice

user_symptom_description: بنتي عمرها 5 سنين وبقالها أسبوعين كل يوم بتغيب عنها وبتحدق في الفضاء ثواني وبعدين ترجع لوحدها. المدرسة قالت بيحصللها في الفصل.
urgency_reason: Frequent absence seizures impair learning and safety; EEG and neurological evaluation are needed to confirm diagnosis and start ethosuximide.
recommended_action: See pediatric neurologist within one week; EEG (with hyperventilation provocation) is diagnostic; avoid driving or swimming unsupervised.
red_flags: Seizure evolving to generalized convulsion
Seizure lasting more than 30 seconds
Developmental regression

user_symptom_description: بنتي لاحظت عليا إني بقيت مش قادرة أتوازن صح وبتعثر وأنا بامشي من أسبوعين. وبقيت بتلعثم في كلامي أحياناً. ومحتاجة أمسك في الحيط لما بامشي. عندي 73 سنة.
urgency_reason: Progressive cerebellar signs with dysarthria in an elderly patient requires urgent MRI brain to exclude posterior fossa stroke or mass.
recommended_action: Go to ER urgently; MRI brain with posterior fossa views; assess for vascular risk factors; CT angiography; antiplatelet therapy if ischaemic; neurosurgery if posterior fossa mass.
red_flags: Sudden headache at onset suggesting haemorrhagic stroke
Worsening consciousness from brain stem compression
Bilateral limb signs suggesting cord involvement

user_symptom_description: I've been getting these really intense episodes where I suddenly can't breathe, my heart is pounding, I start shaking and sweating, and I feel like I'm about to die. They last about 10 minutes and come out of nowhere. Had 5 in the last month.
urgency_reason: Recurrent panic attacks require medical exclusion of phaeochromocytoma and cardiac arrhythmia before a psychiatric diagnosis is confirmed.
recommended_action: ECG and 24-hour Holter; check TSH, urinary catecholamines, and metanephrines; refer for CBT once organic causes excluded; SSRI if recurrent; breathing retraining.
red_flags: Hypertensive crisis during episode suggesting phaeochromocytoma
Documented SVT on ECG during episode
Agoraphobia developing causing social withdrawal

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين عندي ألم في حلقي شديد من جهة اليمين بس، وصعوبة كبيرة في البلع وصوتي اتغير وبحّ. حرارتي 39 ومتورم الحلق من جوا بشكل غريب.
urgency_reason: Peritonsillar abscess with uvular deviation and trismus requires urgent ENT drainage to prevent airway compromise.
recommended_action: Go to ER urgently; ENT assessment for uvular deviation and trismus; needle aspiration or incision and drainage; IV benzylpenicillin and metronidazole; analgesia and IV fluids.
red_flags: Inability to open mouth suggesting severe trismus
Drooling with inability to swallow secretions
Stridor suggesting laryngeal spread

user_symptom_description: I've had very high blood pressure readings at home — 190/115 on average. I also have a splitting headache at the back of my head and my vision seems blurry. I feel confused and not quite right.
urgency_reason: BP above 180/120 with neurological symptoms (confusion, visual change) indicates hypertensive emergency with active end-organ damage requiring immediate controlled IV reduction.
recommended_action: Call 999/911; IV labetalol or nicardipine with target of 25% BP reduction in first hour; fundoscopy for papilloedema; urine dipstick for protein; ECG; CT head to exclude haemorrhage.
red_flags: Focal neurological deficits suggesting hypertensive stroke
Pulmonary oedema with respiratory failure
Acute kidney injury with oliguria

user_symptom_description: ابني عنده 11 سنة وبقاله شهرين يشرب ميه كتير جداً وبيصحى بالليل يروح الحمام أكتر من أربع مرات. وبقى وزنه ناقص مع إنه بياكل كويس. وعيونه بقت ضبابية.
urgency_reason: Classic triad of polydipsia, polyuria, and weight loss in a child with blurred vision suggests new Type 1 DM requiring urgent blood glucose to exclude DKA.
recommended_action: Go to ER today; capillary blood glucose and urine ketones immediately; venous blood gas, HbA1c, and electrolytes; if DKA initiate protocol; insulin commencement; diabetes nurse education.
red_flags: Blood glucose above 20 mmol/L with vomiting suggesting DKA
Kussmaul breathing indicating severe acidosis
Altered consciousness from cerebral oedema

user_symptom_description: I've had increasingly bad pain in my lower right abdomen for about 18 hours. It started around my belly button and moved. I feel sick, have a temperature, and it really hurts to move. I've never had this before.
urgency_reason: Migratory RIF pain with fever and peritonism is acute appendicitis until proven otherwise — perforation risk increases significantly after 24 hours.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; Alvarado score; beta-hCG to exclude ectopic; CT abdomen if ultrasound inconclusive; IV antibiotics and surgical consultation for laparoscopic appendicectomy.
red_flags: Peritonism with guarding suggesting perforation
Fever above 39 with tachycardia indicating sepsis
Pelvic mass suggesting appendix abscess

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 69 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين بحس بألم في عضلاتي في كتفيا وورنقيا بشكل متناظر مع تيبس صباحي. ومش قادر أرفع إيديا فوق رأسي صح. وحرارتي بتطلع أحياناً.
urgency_reason: PMR in an elderly man requires prompt prednisolone therapy to relieve incapacitating pain and exclude associated giant cell arteritis.
recommended_action: ESR and CRP urgently; if very elevated and symptoms fit start prednisolone 15mg; ask specifically about headache or visual loss suggesting GCA; temporal artery biopsy if GCA suspected.
red_flags: New headache or scalp tenderness suggesting giant cell arteritis
Sudden visual loss requiring immediate high-dose steroid
Weight loss suggesting occult malignancy mimicking PMR

user_symptom_description: I've been struggling with my weight my whole life, but now I'm restricting my food severely — sometimes going days without eating, obsessing over calories. I've lost 15 kg in 3 months and my periods have stopped.
urgency_reason: Rapid significant weight loss with amenorrhoea in anorexia nervosa risks cardiac arrhythmia, refeeding syndrome, and bone loss requiring urgent medical and psychiatric assessment.
recommended_action: See eating disorder team urgently; BMI, ECG (QTc), electrolytes, LFTs, bone density scan; if BMI below 15 or haemodynamically compromised consider inpatient admission; supervised refeeding with thiamine.
red_flags: QTc prolongation on ECG from hypokalaemia
Haemodynamic instability with bradycardia and hypotension
BMI below 13 indicating life-threatening starvation

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام وعندي ألم في أسفل ظهري من اليمين بيوجعني جداً وبييجي على هيئة مغص ووبيتحرك لأسفل للبطن والفخذ. وبولي بقى فيه دم وبوجعني جداً لما بتبول.
urgency_reason: Ureteric colic with haematuria requires urgent imaging to confirm diagnosis, assess stone size, and exclude obstruction with infection requiring emergency decompression.
recommended_action: Go to ER; IV diclofenac and strong analgesia; CT KUB without contrast; check WBC, CRP, and creatinine; urine culture; urology for stenting if obstructed infected kidney.
red_flags: Fever with rigors suggesting infected obstructed kidney requiring emergency drainage
Anuria suggesting bilateral obstruction
Creatinine rising suggesting obstructive nephropathy

user_symptom_description: My 3-year-old daughter has had red, cracked lips and a very high temperature for 5 days. Her tongue looks bright red like a strawberry, her eyes are red without discharge, and her palms and feet are swollen and red. She's very miserable.
urgency_reason: Kawasaki disease with 4+ diagnostic criteria requires IVIG within 10 days of fever onset to prevent coronary artery aneurysms.
recommended_action: Admit immediately; echocardiogram to assess coronary arteries; IVIG 2g/kg single infusion; high-dose aspirin; cardiology involvement; repeat echo at 6 weeks.
red_flags: Coronary artery dilatation on echo
Hydrops of gallbladder on ultrasound
Shock or arrhythmia from myocarditis

user_symptom_description: I have a new mole on my back that my partner noticed last month. She says it's gotten bigger, it has an irregular border, different shades of brown and black in it, and it's about 8mm. I'm a nurse and I'm very worried.
urgency_reason: ABCDE criteria-positive changing mole requires urgent excision biopsy within 2 weeks as melanoma depth at diagnosis determines survival.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week wait dermatology referral; dermoscopy; wide local excision with 1-2mm margin for biopsy; sentinel lymph node biopsy if Breslow depth > 1mm; sun protection counselling.
red_flags: Palpable regional lymphadenopathy suggesting nodal spread
Satellite lesions
Ulceration of lesion indicating advanced disease

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين عندي صعوبة في البلع، أول ما حست بيها كانت مع الأكل الصلب، دلوقتي وصلت للسوايل. ووزني نزل كتير من غير ما أحاول. وعندي 71 سنة ومدخن.
urgency_reason: Progressive dysphagia from solids to liquids with weight loss in an elderly smoker is oesophageal cancer until proven otherwise and requires urgent endoscopy.
recommended_action: Urgent upper GI endoscopy with biopsy within 2 weeks; CT chest, abdomen, pelvis for staging; nutritional assessment and nasogastric feeding if severe dysphagia; MDT cancer pathway.
red_flags: Complete inability to swallow requiring emergency stenting
Aspiration pneumonia from dysphagia
Haematemesis suggesting tumour erosion into vessel

user_symptom_description: I've had swollen, painful lymph nodes in my neck and armpits for 3 weeks. I also soak the sheets with sweat every night, have lost 5 kg, and sometimes get fevers at night. I'm 24 and pretty scared.
urgency_reason: B-symptoms (night sweats, fever, weight loss) with multi-station lymphadenopathy in a young adult is lymphoma until excluded and requires urgent biopsy.
recommended_action: Urgent GP visit; FBC with differential, ESR, LDH, uric acid; Monospot and HIV test; CT chest, abdomen, pelvis; urgent haematology or oncology referral for excision node biopsy.
red_flags: Superior vena cava obstruction with facial swelling suggesting mediastinal mass
Rapidly enlarging nodes suggesting aggressive lymphoma
Bone marrow failure with pancytopenia

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع وعندي طفح جلدي على جنبي الأيمن بس، بدأ بحرقة ووجع وبعدين طلع بثور حمرا. الألم شديد جداً حتى لمس الهدوم بيوجع، والمنطقة حمرا ومتورمة.
urgency_reason: Shingles with severe pain requires antiviral treatment within 72 hours of rash onset to reduce postherpetic neuralgia risk.
recommended_action: Start aciclovir 800mg 5 times daily or valaciclovir if within 72 hours of rash onset; amitriptyline for neuropathic pain; wound care; avoid contact with immunocompromised or pregnant contacts.
red_flags: Rash involving the tip of nose suggesting ophthalmic zoster requiring urgent eye assessment
Facial palsy with ear vesicles indicating Ramsay Hunt syndrome
Disseminated zoster in immunocompromised patient

user_symptom_description: I've had a cough for about 4 months now that just won't go away. I'm bringing up blood-tinged sputum and I've lost weight without trying. I've smoked a pack a day for 20 years. I feel tired all the time.
urgency_reason: Haemoptysis with weight loss and prolonged cough in a heavy smoker is lung cancer until excluded and requires urgent chest imaging.
recommended_action: Urgent chest X-ray immediately; if mass or hilar lymphadenopathy refer on 2-week-wait pathway; CT chest with contrast; PET scan and bronchoscopy; sputum for AFB to exclude TB; smoking cessation.
red_flags: Haemoptysis volume above 200ml suggesting major bleed
Superior vena cava obstruction from mediastinal involvement
New neurological symptoms suggesting brain metastases

user_symptom_description: أنا بقالي 3 أشهر ووجعني في عظمة عانتي وبطني التحتاني، وبحس بثقل زي ما فيه حاجة بتنزل في فتحة الفرج. وبقى عندي صعوبة في التبول والتبرز.
urgency_reason: Symptomatic prolapse affecting bladder and bowel function requires gynaecological assessment for conservative or surgical management.
recommended_action: GP pelvic examination; pelvic floor physiotherapy referral; ring pessary fitting if suitable; urodynamics for urinary symptoms; gynaecology referral if surgical repair indicated.
red_flags: Irreducible prolapse with vascular compromise
Obstructive uropathy with rising creatinine
New post-menopausal bleeding suggesting endometrial pathology

user_symptom_description: I accidentally took too many of my antidepressants last night — I took the whole bottle. I've been feeling confused, my heart is racing, and I feel sweaty and shaky. I'm scared of what might happen to me.
urgency_reason: Antidepressant overdose presenting with agitation, tachycardia, and diaphoresis meets Hunter criteria for serotonin syndrome which can be fatal without immediate treatment.
recommended_action: Call 999/911 immediately; identify exact medication and dose taken; IV access; activated charcoal if within 1 hour and no altered consciousness; cyproheptadine for serotonin syndrome; cardiac monitoring; ICU if severe.
red_flags: Hyperthermia above 41 degrees
Clonus or hyperreflexia indicating severe neurotoxicity
QRS widening on ECG from TCA toxicity requiring sodium bicarbonate

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أشهر وعندي ألم في أسفل ظهري وبطني التحتاني بيزيد قبل الدورة وجواها وكمان ألم أثناء العلاقة الزوجية. وبقالي سنتين دورتي شديدة وطويلة.
urgency_reason: Endometriosis with dyspareunia and progressive dysmenorrhoea requires gynaecological assessment to prevent fertility-related complications.
recommended_action: Gynaecology referral; pelvic ultrasound; trial of combined OCP or progestogen; diagnostic laparoscopy if symptoms persist; fertility preservation discussion if relevant.
red_flags: Acute pelvic pain suggesting endometrioma rupture

Infertility concerns requiring early specialist input

user_symptom_description: I've been having terrible heartburn and vomiting for 2 days. I went out drinking heavily last night and this morning my vomit had streaks of bright red blood in it. The bleeding seems to have stopped now but I'm very worried.
urgency_reason: Haematemesis after forceful vomiting requires urgent endoscopy to confirm Mallory-Weiss and exclude actively bleeding peptic ulcer or varices.

red_flags: Haemodynamic instability requiring resuscitation
Ongoing fresh haematemesis
Signs of portal hypertension suggesting varices requiring Terlipressin

user_symptom_description: ابني عنده 7 سنين وبقاله أسبوع بيحكيلي إن جوا نص النهار بيحس بدوخة وضعف وأحياناً بيرتجف وبيعرق كتير ثم بيرجع طبيعي بعد ما ياكل. وعنده تاريخ عائلي بالسكر.
urgency_reason: Episodic hypoglycaemic symptoms in a child with diabetic family history require urgent glucose monitoring and evaluation for insulinoma or diabetic episodes.
recommended_action: Check blood glucose during an episode urgently; fasting glucose, insulin, C-peptide, and HbA1c; supervised fast test under paediatric supervision; refer to paediatric endocrinology.
red_flags: Blood glucose below 3 mmol/L confirmed during episode
Loss of consciousness from severe hypoglycaemia
Seizure triggered by hypoglycaemia

user_symptom_description: I've been very thirsty and going to the toilet constantly for 3 days. I feel sick to my stomach, my breath smells sweet and fruity, and I'm really confused. I was recently diagnosed with Type 1 diabetes but ran out of insulin.
urgency_reason: DKA with confusion and fruity breath in a patient who has run out of insulin is a life-threatening emergency requiring immediate IV insulin and fluid resuscitation.

red_flags: GCS below 13 from cerebral oedema
Potassium below 3.5 before insulin started
pH below 7.0 indicating severe acidosis

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر وعندي صداع في صدغي الأيمن بيوجعني لما بلمسه أو بمضغ. وعيني اليمين اتكسر نظرها فجأة مرتين لدقيقتين وبعدين رجع. وعندي 70 سنة.

user_symptom_description: I've been having really bad stomach cramps, bloody diarrhoea up to 10 times a day, and I've lost 7 kg over 2 months. The cramps come before I go to the toilet. I've also had some joint pain and my right eye is red.
urgency_reason: Bloody diarrhoea 10 times daily with extra-intestinal features and significant weight loss indicates severe IBD flare requiring urgent assessment and possible steroid therapy.

red_flags: Toxic megacolon with colonic dilation on X-ray

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وعندي ألم موجع في إيدي اليمين في المنطقة الرسغية ووجع ينزل في الأصابع ذراع سنما والبنصر والخنصر بيصحيني بالليل. وبيزيد لما بحرك إيدي.
urgency_reason: Ulnar nerve distribution symptoms are not immediately dangerous but require nerve conduction studies to assess severity and plan management.
recommended_action: Nerve conduction studies; elbow X-ray; avoid prolonged elbow flexion; padded elbow splint at night; occupational therapy; surgical decompression if conservative management fails.

user_symptom_description: My 6-year-old fell off the trampoline onto her outstretched arm. She's crying and won't move her left arm at all. The forearm looks a bit bent where it shouldn't be and she says the pain is 10 out of 10.
urgency_reason: Visible forearm deformity with severe pain in a child requires urgent X-ray and reduction under anaesthesia to prevent malunion and neurovascular injury.

Progressive finger oedema and pain suggesting compartment syndrome

user_symptom_description: I've been feeling extremely nauseous and vomiting almost every morning for 2 weeks. My last period was 10 weeks ago. I've also noticed my breasts are tender and I've been very tired.
urgency_reason: Persistent vomiting in early pregnancy risks dehydration, electrolyte disturbance, and Wernicke's encephalopathy if thiamine depleted.

red_flags: Urine ketones 3+ with dehydration requiring admission
Haematemesis from Mallory-Weiss tear
Jaundice from hyperemesis-related liver disease

user_symptom_description: بنتي لاحظت إني بقيت أتأخر كتير في كلامي ووجهي بيتقلص من غير إرادتي أحياناً وإيدي بتترعش لما تقعد ساكنة. وبقالي شهرين كده ووزني بيقل.
urgency_reason: Resting tremor with bradykinesia and masked facies in an elderly patient requires prompt neurological assessment and dopaminergic therapy to maintain function.
recommended_action: Urgent neurology referral; DaTscan if diagnosis uncertain; review medications for drug-induced causes; start co-beneldopa or co-careldopa; Parkinson's specialist nurse; physiotherapy and speech therapy.
red_flags: Rapidly progressive course suggesting Parkinson's Plus syndrome
Falls from postural instability requiring home assessment
Cognitive decline suggesting Lewy body dementia

user_symptom_description: I've had a sudden sharp pain in the right side of my chest for 2 hours that gets worse when I breathe in deeply. I'm also short of breath. I'm 20, tall and slim, and I've never had this before. There was no trauma.
urgency_reason: Spontaneous pneumothorax in a tall young male requires urgent chest X-ray to assess size and determine need for aspiration or chest drain.
recommended_action: Go to ER; urgent erect PA chest X-ray; O2 if hypoxic; needle aspiration if large or symptomatic; chest drain if failing aspiration; consider CT for blebs; discharge planning with follow-up CXR.
red_flags: Tracheal deviation and haemodynamic instability suggesting tension pneumothorax
SpO2 falling below 92%
Bilateral pneumothorax requiring bilateral chest drains

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام وعندي حكة شديدة في جسمي كله مع طفح حمر كبيرة ومتحركة ومتغيرة في الشكل والمكان. مش قادرة أنام من الحكة ومش شفت حاجة بالجهديلا أكلتيها زي دي.
urgency_reason: Acute urticaria requires assessment for anaphylaxis triggers and antihistamine treatment but must exclude urticarial vasculitis if lesions last over 24 hours.
recommended_action: Oral non-sedating antihistamine (cetirizine 10mg) and short course prednisolone; identify and avoid trigger; if persistent over 6 weeks refer for chronic urticaria workup including complement levels.
red_flags: Throat swelling or difficulty swallowing suggesting anaphylaxis
Wheezing or stridor
Hypotension requiring adrenaline injection

user_symptom_description: I keep waking up gasping in the middle of the night. My wife says I stop breathing for long periods and then snore very loudly. I'm exhausted during the day and nearly fell asleep while driving last week. I'm overweight.
urgency_reason: OSA with near-accident from daytime somnolence requires urgent sleep study and CPAP initiation — driving ban recommended until treated.
recommended_action: Urgent sleep study (polysomnography or home oximetry); BMI assessment; advise against driving pending treatment; CPAP titration; weight loss programme; DVLA/driving authority notification.
red_flags: Witnessed apnoea over 30 seconds
Cardiac arrhythmia detected during overnight monitoring
Severe hypoxaemia with SpO2 nadir below 80%

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع ابني عنده 10 سنين وبيشكي من ألم في خصيته اليمين فجأة وهو بيلعب. الألم شديد جداً والخصية بانت أعلى من الطبيعي ووجعها جداً لما بلمسها.
urgency_reason: Acute scrotal pain with elevated testicle is testicular torsion until proven otherwise — testicular salvage requires surgery within 4-6 hours of onset.
recommended_action: Take to ER immediately; scrotal ultrasound with Doppler but do NOT delay surgery for it if diagnosis likely; emergency surgical exploration and orchidopexy; bilateral fixation at same sitting.
red_flags: Duration over 6 hours drastically reducing salvage rate
Absent blood flow on Doppler confirming torsion
Contralateral testicular pain suggesting bilateral torsion

user_symptom_description: I noticed a lump in my right breast 3 weeks ago. It's hard and doesn't move much. I don't have pain but I can see the skin over it looks a bit puckered. I'm 42 years old.
urgency_reason: Hard fixed breast lump with skin puckering in a 42-year-old has high malignancy probability and requires urgent triple assessment.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week wait breast clinic referral; triple assessment — clinical examination, mammogram with ultrasound, and core needle biopsy; staging CT if malignancy confirmed; MDT discussion.
red_flags: Nipple discharge or inversion
Axillary lymphadenopathy suggesting nodal spread
Skin dimpling progressing to peau d'orange

user_symptom_description: عندي 66 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين مش قادر أمشي صح وعندي ألم موجع في ساقيا الاتنين لما بمشي أكتر من 100 متر وبييجي راحة لما بوقف. وعندي تاريخ بيبخ في السجاير.
urgency_reason: Intermittent claudication at 100m indicates significant PAD requiring ABPI measurement and cardiovascular risk management to prevent limb loss and MI.
recommended_action: ABPI measurement; duplex ultrasound of lower limb arteries; fasting lipids, HbA1c, ECG; antiplatelet therapy; statin; supervised exercise programme; vascular surgery referral if ABPI below 0.5.
red_flags: Rest pain indicating critical limb ischaemia
Non-healing ulcer or gangrene
Acute limb ischaemia with absent pulse and pallor

user_symptom_description: I've been having these really painful periods my whole life, but for the past year, I've also been having pain in my pelvis constantly — not just during my period. Sex is very painful and I'm really struggling to get pregnant.
urgency_reason: Endometriosis with infertility and severe constant pelvic pain requires laparoscopic staging and treatment to preserve fertility.
recommended_action: Urgent gynaecology referral for laparoscopy; pelvic MRI for deep infiltrating endometriosis; fertility specialist input; excision or ablation of endometriotic lesions; consider IVF discussion if tubes affected.
red_flags: Endometrioma rupture causing acute abdomen
Ureteric or bowel involvement from infiltrating endometriosis
Complete tubal occlusion on HSG

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 45 سنة وفجأة حسيت بدوار شديد جداً ووجعني دماغي وراسي وبدأت أتقيأ بشدة. الدوخة بالذمة أسوأ حاجة حسيت بيها في حياتي وما بتروحش.
urgency_reason: Sudden thunderclap headache described as worst ever with vertigo and vomiting requires immediate CT head to exclude subarachnoid haemorrhage.
recommended_action: Call 999/911 immediately; urgent non-contrast CT head; if negative LP at 12 hours; CT angiography; neurosurgical consultation; nimodipine; monitor in HDU.
red_flags: Meningism with neck stiffness
Deteriorating consciousness
Focal neurological signs suggesting haematoma

user_symptom_description: I have severe pain around my eye and on my forehead, and the skin around my eye and eyelid is covered in a blistery rash. My eye itself is red and the vision is slightly blurred. It started 2 days ago.
urgency_reason: Zoster ophthalmicus with ocular involvement risks corneal scarring, uveitis, and permanent visual loss requiring emergency ophthalmology assessment.
recommended_action: Emergency ophthalmology today; slit-lamp examination; aciclovir 800mg 5 times daily; topical aciclovir eye ointment; topical steroid if uveitis confirmed; artificial tears; pain management.
red_flags: Dendritic corneal ulcer on fluorescein staining
Elevated intraocular pressure from uveitis
Hutchinson's sign: tip of nose vesicle indicating nasociliary nerve involvement

user_symptom_description: ابني عنده 14 سنة وبقاله شهرين يشكي من ألم خلف ركبته اليمين، الألم بيزيد بعد الكرة وبيقوله الركبة بتعمل صوت أحياناً. وفيه انتفاخ صغير تحت الركبة.
urgency_reason: Osgood-Schlatter disease is a benign self-limiting apophysitis in adolescents managed with activity modification and physiotherapy.
recommended_action: Knee X-ray to confirm tibial tuberosity prominence; relative rest from jumping and sprinting; physiotherapy for quadriceps stretching; patellar tendon strap; NSAIDs for flares; reassure regarding self-limiting nature.
red_flags: Night pain or fever suggesting bone infection or tumour
Avulsion fracture on X-ray requiring immobilisation
Joint effusion suggesting intra-articular pathology

user_symptom_description: I was stung by a bee at a picnic an hour ago. Within minutes, my throat started swelling, I developed hives all over, my blood pressure dropped, and I felt faint. I'm better now but I'm at the hospital.
urgency_reason: Confirmed anaphylaxis with cardiovascular compromise requires emergency adrenaline, observation for biphasic reaction, and prescription of self-injectable adrenaline device.
recommended_action: IM adrenaline 0.5mg if not already given; IV fluids; IV chlorphenamine and hydrocortisone; observe minimum 6-12 hours for biphasic reaction; prescribe 2 epi-pens; allergy specialist referral; medic-alert bracelet.
red_flags: Biphasic reaction 4-12 hours later
Hypotension not responding to adrenaline
Respiratory arrest from laryngeal oedema

user_symptom_description: بقالي 6 أشهر وعندي عظمة من صدري بارزة جداً وبتكبر وبتوجع لما بضغط عليها. ووزني نزل ومش قادر آكل كويس. وعندي 73 سنة.
urgency_reason: Expanding painful sternal lesion with weight loss in an elderly man is malignant until proven otherwise and requires urgent imaging and biopsy.
recommended_action: Urgent chest X-ray and CT chest, abdomen, pelvis; serum protein electrophoresis; PSA and CEA; bone scan; haematology and oncology referral; core biopsy of lesion; pain management.
red_flags: Spinal cord compression from vertebral metastases
Pathological fracture through bony lesion
Hypercalcaemia causing confusion and constipation

user_symptom_description: I took some ecstasy at a festival last night. Now my temperature is extremely high, I'm confused and aggressive, my muscles are rigid, and I've been having seizures. My friends called an ambulance.
urgency_reason: MDMA hyperthermia above 40 degrees with seizures is life-threatening and requires immediate aggressive cooling and ICU admission.
recommended_action: Immediate ice packs and active cooling; IV benzodiazepines for seizures; dantrolene if temperature above 40.5; ICU admission; check CK, renal function for rhabdomyolysis; avoid haloperidol.
red_flags: Temperature above 41 degrees risking multi-organ failure
Rhabdomyolysis with myoglobinuria causing acute kidney injury
DIC from severe hyperthermia

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين لاحظت إن عيني اليمين إبتدت تجحظ وتبان أكبر من الشمال ووجعني وراها أحياناً. ولاحظت إن نظري بقى مزدوج في الاتجاهات الجانبية. وعندي غدة درقية.
urgency_reason: Proptosis with diplopia and pain in a patient with thyroid disease needs urgent MRI orbits to assess optic nerve compression threatening vision.
recommended_action: Urgent ophthalmology assessment; MRI orbits; visual acuity and colour vision testing; thyroid function and TSH receptor antibodies; high-dose IV methylprednisolone for active disease; radiotherapy or orbital decompression if sight-threatening.
red_flags: Decreasing visual acuity suggesting optic nerve compression
Corneal exposure and ulceration from proptosis
Complete inability to close eye from severe proptosis

user_symptom_description: I've been on blood pressure tablets called lisinopril for 3 months. Yesterday I woke up with my lips and tongue swollen. It's not itchy and there's no rash. It happened suddenly and my throat feels a bit full.
urgency_reason: ACE inhibitor angioedema causing throat fullness can rapidly progress to complete airway obstruction regardless of chronicity of medication use.
recommended_action: Call 999/911; stop lisinopril permanently; IM adrenaline if airway compromise; IV icatibant or C1-esterase inhibitor if available; antihistamines and steroids have limited efficacy in bradykinin-mediated angioedema; switch to ARB cautiously or alternative antihypertensive.
red_flags: Stridor from laryngeal involvement
Inability to swallow secretions
Rapid worsening despite initial treatment

user_symptom_description: بنتي عندها 5 سنين وبقاله أسبوعين مش بتبول صح، بتبول في هدومها أحياناً وبتمسك في بطنها وبتتألم لما بتتبول. وبحرارتها بسيط وبتقول مكانها واجعها.
urgency_reason: UTI in a 5-year-old girl with fever requires urine culture and treatment; recurrent UTIs in children need vesicoureteric reflux evaluation.
recommended_action: Clean-catch MSU for culture before antibiotics; oral trimethoprim or nitrofurantoin; renal ultrasound if first UTI with fever; MCUG if recurrent to exclude VUR; hydration and hygiene advice.
red_flags: High fever with flank tenderness suggesting pyelonephritis
Failure to respond to antibiotics in 48 hours
Third UTI in a year suggesting anatomical abnormality

user_symptom_description: I've been having severe cramping abdominal pain after eating, especially fatty meals. I've lost weight because I'm scared to eat. My stool has been pale and greasy and floats in the toilet. I have a history of alcohol use.
urgency_reason: Chronic pancreatitis with steatorrhoea causes significant malnutrition and requires enzyme replacement and pain management to prevent further deterioration.
recommended_action: CT pancreas; faecal elastase and faecal fat estimation; HbA1c for pancreatogenic diabetes; pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy; low-fat diet; strict alcohol cessation; fat-soluble vitamin supplementation.
red_flags: Acute-on-chronic pancreatitis with severe exacerbation
New-onset diabetes requiring insulin
Pancreatic mass on CT suggesting adenocarcinoma

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 74 سنة وبقاله شهرين عندي ألم موجع في صدري لما باتنفس بعمق أو لما بأكعد، وبقيت كمان أكعب فيها دم بعض المرات. ومكنتش مريضة قبل كده.
urgency_reason: Pleuritic chest pain with haemoptysis in an elderly woman suggests pulmonary embolism or lung malignancy both requiring urgent investigation.
recommended_action: Go to ER; D-dimer; CT pulmonary angiogram; Wells score; anticoagulation if high probability; if D-dimer elevated and CT positive start LMWH then DOAC; if normal CT check for lung mass.
red_flags: Haemodynamic instability suggesting massive PE requiring thrombolysis
SpO2 below 90%
Large haemoptysis above 100ml

user_symptom_description: I've had pain and swelling in my right ankle since I twisted it playing football yesterday. The pain is directly over the bone at the side of my ankle, not in the soft tissue. I can't put weight on it at all.
urgency_reason: Bony tenderness with inability to weight-bear meets Ottawa ankle rules criteria for X-ray and possible fracture requiring immobilisation.
recommended_action: Ankle and foot X-ray per Ottawa rules; if fracture confirmed apply below-knee cast or boot; weight-bearing as tolerated if stable fracture; orthopaedic review if displaced or unstable; physiotherapy on healing.
red_flags: Bimalleolar or trimalleolar fracture requiring surgical fixation
Talar shift indicating unstable ankle mortise
Skin compromise from fracture displacement

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر وعندي ألم ووجع في أسفل ظهري الموجع مع تيبس صباحي بيتحسن بالحركة مش بالراحة. بدأت وعمري 28 سنة. وبقالي أسبوعين عيني اليمين حمرا وبتوجعني.
urgency_reason: Inflammatory back pain in a young male with uveitis warrants urgent spondyloarthritis evaluation to start anti-inflammatory therapy before spinal fusion.
recommended_action: HLA-B27 testing; MRI sacroiliac joints; ESR, CRP, and full blood count; ophthalmology for uveitis; naproxen or indomethacin; TNF inhibitor if inadequate NSAID response; physiotherapy.
red_flags: Severe uveitis threatening vision without topical steroid treatment
Rapidly progressive spinal fusion limiting thoracic expansion
Vertebral fracture from rigid ankylosed spine after minor trauma

user_symptom_description: My 8-year-old woke up this morning and both his eyes are stuck together with yellow-green discharge. They're very red and swollen and it's spreading. He also has a runny nose and sore throat.
urgency_reason: Bacterial conjunctivitis in a healthy child with URTI is self-limiting but topical antibiotics speed recovery and reduce school transmission.
recommended_action: Topical chloramphenicol or fusidic acid eye drops; clean discharge with saline swabs; school exclusion until discharge clears; avoid sharing towels; reassess if no improvement in 5 days.
red_flags: Severe eyelid oedema with proptosis suggesting periorbital cellulitis requiring IV antibiotics
Visual impairment
Pain on eye movement suggesting orbital cellulitis

user_symptom_description: بقالي 6 أسابيع وعندي ألم في فتحة الشرج بيزيد كتير لما باتبرز وبيفضل ساعات بعد كده. وفيه دم أحمر فاتح بعد التبرز على الورق. وبحس بشيء بيخرج برا.
urgency_reason: Chronic anal fissure causes significant pain and bleeding but is not immediately dangerous and responds to topical treatment in most cases.
recommended_action: Topical GTN 0.4% or diltiazem 2% ointment twice daily; dietary fibre increase and stool softeners; sitz baths; Botox injection if chronic; surgical lateral internal sphincterotomy if refractory.
red_flags: Purulent discharge suggesting perianal abscess requiring drainage
Fistula in ano requiring surgical management
Dark rectal bleeding suggesting more proximal source needing colonoscopy

user_symptom_description: I've been confused and talking nonsense since this morning. My daughter says I was completely fine yesterday. I've been picking at my bedclothes and seeing things that aren't there. I'm 80 and in hospital for a hip replacement.
urgency_reason: Post-operative delirium in an 80-year-old is associated with significant morbidity and mortality and requires urgent identification and treatment of precipitating causes.
recommended_action: 4AT delirium screen; check electrolytes, glucose, FBC, CRP, urine culture, and drug chart for causative medications; reorientation; ensure adequate hydration; avoid restraints; low-dose haloperidol only if risk to self or others; family involvement.
red_flags: Severe agitation risking fall and fracture
Respiratory compromise from aspiration
Sepsis as an underlying trigger

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 27 سنة وبقالي شهرين وعندي وجع موجع في كتفي وإيدي من فوق، الوجع بييجي من العنق وبيتمد للإيد مع تنميل في أصابع الإبهام والسبابة والوسطى. وبيزيد لما بأنظر لفوق.
urgency_reason: C6 radiculopathy with dermatomal numbness requires cervical MRI and physiotherapy with neurosurgical referral if motor deficit appears.
recommended_action: MRI cervical spine; NSAIDs and physiotherapy; cervical collar for flares; nerve root block if pain uncontrolled; neurosurgery referral for anterior cervical discectomy if motor weakness or intractable pain.
red_flags: Bilateral arm symptoms suggesting central cord involvement
Lower limb weakness suggesting myelopathy
Bladder dysfunction from cord compression

user_symptom_description: I've had a really bad stomach bug — vomiting and severe diarrhoea for 4 days. I've barely been able to keep anything down. I haven't urinated in over 12 hours and my mouth is very dry. I feel dizzy when I stand up.
urgency_reason: Oliguria for 12 hours with orthostatic hypotension and inability to maintain oral intake indicates severe dehydration with pre-renal acute kidney injury requiring IV fluids.
recommended_action: Go to ER; IV 0.9% saline 1 litre over 1 hour; check electrolytes, creatinine, and urine output hourly; antiemetic IV; exclude surgical cause for vomiting; monitor and recheck renal function.
red_flags: Creatinine doubling baseline suggesting AKI stage 2 requiring specialist input
Hypokalaemia from diarrhoea causing cardiac arrhythmia
Seizure from hyponatraemia

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين مش قادرة أتبرز خالص ومعنديش أي غاز. وبطني انتفخ بشكل مزعج وبتألم كتير. ومرة اتقيأت وكان لونه أخضر مر. وعندي 76 سنة وعندي تاريخ جراحي في البطن.
urgency_reason: Complete intestinal obstruction with bilious vomiting and prior abdominal surgery is a surgical emergency with risk of strangulation and perforation.
recommended_action: Call ambulance; NG tube decompression; IV fluids; abdominal X-ray and CT abdomen; nil by mouth; surgical consult for emergency laparotomy if signs of strangulation; conservative management if partial obstruction.
red_flags: Peritoneal signs suggesting strangulation or perforation
Fever with tachycardia indicating bowel necrosis
Closed loop obstruction on CT requiring immediate surgery

user_symptom_description: I've had very high temperatures, shaking chills, and severe sweating that cycles every other day for the past week. I returned from a trip to sub-Saharan Africa 2 weeks ago and didn't take malaria prophylaxis.
urgency_reason: Returning traveller with cyclical fevers from a malaria-endemic region must have urgent thick and thin blood films — falciparum malaria can be fatal within 24 hours.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; urgent thick and thin blood films and rapid malaria antigen test; FBC for anaemia, renal function, glucose, and LFTs; admit if P. falciparum confirmed; artemether-lumefantrine or IV artesunate if severe.
red_flags: Cerebral malaria with altered consciousness
Blood glucose below 4 from parasite consumption of glucose
Hyperparasitaemia above 2% requiring exchange transfusion consideration

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام عندي ألم موجع حول السرة وبعدين انتقل لتحت على اليمين. وبقى معايا غثيان وحرارتي 38.5. الألم بيزيد لما بتحرك. عمري 32 وعندي بنتين صغيرين.
urgency_reason: Migratory RIF pain with fever and guarding in a 32-year-old woman needs urgent surgical assessment as delayed treatment risks perforation and peritonitis.
recommended_action: Go to ER; beta-hCG to exclude ectopic; Alvarado score; CT or ultrasound; IV antibiotics and surgical consult; laparoscopic appendicectomy; ovarian pathology assessed intraoperatively.
red_flags: Generalised peritonism suggesting perforation
Septic shock requiring resuscitation
Pelvic mass suggesting appendix abscess

user_symptom_description: I was working in a confined space with a running generator and I suddenly felt dizzy, had a terrible headache, and felt very confused and sick. My co-workers found me collapsed. I feel very weak now.
urgency_reason: Carbon monoxide poisoning with syncope requires immediate 100% high-flow oxygen and may require hyperbaric oxygen to prevent delayed neurological sequelae.
recommended_action: Remove from source; 100% O2 via non-rebreather mask; carboxyhaemoglobin level on ABG (note: pulse oximetry is falsely normal); ECG; consider hyperbaric oxygen if COHb above 25% or cardiac/neurological involvement.
red_flags: Carboxyhaemoglobin above 40% requiring hyperbaric oxygen urgently
Cardiac arrhythmia from CO-induced myocardial toxicity
Coma or seizure indicating severe neurological toxicity

user_symptom_description: بنتي عندها سنة ونص ومن أمبارح عندها إسهال مائي كتير جداً وبتتقيأ كمان. عملت حفاضات كتير جداً وبقت عيونها غائرة وبتعيط من غير دموع وفمها جاف جداً.
urgency_reason: Severe dehydration with sunken eyes, absent tears, and dry mouth in a toddler is a medical emergency requiring IV rehydration.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; IV or IO access; 20ml/kg 0.9% saline bolus; reassess; NG ORS if IV access difficult; check glucose, electrolytes, and bicarbonate; oral rehydration once stable; stool culture.
red_flags: Shock with mottled skin and prolonged capillary refill
Blood in stool suggesting bacterial or intussusception
Altered consciousness from severe dehydration and hypernatraemia

user_symptom_description: I've been having numbness and tingling in my hands and feet for 3 months. It started in my toes and seems to be slowly moving up. I also feel very unsteady when I walk, especially in the dark. I'm vegetarian and don't supplement.
urgency_reason: Progressive ascending neuropathy with posterior column signs in a non-supplemented vegetarian suggests B12 deficiency causing irreversible cord damage without prompt treatment.
recommended_action: Urgent B12, folate, and MMA levels; FBC for megaloblastic anaemia; MRI spine if myelopathy suspected; IM hydroxocobalamin 1mg daily for 7 days then maintenance; intrinsic factor antibodies; neurological review.
red_flags: Worsening motor weakness suggesting cord involvement
Brisk reflexes with extensor plantars indicating subacute combined degeneration
Confusion or cognitive decline from cerebral B12 deficiency

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع وعندي فتحة صغيرة في جلدي على قدمي الأيمن ما بتتآكلش وبتتكبر من غير ما تتألم. عندي سكر من 15 سنة ومش بتحس بأصابع قدمي.
urgency_reason: A painless enlarging foot ulcer in a diabetic with neuropathy risks osteomyelitis, gangrene, and amputation without prompt multidisciplinary wound care.
recommended_action: Urgent diabetic foot team referral; wound swab and X-ray foot for osteomyelitis; ABPI for arterial component; HbA1c optimisation; offloading with total contact cast; IV antibiotics if infected; vascular surgery if ischaemia.
red_flags: Spreading cellulitis with systemic sepsis
Bone visible in ulcer base confirming osteomyelitis
Crepitus from gas gangrene requiring emergency surgery

user_symptom_description: I've been abusing cocaine for the past year. Last night I took a larger than usual amount and now I have crushing chest pain, my heart is racing incredibly fast, and I'm very sweaty and anxious. I'm 25.
urgency_reason: Cocaine-induced MI can occur at any age — coronary spasm, thrombosis, and accelerated atherosclerosis are all mechanisms requiring emergency cardiac intervention.
recommended_action: Call 999/911; 12-lead ECG; troponin; avoid beta-blockers (risk of unopposed alpha vasoconstriction); use benzodiazepines for agitation; GTN for spasm; aspirin; urgent cath lab assessment.
red_flags: ST elevation confirming STEMI requiring PCI
BP above 200 systolic suggesting aortic dissection
Seizure from cocaine toxicity

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهرين وعندي طفح جلدي على خديا وأنفي أحمر ودائم مش بيروح مع أوردة صغيرة حمرا ظاهرة، وبيزيد لما باحرج أو أشرب قهوة أو الجو سخن. وعيني بتحسساني أحياناً.
urgency_reason: Rosacea is a chronic condition requiring trigger avoidance and topical treatment but is not immediately dangerous.
recommended_action: Topical metronidazole or azelaic acid; oral doxycycline for inflammatory papules; avoid triggers including alcohol, spicy food, heat, and sun; sunscreen SPF50; ophthalmology referral if ocular rosacea with keratitis.
red_flags: Ocular involvement with keratitis threatening vision
Rhinophyma requiring surgical intervention
Severe phymatous changes causing nasal obstruction

user_symptom_description: My 12-year-old has had severe joint pain and swelling that came on 3 weeks after a throat infection that wasn't treated. His knees and elbows are affected and swollen, and now he has some chest pain too. He has a fever.
urgency_reason: Acute rheumatic fever with carditis risk requires urgent echocardiogram as mitral valve damage is a major cause of preventable heart disease worldwide.
recommended_action: Admit urgently; ECG and echocardiogram; ASO titre, anti-DNase B, throat swab; IV benzylpenicillin then oral phenoxymethylpenicillin; aspirin for arthritis; monitor for carditis; secondary prophylaxis with monthly IM penicillin.
red_flags: Cardiac murmur suggesting mitral regurgitation from carditis
Heart failure symptoms from acute valvular dysfunction
Sydenham's chorea indicating neurological involvement

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع ووجعني في أكتر من مكان في جسمي — عضلاتي وعظامي ومفاصلي — من غير إصابة. مع إرهاق شديد وصعوبة في النوم. وبلمس بسيط على جلدي بيوجعني. وعندي 35 سنة.
urgency_reason: Fibromyalgia is not immediately dangerous but requires diagnosis by exclusion and multimodal management to improve function and quality of life.
recommended_action: Exclude inflammatory arthritis, lupus, and thyroid disease with blood tests; FIQ questionnaire; aerobic exercise programme; amitriptyline or duloxetine; CBT for pain management; sleep hygiene; patient education.
red_flags: Elevated inflammatory markers suggesting an alternative diagnosis
Progressive muscle weakness suggesting inflammatory myopathy
Autonomic dysfunction suggesting small fibre neuropathy

user_symptom_description: I've had a gradually worsening headache for 3 weeks, worse in the morning and when I bend forward. I've had two episodes of vomiting without nausea. My vision is occasionally blurred and I've started noticing double vision.
urgency_reason: Papilloedema features with progressive morning headache, vomiting, and visual disturbance indicate raised intracranial pressure requiring immediate neuroimaging.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; urgent CT head with contrast then MRI brain; fundoscopy for papilloedema; dexamethasone if mass lesion with oedema; neurosurgery referral; avoid LP until raised ICP excluded.
red_flags: Rapidly deepening confusion
Unequal pupils from uncal herniation
New focal neurological weakness

user_symptom_description: بقالي يومين وأنا حاسس إن قلبي بيدق بسرعة جداً وبيتخطى ضربات، وبيجيلي دوخة فجأة وبحس إني هاخد على وشي. عندي 68 سنة وعندي ضغط.
urgency_reason: New-onset irregular palpitations with near-syncope in an elderly hypertensive patient requires urgent ECG to rule out AF with rapid ventricular response.
recommended_action: Go to urgent care today for 12-lead ECG and troponin; hold antihypertensives until evaluated; rate control with beta-blocker if AF confirmed; anticoagulation assessment.
red_flags: Loss of consciousness
Chest pain accompanying palpitations
Heart rate above 150 bpm

user_symptom_description: I've had a pounding headache behind my eyes for 3 days now, my neck feels really stiff and I can't look at bright lights without it hurting badly. I also had a fever this morning of 38.9°C.
urgency_reason: Classic meningism triad of headache, neck stiffness, and photophobia with fever demands immediate emergency assessment and empirical antibiotics before LP.
recommended_action: Call 999/911 immediately; IV ceftriaxone and dexamethasone without delay; blood cultures before antibiotics if possible; urgent CT then LP; isolate for meningococcal precautions.
red_flags: Non-blanching petechial rash indicating meningococcaemia
Declining consciousness or seizure
Cushing's triad suggesting raised ICP

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع وعندي ألم في بطني من الشمال تحت، زي ما حاجة بتتقرص فيا، وبيجيلي إسهال وبعدين إمساك بالتناوب وأنا مش عارف أعدي يوم من غير ما بطني يعملي مشاكل.
urgency_reason: Alternating bowel habit with cramping pain in an adult is consistent with IBS but colorectal cancer must be excluded with appropriate investigation.
recommended_action: Stool calprotectin, FBC, CRP, and coeliac serology; colonoscopy if red flags present; low-FODMAP diet trial; antispasmodics for cramping; reassess in 6 weeks.
red_flags: Rectal bleeding or mucus in stool
Unintentional weight loss over 3kg
Nocturnal symptoms waking from sleep

user_symptom_description: My daughter is 4 years old and woke up in the middle of the night making a barking sound when she coughs and a weird noise when she breathes in. She has a mild fever and her voice sounds hoarse.
urgency_reason: Barking cough with stridor in a young child is classic croup requiring assessment for airway compromise and steroid treatment.
recommended_action: Take to A&E or urgent care; single dose oral dexamethasone 0.15mg/kg; nebulised adrenaline if severe stridor at rest; humidified air; monitor oxygen saturation; reassure parents.
red_flags: Drooling or difficulty swallowing suggesting epiglottitis
Cyanosis or severe respiratory distress
Stridor at rest not improving with steroids

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 74 سنة وبقالي شهر ولما بمشي بعيد شوية بيجيلي ألم شديد في ساقي الشمال وبيضطرني أوقف، لما بوقف بيعدي. بس بقى الوجع أقرب وأقرب.
urgency_reason: Progressive claudication with decreasing walking distance in an elderly patient indicates worsening limb ischaemia requiring vascular assessment.
recommended_action: ABPI measurement urgently; duplex ultrasound of lower limb arteries; antiplatelet therapy and statin; cardiovascular risk factor optimisation; vascular surgery referral if ABPI below 0.5.
red_flags: Rest pain indicating critical ischaemia
Non-healing ulcer or gangrene on foot
Sudden cold pale pulseless leg from acute limb ischaemia

user_symptom_description: I've been having really bad heartburn almost every day for 3 months. It gets worse when I lie down at night and after eating. Sometimes I can taste acid in the back of my throat and my voice sounds rough in the mornings.
urgency_reason: Chronic daily reflux symptoms are consistent with GERD and require management to prevent progression to Barrett's oesophagus.
recommended_action: PPI trial for 8 weeks (omeprazole 20mg twice daily); lifestyle modification including weight loss, elevate bed head, avoid triggers; upper endoscopy if no improvement or alarm symptoms; H. pylori test.
red_flags: Dysphagia or odynophagia suggesting stricture or malignancy
Unintentional weight loss
Vomiting blood or black tarry stools

user_symptom_description: بقالي يومين وعندي ألم شديد في بطني من فوق السرة وبيتمد للظهر، وبتقيأ كتير وما قدرتش آكل حاجة. كنت رايحة حفلة وشربت كتير قبلها.
urgency_reason: Epigastric pain radiating to back with vomiting following alcohol excess is acute pancreatitis until proven otherwise requiring hospital admission.
recommended_action: Go to A&E immediately; serum amylase and lipase, LFTs, FBC, CRP; IV fluids and analgesia; nil by mouth; CT abdomen if severe or deteriorating; Glasgow/Ranson scoring for severity.
red_flags: Grey-Turner or Cullen's sign indicating haemorrhagic pancreatitis
Dropping haematocrit or haemodynamic instability
Fever above 39°C suggesting infected necrosis

user_symptom_description: I noticed my hands shake when I try to hold a cup of tea or write something. It started maybe 6 months ago and it's getting worse. My handwriting is getting really small and I feel like my movements are slower than before.
urgency_reason: Progressive tremor with bradykinesia and micrographia in an adult requires neurological assessment to confirm Parkinson's and initiate dopaminergic therapy.
recommended_action: Urgent neurology referral; review medications for causative drugs; DaTscan if diagnosis uncertain; levodopa trial if Parkinson's confirmed; physiotherapy and occupational therapy assessment; driving assessment.
red_flags: Rapid progression over weeks suggesting Parkinson's plus syndrome
Falls from postural instability early in disease
Cognitive decline or hallucinations

user_symptom_description: ابني بيقول إني بقيت بنسى حاجات كتير أوي — زي ما نسيت أسماء جيراني اللي عارفهم من زمان، وبعدين مرة لاقيت نفسي في الشارع ومش عارف أنا فين. عندي 77 سنة.
urgency_reason: Progressive episodic memory loss with disorientation in a 77-year-old requires dementia workup to identify reversible causes and initiate care planning.
recommended_action: MMSE and MoCA assessment; MRI brain; thyroid function, B12, folate, syphilis serology to exclude reversible causes; memory clinic referral; driving cessation discussion; carer support and safeguarding assessment.
red_flags: Sudden step-wise deterioration suggesting vascular events
Visual hallucinations suggesting Lewy body dementia
Urinary incontinence and gait disturbance suggesting NPH

user_symptom_description: I was playing football and suddenly felt a pop in my knee, it swelled up massively within an hour and I can't straighten it properly. The pain is really bad and I can't put any weight on it.
urgency_reason: Acute haemarthrosis with pop and instability following sports injury indicates ACL rupture requiring orthopaedic assessment and MRI.
recommended_action: RICE protocol immediately; X-ray to exclude fracture; MRI knee within 1 week; orthopaedic referral for surgical versus conservative management; crutches and knee immobiliser; analgesia.
red_flags: Neurovascular compromise with cold or pale foot
Associated tibial plateau fracture on X-ray
Locked knee suggesting bucket-handle meniscal tear

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وعندي كحة جافة مستمرة مع ضيق في التنفس، وبحس بتعب شديد وعندي حمى خفيفة. شغلي في مستشفى وحد من زملايا اتعافى من كورونا.
urgency_reason: Healthcare worker with two weeks of progressive respiratory symptoms and fever requires urgent assessment for pneumonia with oxygen saturation monitoring.
recommended_action: PCR and antigen test; CXR and CT chest; SpO2 monitoring — admit if below 94%; FBC, CRP, D-dimer, ferritin; infection control precautions; anticoagulation if PE suspected; dexamethasone if requiring oxygen.
red_flags: SpO2 dropping below 92% at rest
Respiratory rate above 30 per minute
Confusion or inability to complete sentences

user_symptom_description: My 8-year-old has been complaining about pain when he pees for two days. He's going to the toilet very often but only a little comes out each time. He had a slight fever last night and said his tummy hurts.
urgency_reason: Dysuria with frequency and fever in a school-age boy requires urine culture to confirm UTI and assess for upper tract involvement.
recommended_action: Clean catch MSU for culture and sensitivity before antibiotics; oral trimethoprim or nitrofurantoin for 3-7 days pending culture; reassess if fever persists beyond 48 hours; renal ultrasound if recurrent UTI; adequate hydration.
red_flags: High fever with rigors suggesting pyelonephritis
Vomiting preventing oral antibiotics
Known renal abnormality or previous pyelonephritis

user_symptom_description: وقعت من السلم امبارح وأنا بنزل، وعندي ألم شديد في ورك أيميني ومش قادرة أقوم ولا أتحرك خالص. رجلي الأيمن بانت أقصر من الشمال. عندي 80 سنة.
urgency_reason: Shortened externally rotated leg with inability to weight-bear after fall in an 80-year-old is neck of femur fracture requiring emergency surgical repair.
recommended_action: Call 999 immediately; immobilise and pain relief en route; AP pelvis and lateral hip X-ray; IV access and analgesia; orthogeriatric assessment; surgical fixation or hemiarthroplasty within 36 hours; VTE prophylaxis.
red_flags: Haemodynamic instability from associated pelvic fracture
Neurovascular injury to the limb
Delirium from pain or fat embolism

user_symptom_description: I've been getting these episodes where I suddenly smell something burning that isn't there, then my right hand starts twitching on its own and I can't stop it for about a minute. It's happened 4 times this week and I'm fully aware during it.
urgency_reason: New-onset focal seizures with structural aura require urgent MRI to exclude intracranial lesion before antiepileptic therapy.
recommended_action: Urgent MRI brain with contrast; EEG; refrain from driving immediately; blood glucose, electrolytes, FBC; neurology referral within days; levetiracetam or lamotrigine after imaging review; DVLA notification.
red_flags: Secondary generalisation to tonic-clonic seizure
Todd's paresis lasting hours after episode
Rapidly increasing seizure frequency

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 22 سنة وبقالي 3 أسابيع مش قادرة أنام صح، وبفكر في مشاكلي طول الليل وقلبي بيدق بسرعة من غير سبب. بحس بضيق دايماً وزهقت من كل حاجة.
urgency_reason: Chronic anxiety with insomnia and somatic symptoms warrants psychological assessment and thyroid function testing to exclude organic cause.
recommended_action: PHQ-9 and GAD-7 screening; TFTs to exclude hyperthyroidism; CBT referral as first line; SSRI such as sertraline if CBT unavailable or insufficient; sleep hygiene advice; reassess in 4-6 weeks.
red_flags: Suicidal ideation or self-harm thoughts
Rapid weight loss suggesting hyperthyroidism
Panic attacks causing inability to leave home

user_symptom_description: My skin has been breaking out in red itchy raised patches that come and go all over my body. They last a few hours then disappear and show up somewhere else. This has been happening daily for 2 weeks and antihistamines barely help.
urgency_reason: Daily urticaria for over 6 weeks is chronic spontaneous urticaria requiring second-generation antihistamine optimisation and investigation for underlying triggers.
recommended_action: High-dose non-sedating antihistamine (cetirizine 20mg daily); FBC, ESR, TFTs, ANA to exclude systemic cause; food and drug diary; omalizumab referral if antihistamine-resistant; skin biopsy if vasculitis suspected.
red_flags: Associated angioedema of lips or throat
Systemic symptoms suggesting vasculitis
Anaphylaxis with urticaria requiring EpiPen

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وعندي ضيق في التنفس حتى وأنا قاعد، وبحس بتقل في صدري وقدمي انتفخوا جداً. بنام على 3 مخدات عشان أقدر أتنفس. عندي قصور قلب من 5 سنين.
urgency_reason: Orthopnoea with rest dyspnoea and bilateral oedema in a known heart failure patient indicates acute decompensation requiring emergency diuresis.
recommended_action: Call 999 immediately; sit upright; IV furosemide 40-80mg; SpO2 monitoring and oxygen if below 94%; BNP, troponin, CXR, ECG; echocardiogram; review and optimise HF medications; daily weights post-discharge.
red_flags: SpO2 below 90% despite oxygen
Cardiogenic shock with hypotension
New S3 gallop or worsening murmur

user_symptom_description: I've had a painful red eye for 3 days. It started watering a lot then the white part turned really red and I have this yellow-green sticky discharge, especially in the mornings when my eyelids are almost glued shut.
urgency_reason: Mucopurulent discharge with crusting is typical of bacterial conjunctivitis and usually self-limiting but topical antibiotic shortens course.
recommended_action: Topical chloramphenicol 0.5% drops every 2 hours for 48 hours then QID for 5 days; saline eye wash to remove discharge; swab if chlamydia suspected; contact lens wearer needs urgent ophthalmology review; avoid sharing towels.
red_flags: Severe eye pain with halos suggesting acute glaucoma
Corneal opacity or reduced visual acuity
Failure to improve after 5 days of topical antibiotics

user_symptom_description: ابنتي عندها 9 سنين وبقالها 4 أيام وعندها ألم في بطنها من الجهة اليمين السفلانية وحمى وبتتقيأ. وبتقول إن الألم اشتد من لما بدأ.
urgency_reason: Progressive RIF pain with fever and vomiting over 4 days in a child is appendicitis until proven otherwise with high perforation risk.
recommended_action: Go to A&E immediately; FBC, CRP, urinalysis; ultrasound abdomen as first-line imaging; IV access and analgesia; NBM for theatre; surgical consult urgently; IV antibiotics if perforation suspected; laparoscopic appendicectomy.
red_flags: Generalised peritonism suggesting perforation
Rigidity and guarding across whole abdomen
Septic shock from perforated appendix

user_symptom_description: I've been having these episodes of extreme sadness for the past 2 months. I used to love going out and playing guitar but I don't feel anything anymore. I sleep 12 hours and still feel exhausted. I've had some thoughts of not wanting to be here anymore.
urgency_reason: Passive suicidal ideation with severe depressive symptoms requires same-day psychiatric assessment to ensure safety and initiate treatment.
recommended_action: Same-day mental health assessment; PHQ-9 scoring; safety plan and crisis number provided; SSRI initiation (sertraline 50mg); urgent psychotherapy referral; TFTs and substance screen; involve supportive family member; crisis team follow-up.
red_flags: Active suicidal plan or intent
Self-harm behaviour
Psychotic features such as nihilistic delusions

user_symptom_description: بقالي يومين وأنا مش قادر أتنفس صح، وبحس بصفير في صدري وبخرج صوت لما بزفر. عندي ربو بس ما استجبتش للبخاخ اللي عندي. بحس إن التنفس بيتعب جداً.
urgency_reason: Bronchodilator-unresponsive wheeze with severe dyspnoea in an asthmatic is acute severe or life-threatening asthma requiring emergency treatment.
recommended_action: Call 999 immediately; high-flow oxygen; back-to-back salbutamol nebulisers; ipratropium nebuliser; IV hydrocortisone 200mg; PEFR measurement; ICU alert if silent chest or SpO2 below 92%; IV magnesium sulphate 1.2-2g over 20 min.
red_flags: Silent chest indicating complete airflow obstruction
Cyanosis or SpO2 below 92%
Exhaustion or altered consciousness requiring intubation

user_symptom_description: I'm 70 years old and a smoker for 40 years. For the past 6 months I've had a cough that produces a lot of green-yellow phlegm and I get short of breath just walking to the kitchen. Recently I had a chest infection that needed antibiotics.
urgency_reason: Long smoking history with progressive exertional dyspnoea and recurrent infections requires spirometry to confirm COPD and staging.
recommended_action: Post-bronchodilator spirometry; CXR and CT chest to exclude malignancy; sputum culture; LABA/LAMA inhaler; GOLD staging; pulmonary rehabilitation; annual influenza and pneumococcal vaccination; smoking cessation support; consider home oxygen if severe.
red_flags: Haemoptysis suggesting lung cancer or bronchiectasis
Unintentional weight loss over 5%
Acute hypercapnia requiring NIV

user_symptom_description: كنت في المطار وبعد رحلة طويلة من 12 ساعة حسيت بألم مفاجئ في صدري من الجهة الشمال وضيق شديد في التنفس. مش بتوجعني لما بضغط على صدري من الخارج.
urgency_reason: Sudden pleuritic chest pain and dyspnoea following prolonged immobilisation on a long-haul flight is pulmonary embolism until proven otherwise.
recommended_action: Call 999 immediately; Wells PE score calculation; D-dimer if low probability, CT pulmonary angiography if high; SpO2 and ECG; IV access; LMWH or DOAC anticoagulation if high suspicion; thrombolysis if haemodynamically unstable.
red_flags: Haemodynamic instability or cardiogenic shock
Syncope or pre-syncope
SpO2 below 90% despite high-flow oxygen

user_symptom_description: I've been having pain in my lower back that shoots down my right leg all the way to my foot. My foot feels numb and tingly. It gets much worse when I sit for a long time or when I sneeze.
urgency_reason: Dermatomal leg pain with paraesthesia and positive Valsalva features indicates disc herniation with nerve root compression requiring conservative management.
recommended_action: MRI lumbar spine; NSAIDs and muscle relaxants; physiotherapy with McKenzie exercises; epidural steroid injection if no improvement at 6 weeks; surgical microdiscectomy if motor deficit or conservative management fails at 3 months.
red_flags: Bilateral leg weakness or saddle anaesthesia suggesting cauda equina
Bladder or bowel dysfunction
Rapidly progressive motor deficit requiring emergency decompression

user_symptom_description: ابني عنده 3 سنين وحرارته اتعلت فجأة لـ 40 درجة وبعد كده بدأ يرتجف وجسمه كله بيتشنج لمدة دقيقتين تقريباً. بعدها انتهى وبقى نايم. ده أول مرة بيحصل.
urgency_reason: First febrile convulsion in a toddler requires emergency assessment to exclude meningitis and educate parents on recurrence management.
recommended_action: Take to A&E immediately; assess for meningism and perform LP if any doubt; blood glucose; paracetamol for fever; observe for minimum 4 hours; if simple febrile seizure reassure and educate parents; buccal midazolam prescription for recurrence.
red_flags: Seizure lasting more than 5 minutes indicating status epilepticus
Neck stiffness or bulging fontanelle
Failure to return to baseline within 30 minutes

user_symptom_description: I woke up this morning and the right side of my face feels numb. When I looked in the mirror I noticed my smile is crooked and I can't close my right eye properly. I have no weakness in my arms or legs.
urgency_reason: Acute lower motor neuron facial palsy requires prompt steroid treatment within 72 hours for best recovery and urgent assessment to exclude central cause.
recommended_action: Prednisolone 50mg daily for 10 days within 72 hours of onset; eye lubricants and taped eye closure at night; acyclovir if vesicles present suggesting Ramsay Hunt; MRI if central cause suspected; ENT referral if parotid mass palpable.
red_flags: Forehead sparing suggesting upper motor neuron stroke
Vesicles in ear canal confirming Ramsay Hunt
Bilateral facial palsy suggesting Guillain-Barré or sarcoidosis

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر وعندي ألم شديد في مفاصل إيدي الصغيرة وركبتي، الألم بيكون أشد الصبح وبيحتاج ساعة أو أكتر عشان يخف. إيدي بانت منتفخة وأحمر عليها.
urgency_reason: Symmetrical small joint arthritis with prolonged morning stiffness requires urgent rheumatology review to start DMARDs within 3 months and prevent joint destruction.
recommended_action: Anti-CCP, RF, CRP, ESR, FBC, LFTs; X-rays hands and feet; ultrasound for synovitis; methotrexate initiation after TB and hepatitis screen; bridge with prednisolone; folic acid supplementation; occupational therapy referral.
red_flags: Atlanto-axial instability causing neck pain with cervical myelopathy
Rapidly progressive joint destruction on imaging
Extra-articular manifestations including vasculitis or pericarditis

user_symptom_description: I had unprotected sex about 3 weeks ago and now I have a painful blister-like sore on my genitals and my groin lymph nodes are really swollen and tender. I also have a mild fever and feel generally unwell.
urgency_reason: Painful genital ulceration with inguinal lymphadenopathy and systemic symptoms following unprotected sex requires STI screen and antiviral treatment.
recommended_action: Swab ulcer for HSV PCR; full STI screen including syphilis serology, HIV, chlamydia, gonorrhoea; oral acyclovir 400mg three times daily for 5 days; analgesia; partner notification; abstain from sexual contact until healed; long-term suppressive therapy if recurrent.
red_flags: Urinary retention from sacral nerve involvement
Disseminated HSV in immunocompromised patient
Concurrent HIV acquisition risk requiring PEP

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهرين وبحس بتنميل وخدر في أصابع إيدي الصغيرة من الجهة الشمال، وبيشتد الليل. بيجيلي أحياناً ألم بيمشي للكتف. شغلي على الكمبيوتر طول اليوم.
urgency_reason: Ulnar nerve distribution paraesthesia with nocturnal worsening in an office worker suggests cubital tunnel syndrome requiring conservative management.
recommended_action: Nerve conduction studies; elbow padding to avoid prolonged flexion; night splint in elbow extension; ergonomic workstation adjustment; refer to hand surgeon if progressive intrinsic muscle wasting or failed conservative treatment at 3 months.
red_flags: Intrinsic hand muscle wasting indicating severe compression
Loss of finger abduction and adduction
Rapid progression of weakness despite conservative measures

user_symptom_description: My blood pressure was checked at the pharmacy and it was 190/115. I have a terrible headache at the back of my head and my vision went blurry for a few minutes. I feel like something is very wrong.
urgency_reason: Severely elevated BP with neurological symptoms including headache and visual disturbance constitutes hypertensive emergency with end-organ damage.
recommended_action: Call 999; go to A&E immediately; IV labetalol or nicardipine — aim to reduce MAP by 25% in first hour; urinalysis for proteinuria; ECG; troponin; CT head; fundoscopy for papilloedema; do not drop BP too fast.
red_flags: Focal neurological deficit from hypertensive stroke
Chest pain suggesting hypertensive aortic dissection
Seizures from hypertensive encephalopathy

user_symptom_description: My 7-year-old has had a sore throat, headache, and fever of 39.2°C for 2 days. She says it really hurts to swallow and I can see white patches at the back of her throat. No cough at all.
urgency_reason: Centor score 4 with exudate, fever, no cough, and tender nodes indicates likely streptococcal tonsillitis requiring antibiotic treatment to prevent rheumatic fever.
recommended_action: Throat swab for culture; penicillin V for 10 days or single dose IM benzylpenicillin; if penicillin allergy use azithromycin; paracetamol for fever; avoid amoxicillin — causes rash if EBV; monospot test if lymphadenopathy is generalised.
red_flags: Unilateral peritonsillar swelling with uvula deviation suggesting abscess
Stridor or drooling suggesting airway involvement
Petechiae on palate with generalised lymphadenopathy suggesting EBV

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام وجعني خاصرتي الأيمن من الخلف جداً، زي ما حد بيضربني على ظهري، والوجع بينزل لأسفل بطني. وعندي حرقان لما بجيب بول وبولي اتلوّن.
urgency_reason: Severe loin-to-groin colicky pain with haematuria and dysuria is ureteric colic requiring analgesia, hydration, and CT KUB to confirm stone.
recommended_action: CT KUB without contrast; urinalysis and urine culture; IV diclofenac or morphine; IV fluids; FBC and CRP to exclude infection; urology referral if stone above 6mm or obstructed infected kidney — emergency decompression with nephrostomy if latter.
red_flags: Fever with loin pain suggesting infected obstructed kidney requiring emergency drainage
Pulsatile abdominal mass suggesting aortic aneurysm
Anuria in solitary kidney

user_symptom_description: I gave birth 6 weeks ago and for the past 3 weeks I've felt completely empty — like I'm not bonding with my baby at all. I cry all day, I'm terrified I'm going to hurt him even though I would never want to. I feel like a terrible mother.
urgency_reason: Severe postnatal depression with intrusive thoughts requires same-day assessment to exclude postpartum psychosis and ensure maternal and infant safety.
recommended_action: Same-day perinatal mental health assessment; Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale; intrusive thoughts are ego-dystonic and common — validate and reassure; SSRI safe in breastfeeding (sertraline); perinatal CBT; health visitor daily contact; explore social support.
red_flags: Auditory hallucinations or delusional beliefs about the baby suggesting psychosis
Intent to harm self or baby
Rapid deterioration in self-care

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 28 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين عندي ألم شديد في إبهام رجلي الأيمن، منتفخ وأحمر وسخن. مش قادر أمشي. ما أكلش حاجة غريبة بس شربت كتير الأسبوع اللي فات.
urgency_reason: Classic podagra with acute first MTP joint inflammation after alcohol intake requires urate-lowering therapy after the acute attack subsides.
