urgency_reason: Tender cervical lymph node persisting 3 weeks after a viral illness most likely represents reactive adenopathy; EBV serology and 2-week observation before biopsy is appropriate.
recommended_action: GP appointment; EBV monospot, FBC, and CRP; reassess in 2-4 weeks; if node enlarging, non-tender, or multiple sites involved, urgent ENT or haematology referral for biopsy.
red_flags: Node doubling in size over 2 weeks
Night sweats and weight loss suggesting lymphoma
Hard fixed non-tender node

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع بحس بألم حاد في بطني حول السرة وجنبه الأيمن، بيزيد بالحركة وعندي إقياء مستمر ودرجة حرارتي 38.5.
urgency_reason: Classical appendicitis migration pattern with fever and vomiting requires emergency surgical assessment to prevent perforation and peritonitis.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; nothing by mouth; CBC, CRP, and CT abdomen; surgical consult for appendicectomy; IV antibiotics and fluids preoperatively.
red_flags: Sudden relief of pain followed by spreading abdominal pain suggesting perforation
Rigid abdomen on palpation
Haemodynamic instability

user_symptom_description: My 77-year-old mother keeps saying her mouth is very dry even though she drinks plenty of water. Her eyes are also very gritty and uncomfortable, and she had to stop wearing her contact lenses.
urgency_reason: Bilateral sicca syndrome in an elderly woman requires autoimmune panel and Schirmer's test to confirm Sjögren's and screen for systemic complications.
recommended_action: GP referral to rheumatology; ANA, anti-Ro, anti-La antibodies; Schirmer's test and minor salivary gland biopsy; artificial tears and saliva substitutes; dental review for caries risk.
red_flags: Parotid gland swelling suggesting lymphoma transformation in Sjögren's
Peripheral neuropathy or vasculitic rash
Kidney involvement with proteinuria

user_symptom_description: I've been having episodes of very fast heartbeats that come and go suddenly. Each episode lasts about 15-20 minutes and makes me feel dizzy and short of breath. It started 3 weeks ago and has happened 4 times.
urgency_reason: Recurrent paroxysmal tachycardia with hemodynamic symptoms requires 24-hour Holter monitoring and electrophysiology review to exclude dangerous arrhythmia.
recommended_action: Urgent cardiology referral; 12-lead ECG; 24-hour Holter or event monitor; echocardiogram; teach Valsalva maneuver for termination; discuss ablation vs beta-blocker depending on mechanism.
red_flags: Syncopal episode during palpitations
Heart rate above 200bpm on monitor
ECG showing delta waves suggesting WPW

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بشوف صفرة في جلدي وعيني وبولي اتصفر. ما عنديش ألم في بطني بس بحس بإرهاق وغثيان خفيف. ما بدخنش وما بشربش كحول.
urgency_reason: New jaundice with fatigue requires urgent liver function tests and hepatitis serology to identify the cause and prevent progression to liver failure.
recommended_action: Go to GP or urgent care today; LFTs, bilirubin, hepatitis A IgM, hepatitis B surface antigen, and EBV serology; abdominal ultrasound; contact isolation guidance; avoid hepatotoxic drugs.
red_flags: Confusion or drowsiness suggesting hepatic encephalopathy
INR rising indicating synthetic liver failure
Abdominal pain with fever suggesting cholangitis

user_symptom_description: I've been on a hiking trip in a forested area and noticed a circular red rash expanding from a small bite on my lower leg. The outer ring is darker and the center is clearing. I feel mildly tired and have a slight fever.
urgency_reason: Erythema migrans is pathognomonic for early Lyme disease and requires prompt antibiotic treatment to prevent dissemination to the heart and nervous system.
recommended_action: Start doxycycline 100mg twice daily for 21 days without waiting for serology; Lyme serology at 4 weeks; monitor for joint pains, palpitations, or facial palsy suggesting disseminated disease.
red_flags: Heart block symptoms like syncope or extreme bradycardia
Facial nerve palsy
Arthritis in large joints weeks after the rash

user_symptom_description: ابني عمره 4 سنين ومن 5 أيام عنده حرارة مستمرة فوق 39 وبعد يومين لاحظنا إن عيناه زي ما هما محمرين من غير قيح وشفايفه متشققة وحمرا وفيه طفح أحمر في جسمه.
urgency_reason: Five or more days of fever with bilateral non-purulent conjunctivitis, mucosal changes, and rash meets Kawasaki criteria; untreated coronary aneurysms develop in 25% of cases.
recommended_action: Go to pediatric ER immediately; echocardiogram to assess coronary arteries; IVIG 2g/kg single infusion plus aspirin; admit for cardiac monitoring; repeat echo at 2 and 6 weeks.
red_flags: Coronary artery dilation on echo requiring anticoagulation
Myocarditis with cardiac failure
Persistent fever after IVIG requiring retreatment

user_symptom_description: I was in a car accident 2 days ago. I had neck pain straight after but it seemed to settle. Now it's much worse, I have a headache at the base of my skull, and my left arm feels tingly.
urgency_reason: Delayed worsening neck pain with arm paresthesia after a road accident raises concern for missed cervical fracture or vertebral artery dissection requiring urgent spinal imaging.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; do not manipulate the neck; CT cervical spine and CT angiography of vertebral arteries; neurosurgical consult; MRI if no bony injury but neurology persists.
red_flags: Weakness or paralysis developing in arms or legs
Sudden worsening headache suggesting subarachnoid hemorrhage
Loss of bladder or bowel control

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر بحس بثقل وألم في كيس الصفن من تحت في الجانب الأيمن وبيزيد لما بوقف أو بمشي كتير وبيروح لو اتمددت. مش في ألم حاد.
urgency_reason: Positional scrotal aching relieved by lying down is classic for varicocele; scrotal ultrasound confirms diagnosis and excludes testicular mass.
recommended_action: Scrotal ultrasound with Doppler to confirm varicocele grade; urology referral; discuss embolization or surgical ligation if pain is significant; PSA and tumor markers to exclude concurrent malignancy.
red_flags: Rapid increase in testicular size alongside the varicocele
Right-sided varicocele in an older man suggesting retroperitoneal mass compressing renal vein
New onset varicocele in elderly patient warrants renal ultrasound

user_symptom_description: I have type 1 diabetes and I woke up this morning with my blood sugar at 42mg/dL. I was shaking, sweating, very confused, and barely able to stand. My husband gave me orange juice and I'm a bit better now.
urgency_reason: Severe hypoglycemia with neurological symptoms in a T1DM patient requires ER evaluation to ensure sustained glucose recovery and insulin dose review.
recommended_action: Go to ER for IV dextrose confirmation, sustained glucose monitoring, and insulin review; check if cause is accidental overdose; endocrinology follow-up within 48 hours to adjust basal-bolus regimen.
red_flags: Losing consciousness before treatment is reached
Seizure triggered by hypoglycemia
Recurrent episodes suggesting hypoglycemic unawareness needing CGM

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 23 سنة وبعد ما رجعت من رحلة سفر قبل أسبوعين، بقاله 10 أيام عندي إسهال مستمر مائي مع مغص وغثيان. مش عندي دم في البراز وما عنديش حمى.
urgency_reason: Persistent non-bloody diarrhea 10 days after travel is most consistent with Giardia or other protozoal infection requiring stool examination.
recommended_action: Stool for ova, cysts, and parasites x3 samples; stool Giardia antigen ELISA; start metronidazole 400mg TDS for 5 days if Giardia positive; oral rehydration; reassess if diarrhea persists beyond 2 weeks.
red_flags: Bloody diarrhea developing
Fever with high white cell count suggesting bacterial colitis
Severe dehydration with inability to tolerate oral fluids

user_symptom_description: I've had a dry cough for about 5 weeks that won't go away. It happens mostly at night and sometimes when I laugh or breathe in cold air. I don't smoke and have no fever. I started a new blood pressure tablet 6 weeks ago.
extracted_symptoms: triggered by cold air and laughter
urgency_reason: Dry cough developing within 4-8 weeks of ACE inhibitor initiation is a class side effect occurring in 15% of patients, reversible with medication switch.
recommended_action: GP appointment; switch ACE inhibitor to ARB such as losartan; cough should resolve within 4 weeks; if persistent, spirometry and ENT review to exclude alternative cause.
red_flags: Hemoptysis at any point
Fever with productive cough suggesting superimposed infection
Cough not resolving 8 weeks after stopping ACE inhibitor

user_symptom_description: بقالها 3 أسابيع بكي مستمر وما بتاكلش ومش قادرة تنام وبتقول إنها مش لاقية أي معنى للحياة. توفي جوزها من 3 شهور.
urgency_reason: Functional impairment with passive death wishes and anorexia in a bereaved elderly woman exceeds normal grief and warrants psychiatric evaluation.
recommended_action: GP referral to old-age psychiatry within 1 week; assess for active suicidal ideation; low-dose mirtazapine to address both mood and appetite; grief counseling; community support services.
red_flags: Active suicidal statements or plan
Refusing food or fluids leading to medical deterioration
Psychotic symptoms such as hearing deceased spouse's voice commanding harm

user_symptom_description: I was working construction and a small piece of metal flew into my right eye. I can feel something in there, my eye is red and watering constantly, and my vision looks a bit blurry.
urgency_reason: Metallic foreign body in the eye from a high-velocity source can penetrate the globe and requires immediate ophthalmological assessment to exclude intraocular injury.
recommended_action: Go to eye casualty immediately; do not rub or patch eye; slit-lamp examination with fluorescein staining; if penetrating injury suspected, CT orbits; foreign body removal under topical anaesthesia.
red_flags: Loss of vision
Uveal tissue prolapse indicating open globe injury
Pain disproportionate to injury size

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام بحس بألم في صدري الأيسر بيزيد لما باخد نفس عميق أو باتحرك. بعد ما رجعت من سفر طويل بالطيارة 12 ساعة وبقالها 4 أيام.
urgency_reason: Pleuritic chest pain after prolonged immobility on a long-haul flight is pulmonary embolism until excluded; Wells score is high and D-dimer or CTPA is mandatory.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; Wells score calculation; D-dimer if low probability; CTPA if intermediate-high probability; start LMWH or DOAC anticoagulation if PE confirmed; oxygen supplementation.
red_flags: Haemoptysis
Hypotension or tachycardia suggesting massive PE
Oxygen saturation below 90%

user_symptom_description: I've had severe lower back pain for 3 days after lifting heavy boxes. The pain shoots down my right leg to the foot and my toes feel numb. Coughing makes the pain much worse.
extracted_symptoms: acute low back pain after lifting
urgency_reason: L4/L5 or L5/S1 disc prolapse with dermatomal radiculopathy requires MRI to confirm and monitor for progression to cauda equina involvement.
recommended_action: MRI lumbar spine within 1 week; NSAIDs and short course diazepam for spasm; physiotherapy; neurosurgical referral if foot drop or cauda equina signs develop; advise to remain active.
red_flags: Saddle anesthesia or perineal numbness
New bladder or bowel incontinence
Progressive foot drop requiring emergency MRI and surgery within 24 hours

user_symptom_description: عندي 71 سنة وبقاله أسبوعين بفقد توازني وبتعثر وأنا بمشي. إيدي بتترعش وقت السكون وحركاتي بقت بطيئة وجسمي متصلب.
urgency_reason: Classic Parkinsonian triad of resting tremor, bradykinesia, and rigidity requires neurological confirmation to initiate dopaminergic therapy and prevent fall-related injury.
recommended_action: Neurology appointment within 2 weeks; medication review to exclude drug-induced cause; DaTSCAN if diagnosis uncertain; start levodopa/carbidopa; physiotherapy and falls prevention program.
red_flags: Rapid symptom progression over weeks
Early falls suggesting PSP rather than Parkinson's
Hallucinations before medication start suggesting Lewy body dementia

user_symptom_description: My 18-month-old has had a very high fever of 104°F and has been very irritable. A few hours ago I noticed a red rash that started on her face and spread to her body. The spots don't go away when I press a glass on them.
urgency_reason: Non-blanching petechial rash with high fever in a toddler is meningococcal disease until excluded — minutes matter and immediate IV penicillin can be life-saving.
recommended_action: Call emergency services immediately; give IM or IV benzylpenicillin now if available; go to pediatric ER; blood cultures and lumbar puncture; ceftriaxone IV; ICU admission; public health notification.
red_flags: Rash spreading rapidly within minutes to hours
Child becoming unresponsive
Cold peripheries and mottled skin indicating septic shock

user_symptom_description: عندي 35 سنة وبقالي أسبوع بحس بألم في بطني الأيسر من تحت وبيجيلي إسهال وغازات كتير. الألم بيقل بعد ما بروح الحمام. مش عندي دم.
urgency_reason: Cramping with defecation relief and altered bowel habit in the absence of alarm features in a young adult is consistent with IBS requiring dietary modification.
recommended_action: Low-FODMAP diet for 4-6 weeks; peppermint oil capsules for IBS-D; stool culture to exclude infectious cause; coeliac serology; reassess in 6 weeks with dietary diary.
red_flags: Blood in stool or rectal bleeding
Weight loss or anemia
Night sweats or fever

user_symptom_description: I've been feeling confused and having trouble concentrating for the past week. I also have a severe headache, sensitivity to light, and my neck feels stiff and painful when I try to touch my chin to my chest.
urgency_reason: The classic triad of headache, neck stiffness, and photophobia with confusion meets criteria for meningitis, which carries 30% mortality if treatment is delayed beyond 1 hour.
recommended_action: Call emergency services immediately; go to ER for CT brain before lumbar puncture if signs of raised ICP; start IV ceftriaxone plus dexamethasone immediately — do not wait for CT results if CT unavailable.
red_flags: Non-blanching petechial rash indicating meningococcal disease
Seizure or loss of consciousness
Papilledema suggesting raised intracranial pressure

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهرين بحس بألم وتورم في كتفي الأيسر وركبتي اليمين بالتناوب. الألم بيكون حار وبيصحيني من النوم وبيروح من تلقاءه. عندي بقع بيضاء في فمي.
urgency_reason: Migratory arthritis with oral lesions requires systemic disease workup including HIV, ANA, and HLA-B27 to guide the appropriate treatment pathway.
recommended_action: Rheumatology referral within 2 weeks; HIV test, HLA-B27, ANA, CRP, ESR, and VDRL; recent STI and GI infection history; NSAIDs for acute episodes; treat underlying trigger if identified.
red_flags: Eye redness suggesting uveitis — the third element of Reiter's triad
Urethral discharge
Skin lesions on palms or soles suggesting keratoderma

user_symptom_description: My 79-year-old aunt has been refusing to eat or drink for 2 days and just lies in bed staring at the ceiling. She was diagnosed with advanced cancer 3 months ago. She says she is ready to die.
possible_conditions: Clinical Depression with Passive Death Wish
urgency_reason: Refusal of food and fluid in terminal cancer may represent the natural dying process, but comfort care needs, pain assessment, and family support require palliative care involvement.
recommended_action: Urgent palliative care or hospice team referral; stop unnecessary medications; ensure mouth care and pain control; do not initiate IV fluids without patient consent; support family with end-of-life guidance.
red_flags: Uncontrolled pain or distress requiring syringe driver
Respiratory distress needing low-dose opioids
Family or patient requesting active intervention conflicting with advance directive

user_symptom_description: من ساعتين وأنا بحس بحكة شديدة في الحلق وتورم في لساني وشفايفي بعد ما أكلت مكسرات. بحس إن نفسي بيضيق.
urgency_reason: Airway angioedema with dyspnea after a known food allergen is life-threatening anaphylaxis requiring immediate epinephrine.
recommended_action: Call emergency services immediately; inject epinephrine 0.3mg IM into outer thigh; sit upright if breathing difficulty; go to ER; IV hydrocortisone and chlorphenamine after epinephrine; prescribe auto-injector on discharge.
red_flags: Stridor or voice change indicating airway compromise
Loss of consciousness
Rash not improving after epinephrine requiring second dose

user_symptom_description: I've had a mole on my back that my girlfriend noticed has gotten bigger and darker over the past few months. It used to be a flat brown spot but now it has irregular edges and different colors — brown and black.
urgency_reason: ABCDE criteria met with asymmetry, irregular border, and color variation in a changing mole — urgent dermatological excision biopsy required within 2 weeks.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week cancer pathway dermatology referral; full skin examination and dermoscopy; excision biopsy with 2mm margin; do not use laser or shave biopsy; sentinel node biopsy if melanoma confirmed.
red_flags: Ulceration or bleeding from the lesion
Satellite lesions appearing around the mole
Palpable lymph nodes in the axilla or groin

user_symptom_description: عندي 38 سنة وبحس بوخز وتنميل في إيدي الشمال خصوصاً الإبهام والسبابة والوسطى، بيزيد بالليل ولما بمسك إيدي، وبيروح لو رفعت إيدي لفوق.
extracted_symptoms: thumb and first two finger paresthesia
urgency_reason: Classic median nerve distribution paresthesia with nocturnal symptoms and Phalen's relief pattern is carpal tunnel syndrome requiring nerve conduction study to confirm severity.
recommended_action: Neutral position wrist splint at night; nerve conduction studies; steroid injection into carpal tunnel; surgical decompression if conservative management fails after 3 months.
red_flags: Thenar muscle wasting
Complete hand numbness on waking not resolving with movement
Weakness of thumb abduction suggesting advanced median nerve damage

user_symptom_description: I have atrial fibrillation and take warfarin. Last night I had a severe headache that came on suddenly and this morning the left side of my face is drooping and my arm is weak. The headache is still there.
urgency_reason: Thunderclap headache with acute motor deficit on anticoagulation is intracranial hemorrhage until proven otherwise requiring immediate reversal of anticoagulation.
recommended_action: Call emergency services immediately; note warfarin dose and last INR; go to stroke unit for emergency CT brain; give vitamin K and prothrombin complex concentrate to reverse anticoagulation urgently; neurosurgical assessment.
red_flags: Rapidly declining consciousness level
Bilateral motor weakness indicating brainstem involvement
Vomiting with worsening headache suggesting raised intracranial pressure

user_symptom_description: بقالي 4 أشهر بيجيلي ألم في وجهي على شمال من عيني لخدي زي الصاعقة بيدوم ثواني بس مؤلم جداً. بيجيلي لما باكل أو أتكلم أو حتى ألمس وجهي.
urgency_reason: Classic electric shock facial pain with cutaneous triggers on the trigeminal distribution requires MRI to exclude vascular compression and initiate carbamazepine treatment.
recommended_action: Neurology appointment within 2 weeks; MRI brain with trigeminal protocol to exclude neurovascular contact or MS plaque; start carbamazepine 100mg titrating slowly; consider MVD surgery if refractory.
red_flags: Bilateral trigeminal pain suggesting multiple sclerosis
Sensory loss accompanying the pain
Pain persisting between attacks suggesting atypical etiology

user_symptom_description: My 12-year-old has a painful bony lump just below his right knee for 3 weeks. It's worse after football practice and he's been limping. There's no redness and no fever.
urgency_reason: Tibial tuberosity apophysitis in an active adolescent is Osgood-Schlatter disease, a self-limiting condition managed with activity modification.
recommended_action: Reduce high-impact activity; ice packs after sports; physiotherapy for quadriceps stretching; knee X-ray to confirm apophyseal fragmentation; reassure parents that it resolves after growth plates close.
red_flags: Night pain waking from sleep suggesting bone tumor
Rapidly enlarging soft tissue mass
Fever or constitutional symptoms

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وجنبي الأيمن من تحت الإبط بيوجع وأنا باخد نفس أو بأتحرك. عندي حمى خفيفة وسعال بيطلع بلغم أصفر. مش بدخن.
extracted_symptoms: right lateral chest pain on breathing
urgency_reason: Pleuritic chest pain with productive cough and fever meets criteria for pneumonia requiring antibiotic treatment; CURB-65 score needed to assess hospital admission.
recommended_action: Go to urgent care for chest X-ray and CURB-65 scoring; start amoxicillin 500mg TDS plus clarithromycin for 5-7 days if outpatient; return if breathing worsens or oxygen drops; blood cultures if admitted.
red_flags: Oxygen saturation below 94%
Confusion or hypotension
Rapidly increasing effusion on repeat X-ray requiring drainage

user_symptom_description: I was helping a friend move and suddenly felt a pop in my left calf followed by severe pain and swelling. Now I can't walk normally and pressing on the back of my calf is extremely tender.
urgency_reason: Calf muscle tear needs to be distinguished from Achilles rupture and DVT by clinical examination and ultrasound before rehabilitation planning.
recommended_action: RICE protocol immediately; calf ultrasound to confirm tear and exclude DVT; partial weight-bearing with crutches; physiotherapy at 48 hours; compression bandage; reassess in 2 weeks.
red_flags: Inability to perform any plantarflexion suggesting Achilles rupture
Increasing swelling with calf warmth and pitting edema suggesting DVT
Compartment syndrome with extreme pain and hard tight calf

user_symptom_description: بقالها شهرين بقشعريرة ليلية وعرق شديد بالليل مع فقدان وزن 7 كيلو وبيجيلها حمى متكررة. الغدد اللمفاوية في رقبتها كبرت.
urgency_reason: Classic B symptoms with lymphadenopathy in an elderly woman require urgent haematology evaluation to confirm lymphoma diagnosis and initiate staging.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week cancer referral; FBC with differential, LDH, ESR, and CRP; CT chest-abdomen-pelvis; lymph node excision biopsy; PET scan for staging; haematology MDT review.
red_flags: Mediastinal mass causing superior vena cava obstruction with facial swelling
Splenomegaly with thrombocytopenia suggesting bone marrow involvement
Fever above 39°C unresponsive to antipyretics

user_symptom_description: I've been drinking a lot more alcohol lately — about a bottle of wine a night — and I noticed my hands shake every morning until I have a drink, and I feel anxious and sweaty without alcohol.
possible_conditions: Alcohol Use Disorder with Physiological Dependence
primary_condition: Alcohol Use Disorder with Physiological Dependence
urgency_reason: Physical withdrawal symptoms including tremor, anxiety, and sweating indicate alcohol dependence; abrupt cessation risks seizures and delirium tremens without medical support.
recommended_action: Seek GP or addiction service urgently before stopping alcohol; CIWA-Ar assessment; supervised chlordiazepoxide reducing regimen; thiamine IV then oral; AUDIT questionnaire; motivational interviewing.
red_flags: Seizure occurring after missed drinks
Hallucinations visual or auditory indicating delirium tremens
Sudden cessation without medical supervision

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 20 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين واحة في ظهر رقبتي بيكبر ببطء وبيوجع لما أضغط عليه. جلده أحمر وحاسة بيجيلي حرارة خفيفة.
urgency_reason: Fluctuant infected neck swelling with fever requires incision and drainage and antibiotic cover to prevent spread to deep neck spaces.
recommended_action: GP appointment within 24 hours; if fluctuant, incision and drainage; send pus for MC&S; co-amoxiclav 5-7 days; warm compress twice daily; urgent ENT if swelling moves into deep neck.
red_flags: Neck stiffness or difficulty swallowing
Rapidly spreading redness or stridor
Swelling tracking toward the chest

user_symptom_description: I've been having lower abdominal pain and fever for 3 days. When I press deeply on my lower right abdomen the pain is much worse, and I notice pain when I release the pressure quickly. My last colonoscopy showed diverticula.
urgency_reason: Rebound tenderness with fever in a patient with known diverticulosis suggests acute diverticulitis with possible localized perforation requiring CT and IV antibiotics.
recommended_action: Go to ER for CT abdomen with contrast; IV metronidazole plus co-amoxiclav; nil by mouth; surgical consultation if perforation or abscess on CT; follow-up colonoscopy at 6 weeks after resolution.
red_flags: Free air under diaphragm on X-ray suggesting perforation
Haemodynamic instability from peritonitis
Feculent or foul-smelling fistula discharge

user_symptom_description: بقالها أسبوعين بكاء وتشاؤم وبتقول إنها مسؤولة عن مرض ابنها رغم إنها مش سبب فيه. بتسمع صوت بيعاتبها وبتشوف ناس في أوضتها. مش بتنام.
urgency_reason: Depressive illness with psychotic features including guilt delusions and hallucinations in an elderly woman requires urgent psychiatric assessment and combined antidepressant-antipsychotic therapy.
recommended_action: Urgent psychiatry referral; rule out organic cause with FBC, TFTs, vitamin B12, and urinalysis; start risperidone plus sertraline or consider ECT; consider inpatient admission for safety; engage family.
red_flags: Active suicidal plan acting on delusional guilt
Refusing food and fluids
Medically reversible cause such as UTI or medication toxicity missed

user_symptom_description: I accidentally took double my metformin dose this morning — 4 grams instead of 2. I feel nauseous and have diarrhea. I know metformin can sometimes cause a dangerous condition with high lactate.
urgency_reason: A single double dose of metformin 4g rarely causes lactic acidosis in a patient with normal renal function, but symptoms and serum lactate must be assessed urgently given the potential severity.
recommended_action: Go to ER for serum lactate, renal function, blood gas, and glucose; withhold next metformin dose; IV hydration if unable to tolerate fluids; hold metformin for 48 hours pending renal function results.
red_flags: Rapid breathing suggesting metabolic acidosis
Confusion or extreme fatigue from rising lactate
Creatinine rise suggesting renal impairment increasing toxicity risk

user_symptom_description: I've had a painless yellow bump on my upper eyelid for about 3 weeks. It's not red or hot but it's getting bigger and now my eyelid feels heavy. It started as a small pimple.
urgency_reason: A painless non-inflamed eyelid nodule growing slowly over weeks is consistent with a chalazion manageable with warm compresses before considering incision.
recommended_action: Warm compress 10 minutes four times daily for 4 weeks; lid massage after compress; ophthalmology referral for incision and curettage if no resolution; biopsy if recurrent to exclude sebaceous carcinoma.
red_flags: Loss of lashes around the lesion suggesting malignancy
Recurrence in the same site after drainage
Orbital extension with proptosis

user_symptom_description: عندي 74 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين بحس بضيق نفس مفاجئ بيجيلي وبيروح، وبلاقي قلبي بيرفرف بشكل غريب. بقيسي نبضي وبيطلع مش منتظم أحياناً.
urgency_reason: New irregular pulse with dyspnea in an elderly man requires ECG confirmation of AF and urgent assessment for stroke risk and rate control.
recommended_action: Go to urgent care or ER today for ECG; if AF confirmed start rate control with bisoprolol or digoxin; calculate CHA2DS2-VASc for anticoagulation; echocardiogram; thyroid function tests.
red_flags: Hemodynamic instability requiring DC cardioversion
Rapid ventricular rate above 150 with chest pain
New neurological symptoms suggesting cardioembolic stroke

user_symptom_description: I've been intensely restricting what I eat for about 6 months. I'm 5'4" and weigh 88 pounds. I feel cold all the time, my hair is falling out, and I haven't had a period in 4 months. My family is very worried.
urgency_reason: Critically low BMI with amenorrhea and metabolic signs indicates medically severe anorexia nervosa requiring urgent nutritional and psychiatric assessment to prevent cardiac or renal failure.
recommended_action: Urgent GP referral to eating disorder service; ECG for QTc prolongation; electrolytes, phosphate, magnesium, and glucose for refeeding syndrome risk; consider inpatient admission if BMI below 14 or cardiac instability.
red_flags: Syncope or bradycardia below 50 from cardiac muscle wasting
Potassium below 3.0 mmol/L
Refusal of all oral nutrition requiring nasogastric feeding

user_symptom_description: عندي 42 سنة وبقالي أسبوع بحس بألم حاد في منطقة الخاصرة الأيمن بيشع لأسفل بطني ولكيس الصفن. الألم جاي وراح وبولي فيه دم خفيف.
urgency_reason: Classic ureteric colic with hematuria requires imaging to confirm stone location, assess obstruction, and plan management to protect renal function.
recommended_action: Go to urgent care or ER for CT KUB non-contrast; IV diclofenac and morphine for pain; IV fluids; urine dipstick; urology referral if stone above 6mm or obstructed kidney; strain urine to catch stone.
red_flags: Fever with loin pain suggesting obstructed infected kidney — urological emergency
Single kidney with complete obstruction
Pain not relieved with IV analgesia

user_symptom_description: My 5-year-old has been complaining that her tummy hurts around her belly button every day for 3 weeks. The pain usually goes away on its own and she doesn't have a fever, vomiting, or diarrhea. She seems fine between episodes.
urgency_reason: Recurrent periumbilical pain with no alarm features and normal intervals in a school-age child is most likely functional and managed with reassurance and dietary review.
recommended_action: Pediatric appointment; dietary diary and fiber intake review; exclude constipation with abdominal X-ray; reassure parents; consider stress or school anxiety trigger; review in 4 weeks.
red_flags: Pain waking child from sleep at night
Weight loss or growth faltering
Blood in stool or persistent vomiting

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر بيجيلي ألم في رسغي الأيمن بيزيد لما بمسك حاجة أو بلف إيدي، وفيه تورم خفيف فوق الإبهام. بدأ بعد ما ابني الجديد اتولد وأنا بشيله كتير.
extracted_symptoms: worsened by gripping and wrist rotation
urgency_reason: De Quervain's tenosynovitis is common postpartum from repetitive lifting and is managed conservatively with splinting and steroid injection.
recommended_action: Thumb spica splint for 4-6 weeks; avoid aggravating activities; corticosteroid injection into first dorsal compartment if no improvement; wrist X-ray to exclude scaphoid fracture.
red_flags: Anatomical snuffbox tenderness suggesting scaphoid fracture
Complete inability to oppose thumb
Numbness in thumb suggesting radial nerve involvement

user_symptom_description: I'm 70 and I've been having burning pain when I urinate and have to go urgently but only pass a few drops each time. This has happened 4 times in the past 6 months. The urine is sometimes pink.
urgency_reason: Recurrent UTIs with hematuria in a postmenopausal woman require cystoscopy to exclude bladder malignancy alongside treatment for atrophic vaginitis as a predisposing cause.
recommended_action: Urine MC&S; cystoscopy to exclude bladder cancer given recurrent hematuria; topical vaginal estrogen for atrophic vaginitis; low-dose prophylactic nitrofurantoin; urology follow-up.
red_flags: Painless macroscopic hematuria throughout the stream
Pelvic mass on examination
Weight loss with recurrent bladder symptoms

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 27 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين بقلقل من أي حاجة، نفسي بيقصر، قلبي بيدق بسرعة وكل شوية بيجيلي إحساس إن حاجة وحشة هتحصل من غير سبب واضح.
urgency_reason: Chronic free-floating anxiety with somatic symptoms requires TSH and ECG to exclude organic cause before initiating CBT and SSRI therapy.
recommended_action: GP appointment for TSH, ECG, and CBC; refer to CBT as first-line; consider sertraline if symptoms impairing function; relaxation techniques and sleep hygiene; review in 6 weeks.
red_flags: Panic attacks leading to complete inability to leave home
Suicidal ideation linked to hopelessness
New thyroid swelling or weight loss suggesting hyperthyroid cause

user_symptom_description: I noticed that the whites of my eyes have turned slightly yellow and my skin looks a bit yellow too. I've also had dark urine and pale clay-colored stools for the past week. No pain at all.
urgency_reason: Painless obstructive jaundice with pale stools and dark urine is pancreatic head cancer or biliary obstruction until proven otherwise, requiring urgent imaging.
recommended_action: Urgent CT abdomen with contrast and MRCP; CA 19-9 tumor marker; LFTs and bilirubin; surgical oncology referral; ERCP for biliary decompression if obstructed; 2-week cancer pathway.
red_flags: Rapid deepening jaundice with pruritus
New-onset diabetes accompanying jaundice
Weight loss and back pain suggesting advanced disease

user_symptom_description: ابني لاحظ إني بقيت بحكي في نومي وبتحرك كتير وبضرب في الهوا وكأني بلعب دور في حلم. بقاله 3 شهور وخبطت مراتي مرة من غير قصد.
urgency_reason: REM sleep behavior disorder in an elderly man is a prodromal marker of Parkinson's disease or Lewy body dementia and requires neurological evaluation and injury prevention.
recommended_action: Neurology referral within 2 weeks; polysomnography to confirm RBD; neurological examination for early Parkinsonism; clonazepam 0.5mg at bedtime; remove sharp objects from bedroom; bed rail for safety.
red_flags: Falls from bed causing injury
Daytime neurological symptoms like tremor or rigidity
Hallucinations developing during waking hours

user_symptom_description: I've had painful sores in my mouth that keep coming back every few weeks. They're round with a white center and a red rim and last about 10 days. I get 2 or 3 at a time and they make eating miserable.
extracted_symptoms: round with white center and red halo
urgency_reason: Recurrent minor aphthous ulcers are common and benign but frequent recurrence warrants celiac and vitamin B12 serology to exclude a treatable nutritional cause.
recommended_action: GP review; coeliac serology, B12, folate, and iron studies; topical triamcinolone or benzydamine for pain; chlorhexidine mouthwash; referral to oral medicine if ulcers are large or non-healing.
red_flags: Genital ulcers accompanying oral lesions suggesting Behcet's disease
Ulcer persisting beyond 3 weeks without healing
Weight loss or diarrhea suggesting malabsorption

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بحس بألم وتورم في صدري من الجانب الأيسر، في العظمة اللي بتربط ضلعي بالصدرة. بيزيد لما أضغط عليه أو باخد نفس عميق.
urgency_reason: Localized costochondral swelling with tenderness is Tietze syndrome, a benign self-limiting condition; cardiac cause must be excluded by ECG given chest location.
recommended_action: ECG and troponin to exclude cardiac cause; NSAIDs for 7-10 days; reassure patient; avoid chest wall strain; local heat application; if no improvement in 4 weeks, consider steroid injection.
red_flags: ECG changes or elevated troponin
Fever with chest wall swelling suggesting osteomyelitis
Radiation of pain to arm or jaw

user_symptom_description: I've been getting hives — red itchy welts all over my body — that come and go randomly every day for the past 6 weeks. They appear, last a few hours, and disappear. No food or drug trigger I can identify.
urgency_reason: Chronic urticaria lasting over 6 weeks without identifiable trigger is chronic spontaneous urticaria requiring antihistamine therapy and thyroid autoimmune screening.
recommended_action: Cetirizine 10mg daily as first-line; thyroid function and anti-TPO antibodies; total IgE and ANA; if refractory to antihistamines, dermatology referral for omalizumab; urticaria activity score diary.
red_flags: Lesions persisting beyond 24 hours in one spot suggesting vasculitis
Throat swelling or angioedema
Systemic symptoms like fever or joint pain

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 73 سنة وبقالي 3 أشهر بحس بألم وحرقة في الجلد على جانب وجهي الأيمن حتى قبل ما يطلع أي طفح. طلع طفح مؤخراً قريب من عيني وبدأ بصري يتأثر.
urgency_reason: Zoster involving the ophthalmic branch of V1 with visual symptoms requires same-day ophthalmology assessment to prevent corneal scarring and vision loss.
recommended_action: Go to ophthalmology emergency today; start oral valacyclovir 1g TDS immediately; slit-lamp examination for keratitis and uveitis; topical steroid drops if uveitis confirmed; oral prednisolone if severe.
red_flags: Complete vision loss in the affected eye
Corneal ulceration with discharge
Involvement of both eye and nose tip — Hutchinson's sign indicating high ocular risk

user_symptom_description: I've been feeling very thirsty and passing huge amounts of very pale, almost colorless urine — maybe 8 to 10 liters a day. I'm not diabetic. I've been drinking all day and night and still feel thirsty.
urgency_reason: Massive polyuria with very dilute urine in a non-diabetic patient requires water deprivation test and MRI pituitary to distinguish central from nephrogenic diabetes insipidus.
recommended_action: Urgent endocrinology referral; fasting glucose and HbA1c to exclude diabetes mellitus; serum osmolality and urine osmolality; water deprivation test; MRI pituitary; desmopressin trial if central DI confirmed.
red_flags: Severe hypernatremia with confusion from dehydration
Visual field defects from pituitary mass
Inability to keep up with urine losses leading to hypovolemic shock

user_symptom_description: معلمة ابني في المدرسة اتصلت بيا وقالتلي إنه مش قادر يركز في أي حاجة وبيتحرك طول الوقت في الفصل ومش مذاكر. عمره 8 سنين وده بقاله سنة.
urgency_reason: Persistent inattention and hyperactivity impairing school function for over a year meets ADHD screening criteria and warrants formal pediatric neurodevelopmental assessment.
recommended_action: Pediatric referral for Conners rating scales from parent and teacher; exclude hearing and vision problems; EEG only if blank staring episodes present; consider methylphenidate after formal diagnosis; school support plan.
red_flags: Staring spells during inattentive episodes suggesting absence epilepsy
Regression in previously mastered skills
Tic disorder emerging alongside hyperactivity

user_symptom_description: I'm 3 months postpartum and I've been feeling incredibly sad, crying constantly, and having intrusive thoughts about accidentally hurting my baby. I feel like a terrible mother and I'm terrified of these thoughts.
urgency_reason: Postpartum depression with intrusive thoughts at 3 months requires urgent psychiatric assessment to distinguish ego-dystonic OCD thoughts from psychotic risk and initiate treatment.
recommended_action: Urgent perinatal psychiatry referral; Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale; reassure patient that ego-dystonic intrusive thoughts are OCD not intent; start SSRI safe in breastfeeding; weekly follow-up.
red_flags: Thoughts that feel pleasurable or that she wants to act on — suggest psychosis not OCD
Auditory hallucinations commanding harm to baby
Refusal to be left alone with infant from fear

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر بحس بألم في ظهر العنق بيشع لكتفي الأيمن وأحياناً لإيدي. الألم بيزيد لو بصيت لفوق وبيقل لو حطيت إيدي على راسي.
urgency_reason: Dermatomal arm radiation with postural relief is cervical radiculopathy requiring MRI to confirm disc level and guide conservative or surgical management.
recommended_action: MRI cervical spine; cervical collar for acute pain relief; physiotherapy with traction; NSAIDs and gabapentin for neuropathic component; neurosurgical referral if hand weakness develops.
red_flags: Hand weakness or thenar wasting
Bilateral arm symptoms or gait disturbance suggesting cord compression
Bladder dysfunction suggesting cervical myelopathy

user_symptom_description: I've been losing control of my bladder — sometimes I don't make it to the toilet in time and wet myself. It happens day and night. I also have to go about 12 times a day and it's ruining my social life. I'm 73.
urgency_reason: Urge incontinence with high frequency in an elderly man requires PSA, post-void residual, and urodynamics to exclude BPH overflow and neurological cause before anticholinergic therapy.
recommended_action: GP referral to urology; bladder diary; PSA and post-void residual ultrasound; urodynamic study; pelvic floor exercises and bladder training; mirabegron preferred over anticholinergics in elderly for cognitive safety.
red_flags: Hematuria at any point
Post-void residual above 300ml suggesting chronic retention
New onset in a Parkinson's patient suggesting disease progression

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 33 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين وجعة ضرسي الأيمن من تحت ووجعه بيزيد بالأكل الساخن والبارد ومش قادرة أنام منه بالليل. وشي منتفش من الجانب الأيمن.
urgency_reason: Dental abscess with facial swelling can spread to deep neck spaces causing Ludwig's angina — an airway emergency requiring urgent dental and possibly surgical management.
recommended_action: Emergency dental appointment today; dental X-ray; drainage of abscess plus amoxicillin 500mg TDS for 5 days; if swelling extending to neck or floor of mouth go to ER immediately for airway assessment.
red_flags: Swelling extending to floor of mouth or neck
Difficulty opening mouth or swallowing
Fever with airway compromise requiring intubation

user_symptom_description: I fell off my bike going fast and landed hard on my right hand. My wrist looks swollen and I can't move it without severe pain. The pain is worst when I press right in the hollow between my thumb tendons.
urgency_reason: Anatomical snuffbox tenderness after a fall on outstretched hand is a scaphoid fracture until proven otherwise; if missed, avascular necrosis of the scaphoid can cause permanent disability.
recommended_action: Go to ER for wrist X-ray; if snuffbox tenderness present with normal X-ray, treat as scaphoid fracture with thumb spica cast and repeat MRI at 10 days; orthopedic review if displaced fracture.
red_flags: Neurovascular compromise with cold numb fingers
Displaced fracture on X-ray requiring surgical fixation
Persistent non-union at 12 weeks on follow-up imaging

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين عندي إفرازات بيضاء كتير من المهبل مع حكة شديدة وحرقة، والإفرازات شكلها زي الجبن القريش وما عنديش رائحة غريبة.
urgency_reason: Classic presentation of candidal vaginitis with thick white odorless discharge and intense pruritus can be treated empirically with a single dose antifungal.
recommended_action: Single dose oral fluconazole 150mg or intravaginal clotrimazole pessary; avoid douching; if recurrent more than 4 times yearly, check HbA1c and consider 6-month fluconazole prophylaxis.
red_flags: Recurrence more than 4 times in a year
Fever or lower abdominal pain suggesting PID
No response to antifungal requiring high vaginal swab to confirm diagnosis

user_symptom_description: I'm 80 and I've been having terrible pain in my right hip at rest, especially at night, for the past month. I can barely sleep. The hip hasn't been injured but it's worse than my usual arthritis.
urgency_reason: Severe rest pain disproportionate to baseline arthritis in an elderly man raises concern for bone metastases or pathological fracture risk requiring urgent imaging.
recommended_action: Urgent X-ray and bone scan; PSA and full cancer screen; CBC and CRP; if metastases confirmed, urgent oncology and orthopedic review to assess prophylactic fixation risk; adequate pain control with morphine.
red_flags: Pathological fracture through the lesion
Spinal metastases with cord compression symptoms
Hypercalcemia from bone destruction causing confusion

user_symptom_description: My 7-year-old woke up with her eye completely sealed shut with yellow crust. The white of her eye is red and it keeps watering. It started in one eye yesterday and now both eyes look red.
urgency_reason: Purulent bilateral conjunctivitis in a child is usually bacterial and self-limiting but responds faster to topical antibiotics; school exclusion until discharge stops.
recommended_action: Topical chloramphenicol drops 4 times daily for 5 days; clean discharge with cool boiled water; separate towels; school exclusion until discharge resolves; conjunctival swab if no improvement at 48 hours.
red_flags: Severe eye pain or photophobia suggesting keratitis
Vision blurring not improving after discharge cleared
Unilateral proptosis suggesting periorbital cellulitis

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام عندي ألم شديد في أسفل ظهري بسبب إني رفعت حاجة تقيلة وبقدرش أستقيم. الألم مش بيشع في رجليا وبيروح شوية لو اتمددت.
extracted_symptoms: acute low back pain after lifting
urgency_reason: Acute mechanical back pain after a lifting injury without neurological features is almost always a muscle or ligament strain resolving with analgesia and early mobilization.
recommended_action: NSAIDs plus short course diazepam for muscle spasm; remain as active as possible; avoid bed rest beyond 2 days; physiotherapy referral if not improving at 6 weeks; no imaging needed at this stage.
red_flags: New leg weakness or foot drop
Bladder or bowel dysfunction
Constant night pain not relieved by any position

user_symptom_description: I've been having very heavy and irregular periods and my doctor found a 6cm fibroid in my uterus. I've been having pressure in my pelvis and needing to urinate more often. I'm 29 and want to have children.
urgency_reason: Symptomatic 6cm fibroid causing menorrhagia and bladder compression in a fertility-desiring young woman requires gynecological intervention planning to preserve future conception.
recommended_action: Gynecology referral; MRI pelvis for fibroid mapping; tranexamic acid during menstruation; discuss uterine artery embolization, myomectomy, or GnRH agonist; iron supplementation for anemia from blood loss.
red_flags: Fibroid growth during pregnancy causing complications
Rapid fibroid enlargement suggesting sarcomatous change
Hemoglobin below 8 g/dL requiring transfusion

user_symptom_description: عندي 55 سنة ومن 3 أيام بحس بألم حاد في بطني من فوق في المنتصف بيشع لظهري زي الحزام. الألم بيزيد لما باكل وبيقل لما بانحني للأمام. عندي سكر.
urgency_reason: Epigastric pain radiating to back with alimentary worsening and forward lean relief is classic pancreatitis requiring immediate serum amylase, lipase, and hospital admission.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; nil by mouth; serum amylase, lipase, LFTs, and CBC; CT abdomen with contrast if severe; IV fluids and analgesia; ERCP if gallstone pancreatitis; ICU if Ranson criteria met.
red_flags: Grey Turner or Cullen signs indicating hemorrhagic pancreatitis
Fever with rigors suggesting infected pancreatic necrosis
Hypotension and confusion indicating severe acute pancreatitis

user_symptom_description: I've had a persistent painless lump in my right groin for 2 months. It bulges more when I stand or cough and goes back in when I lie down. I occasionally feel uncomfortable dragging in the area.
urgency_reason: Femoral hernias in elderly women carry a high strangulation risk and should be surgically repaired electively before emergency presentation with bowel strangulation.
recommended_action: GP referral to general surgery for elective repair; groin ultrasound to confirm hernia type; advise to go to ER immediately if hernia becomes hard, red, painful, or irreducible.
red_flags: Sudden inability to reduce hernia indicating incarceration
Vomiting and complete constipation suggesting strangulated bowel obstruction
Skin redness and fever over the hernia

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 25 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين بصحي كل يوم بقلق شديد من إني هموت من مرض زي السرطان أو القلب. بفحص جسمي كل يوم وبدور على أعراض على الإنترنت وده بيزيد قلقي أكتر.
urgency_reason: Illness anxiety with compulsive checking and internet symptom searching is maintaining the cycle and requires CBT intervention rather than repeated medical investigations.
recommended_action: Refer for CBT focused on health anxiety; advise against symptom searching online; avoid repeated physical investigations which reinforce the cycle; SSRI if CBT alone insufficient; basic physical examination to provide genuine reassurance.
red_flags: Complete inability to function or leave home
Suicidal ideation from health-related despair
Emerging somatic delusions not responsive to CBT

user_symptom_description: My fingernails have been thickening, yellowing, and crumbling for the past 6 months — especially on my right hand. The nail lifts away from the nail bed and there's a bad smell.
urgency_reason: Fungal nail infection is chronic and non-urgent but requires laboratory confirmation before systemic antifungal therapy due to treatment duration and hepatotoxicity risk.
recommended_action: Nail clipping sent for mycology culture and PCR; if confirmed, oral terbinafine 250mg daily for 6 weeks (fingernails) or 12 weeks (toenails); check LFTs at baseline; topical amorolfine as adjunct.
red_flags: Signs of spreading skin tinea
Painful periungual inflammation suggesting bacterial superinfection
Diabetic patient with nail infection at high ulceration risk

user_symptom_description: عندي طفل عمره 9 سنين وبقاله أسبوعين بيتألم لما بيبول وبيروح الحمام كتير. الدكتور قاله ما فيش عدوى في البول. بيقول إن وجعه في رأس الأداة.
urgency_reason: Dysuria with frequency and negative culture in a boy may represent meatal stenosis causing obstructive urinary symptoms requiring urological examination.
recommended_action: Pediatric urology referral; uroflowmetry and post-void residual; examination of urethral meatus; meatotomy under local anesthesia if stenosis confirmed; topical steroid cream as first trial for mild cases.
red_flags: Thin or forceful spray urinary stream
Recurrent UTIs despite negative cultures
Renal tract dilation on ultrasound suggesting chronic obstruction

user_symptom_description: I've been having a lot of gas and bloating after eating bread, pasta, and anything with wheat for years. I also get cramping and loose stools within an hour of eating these foods. My brother has celiac disease.
urgency_reason: Gastrointestinal symptoms triggered by wheat with a first-degree relative with celiac disease warrants serological testing before instituting a gluten-free diet.
recommended_action: Do NOT start gluten-free diet before testing; anti-TTG IgA and total IgA blood test; if positive, gastroenterology referral for duodenal biopsy; DXA bone scan if confirmed celiac; dietitian referral.
red_flags: Weight loss and severe malabsorption
Dermatitis herpetiformis skin rash
Iron-deficiency anemia not responding to supplementation

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بحس بضعف في رجلي الأيسر وصعوبة في المشي. لما بيجيلي الضعف بييجي معاه تنميل في نفس الرجل. بقالي سنتين عندي مرض التصلب اللويحي.
possible_conditions: MS Pseudorelapse from Infection or Heat
urgency_reason: New neurological deficit lasting over 24 hours in an MS patient suggests a relapse requiring MRI and high-dose steroid treatment to shorten attack duration.
recommended_action: Contact neurology MS team urgently; exclude pseudorelapse by checking for infection, fever, and Uthoff's phenomenon; MRI brain and spine with gadolinium; IV methylprednisolone 1g daily for 3 days if true relapse.
red_flags: Rapid progression to paraplegia
Bowel or bladder dysfunction from spinal cord involvement
Fever suggesting an infection triggering pseudorelapse that needs treatment first

user_symptom_description: I was in the sun all day without a hat and now I have a severe headache, I feel nauseous, and my skin is hot and dry. I stopped sweating even though it's very hot. I feel confused and my friend says I look flushed.
urgency_reason: Hot dry skin with confusion and anhidrosis indicates classic heat stroke with core temperature likely above 40°C — a medical emergency with high mortality if cooling is delayed.
recommended_action: Call emergency services immediately; move to shade; remove excess clothing; apply ice packs to neck, armpits, and groin; fan with cold water spray; go to ER for core temperature monitoring, IV fluids, and organ function tests.
red_flags: Loss of consciousness or seizure
Core temperature above 41°C
Rhabdomyolysis with dark brown urine from muscle breakdown

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام عندي طفح أحمر متفرق على وجهي وصدري مع حمى وآلام في مفاصلي وأحسيت بتعب شديد بعد ما رحت حفلة بالخارج الأسبوع اللي فات.
urgency_reason: Rash with arthralgia and fever in a young woman following a social gathering warrants parvovirus serology and pregnancy test given teratogenic risk if pregnant.
recommended_action: GP appointment within 2 days; pregnancy test urgently; parvovirus B19 IgM; ANA and anti-dsDNA to exclude lupus; NSAIDs for joint pain; notify any pregnant contacts who may have been exposed.
red_flags: Pregnancy at any gestation — urgent fetal medicine referral
Aplastic crisis with Hb drop in sickle cell or chronic hemolytic anemia patient
Encephalitis with worsening headache and confusion

user_symptom_description: I've developed a tremor in my right hand that comes and goes, mainly when I'm using it — like when I'm writing or holding a cup. It's been getting worse over the past year. My father had the same thing.
urgency_reason: Action tremor worsened by intentional use with a positive family history is classic essential tremor, distinguishable from Parkinson's resting tremor on clinical examination.
recommended_action: Neurology appointment; propranolol 40mg twice daily as first-line; avoid caffeine and alcohol as triggers; primidone if propranolol contraindicated; DaTSCAN only if Parkinson's cannot be clinically excluded.
red_flags: Resting tremor developing alongside action tremor
Gait changes or rigidity emerging
Tremor becoming so severe it prevents eating or self-care

user_symptom_description: عندي 69 سنة وبقالي شهر بحس بألم وحرقة في رجليا وبيزيد لما بوقف أو بمشي وبيروح لو قعدت واتكأيت للأمام على ترولي التسوق. عندي ديسك في الضهر.
extracted_symptoms: relief with sitting and forward flexion
urgency_reason: Neurogenic claudication with postural relief is lumbar spinal stenosis requiring MRI for severity assessment and decompression planning.
recommended_action: MRI lumbar spine; physiotherapy focusing on flexion exercises; walking aids; epidural steroid injection for symptomatic relief; surgical decompression laminectomy if mobility severely limited.
red_flags: Sudden onset cauda equina symptoms with bladder dysfunction
Rapid leg weakness progression
Bilateral symptoms with perineal numbness requiring emergency MRI

user_symptom_description: I gave birth 6 weeks ago and my calf has been painful, red, and swollen for 2 days. When I walk it throbs. I'm still taking the combined pill I was on before pregnancy but forgot to stop after delivery.
urgency_reason: Postpartum DVT with high thrombotic risk from ongoing combined OCP use requires same-day anticoagulation to prevent fatal pulmonary embolism.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; stop combined pill now; leg Doppler ultrasound; start LMWH or apixaban if DVT confirmed; at least 3 months anticoagulation; thrombophilia screen before stopping treatment.
red_flags: Sudden chest pain or breathlessness indicating pulmonary embolism
Oxygen saturation drop
Bilateral leg swelling suggesting iliofemoral DVT extension

user_symptom_description: بنتي عمرها 3 سنين وبقالها يومين ما بتاكلش وبتوقع وبحسها تعبانة. طلع عليها بثور صغيرة حمرا على فمها وإيدها ورجلها.
extracted_symptoms: vesicular rash on mouth hands and feet
urgency_reason: Classic HFMD distribution with oral vesicles in a toddler is a self-limiting viral illness managed with supportive care and hydration.
recommended_action: Paracetamol for fever and pain; offer cold soft foods and fluids frequently; school and nursery exclusion until all blisters crusted; seek review if child not tolerating fluids or becomes drowsy.
red_flags: Inability to swallow any fluids suggesting severe dehydration
High fever persisting beyond 5 days
Neurological symptoms like neck stiffness or seizures — rare Enterovirus 71 complication

user_symptom_description: I've been feeling very low and hopeless for months. I've started drinking a bottle of vodka most days to cope and the alcohol is the only thing that makes me feel normal. I haven't been to work in 3 weeks.
possible_conditions: Comorbid Depression and Alcohol Use Disorder
primary_condition: Comorbid Depression and Alcohol Use Disorder
urgency_reason: Co-occurring depression with severe alcohol dependence significantly elevates suicide risk and requires urgent dual-diagnosis psychiatric and addiction assessment.
recommended_action: Urgent GP referral to dual-diagnosis addiction psychiatry; suicide risk assessment at first contact; supervised alcohol detox with chlordiazepoxide; thiamine IV; defer antidepressants until 4 weeks abstinence to separate diagnoses.
red_flags: Suicidal ideation or plan
Seizure from abrupt alcohol reduction
Liver failure symptoms like jaundice with confusion

user_symptom_description: بقالها أسبوع بتشكي من ألم فوق عينيها وبين عينيها بيزيد لما تتكأ للأمام، وعندها سيلان من أنفها وحرارة. كانت عندها زكام قوي قبليه بأسبوعين.
urgency_reason: Sinusitis persisting 10 days after a viral URI with fever suggests bacterial superinfection requiring antibiotic treatment to prevent orbital or intracranial spread.
recommended_action: Amoxicillin-clavulanate 625mg TDS for 7 days; saline nasal irrigation; nasal decongestant spray for 3 days only; analgesia; ENT referral if no improvement at 72 hours or periorbital swelling develops.
red_flags: Periorbital swelling or restricted eye movement suggesting orbital cellulitis
Severe frontal headache with neck stiffness
Vision changes indicating intracranial extension

user_symptom_description: I've had pain and stiffness in both shoulders and hips for 3 weeks. It's worst first thing in the morning and takes over an hour to loosen up. I feel generally unwell and my GP said my ESR is very high.
extracted_symptoms: bilateral shoulder and hip girdle stiffness
urgency_reason: Girdle stiffness with markedly elevated ESR in an elderly woman has a dramatic response to low-dose prednisolone confirming PMR; temporal arteritis must be excluded to prevent blindness.
recommended_action: Start prednisolone 15mg daily; ESR, CRP, and CK; ask specifically about jaw claudication and visual symptoms; temporal artery biopsy if giant cell arteritis suspected; bone protection with bisphosphonate.
red_flags: New headache, scalp tenderness, or jaw claudication suggesting GCA
Sudden visual loss requiring immediate high-dose steroids
Failure to respond to prednisolone challenging the diagnosis

user_symptom_description: عندي 38 سنة وبقالي شهر بحس بخفقان وقلبي بيدق بسرعة لو شربت قهوة أو كحول أو لما بكون متوتر. بيدوم دقيقتين وبيروح لوحده. ECG الأساسي طبيعي.
extracted_symptoms: palpitations with caffeine and alcohol triggers
urgency_reason: Short palpitations with clear lifestyle triggers and a normal resting ECG are most likely benign ectopic beats or sinus tachycardia, managed with trigger avoidance.
recommended_action: Reduce caffeine, alcohol, and energy drinks; 24-hour Holter monitor to capture symptoms; thyroid function tests; reassure if Holter shows only isolated ectopics; echocardiogram if structural heart disease suspected.
red_flags: Syncope during a palpitation episode
Palpitations lasting over 30 minutes
Family history of sudden cardiac death in a young person

user_symptom_description: My 14-year-old son has had severe acne for over a year. He's been to the GP twice and tried two topical creams with no improvement. He's started avoiding school and refuses to show his face. He seems very down.
possible_conditions: Severe Acne Vulgaris with Psychological Impact
primary_condition: Severe Acne Vulgaris with Psychological Impact
urgency_reason: Severe acne causing school avoidance and depressive features in an adolescent requires urgent dermatology referral for isotretinoin and simultaneous mental health assessment.
recommended_action: Urgent dermatology referral for isotretinoin candidacy; baseline LFTs, lipids, and pregnancy test for females; PHQ-A adolescent depression screen; school support plan; CBT referral for body image issues.
red_flags: Suicidal ideation linked to appearance distress
Acne fulminans with fever and joint pain
Rapid worsening in a male suggesting anabolic steroid use

user_symptom_description: بعد ما أكلت أكل بحري في مطعم من 4 ساعات بدأ يجيلي تقلصات في بطني وإسهال مائي شديد وقيء متكرر. أنا حاسة بضعف شديد ودوخة.
urgency_reason: Rapid-onset severe vomiting and diarrhea after eating seafood with significant dehydration symptoms requires oral or IV rehydration and monitoring for electrolyte imbalance.
recommended_action: Oral rehydration salts regularly; go to ER if unable to keep fluids down or dizziness worsens; stool culture if bloody diarrhea develops; antibiotics not indicated for typical food poisoning; rest and bland diet.
red_flags: Signs of severe dehydration: sunken eyes, dry mucous membranes, low urine output
High fever with bloody diarrhea suggesting Vibrio or Salmonella
Numbness or tingling around mouth suggesting scombroid or neurotoxic shellfish poisoning

user_symptom_description: My 83-year-old father has been increasingly short of breath over 4 weeks and has swollen legs up to his knees. He sleeps with 3 pillows now because lying flat makes his breathing worse. He has a known weak heart.
urgency_reason: Worsening heart failure with orthopnea and bilateral leg edema to knees requires same-day medical review to adjust diuresis and prevent acute pulmonary edema.
recommended_action: Go to urgent care or ER today; BNP, renal function, and chest X-ray; increase furosemide dose or add IV furosemide; daily weight monitoring; echocardiogram; review all medications including NSAIDs and calcium channel blockers.
red_flags: Oxygen saturation below 88%
Pink frothy sputum from acute pulmonary edema
Rapid clinical deterioration requiring BiPAP or IV inotropes

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع بحس بألم في كعبي الأيمن بيزيد خطواتي الأولى الصبح لما بقوم من النوم وبعد ما بقعد فترة. بقيت أتمرن كتير مؤخراً.
extracted_symptoms: heel pain worst with first steps in morning
urgency_reason: First-step morning heel pain in an active young adult after increased training is classic plantar fasciitis manageable with stretching and footwear modification.
recommended_action: Calf stretching and plantar fascia stretching before getting out of bed; supportive footwear with heel cushion; reduce training volume; night splint if pain persists; physiotherapy; steroid injection if no improvement at 3 months.
red_flags: Constant severe pain at rest suggesting stress fracture
Foot swelling with bruising after a specific incident
Numbness along the arch suggesting tarsal tunnel syndrome

user_symptom_description: I've been experiencing explosive outbursts of rage over small things for the past year. Last week I punched a wall at work and my colleagues are afraid of me. I feel shame afterward and can't explain why I do it.
urgency_reason: Recurrent explosive aggression with occupational consequences requires psychiatric assessment to exclude bipolar disorder, PTSD, or organic cause before starting anger-focused CBT.
recommended_action: Urgent psychiatry referral; MRI brain to exclude frontal organic cause; trauma history assessment for PTSD; CBT anger management; consider fluoxetine or valproate; occupational health involvement.
red_flags: Violence directed at people rather than objects
Legal consequences from outburst
Neurological symptoms suggesting frontal lobe lesion

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 76 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين بقشعريرة وحمى وكحول جاف وأحسيت بضيق في نفسي. الأشعة اللي عملتها عند الدكتور بينت تكثيف في رئتي الأيمن.
urgency_reason: Lobar consolidation with fever and dyspnea in an elderly woman requires CURB-65 scoring and likely hospital admission for IV antibiotics and respiratory monitoring.
recommended_action: Go to ER for CURB-65 scoring; blood cultures and sputum culture; IV co-amoxiclav plus clarithromycin if admitted; oxygen supplementation to maintain sat above 94%; repeat X-ray at 6 weeks to confirm resolution.
red_flags: Oxygen saturation below 90% requiring supplemental oxygen
Pleural effusion on X-ray needing diagnostic tap
Failure to improve at 48 hours requiring CT to exclude malignancy

user_symptom_description: I have lupus and over the past week I've developed a painful red rash on my cheeks and nose, joint pain in my hands, and I'm exhausted. My urine also looks frothy lately.
urgency_reason: Foamy urine indicating proteinuria during an SLE flare suggests lupus nephritis, which can progress to renal failure without prompt escalation of immunosuppression.
recommended_action: Contact rheumatology team urgently; urinalysis with protein:creatinine ratio and 24-hour urine protein; anti-dsDNA and complement levels; renal biopsy if nephritis confirmed; escalate to high-dose steroids and mycophenolate.
red_flags: Creatinine rising suggesting active renal injury
Hematuria with casts in urine
CNS symptoms such as seizures indicating neuropsychiatric lupus

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بيجيلي صداع خلف رأسي لما بنظر لفوق أو بقلب رأسي بسرعة، وبحس بدوخة للحظات. الصداع بيروح لوحده.
extracted_symptoms: triggered by neck extension and rotation
urgency_reason: Positional headache linked to neck movement without neurological deficit is most likely cervicogenic, but vertebrobasilar TIA must be considered given the dizziness.
recommended_action: Neurology appointment; carotid and vertebral Doppler; MRI brain and cervical spine; physiotherapy for cervical facet joint mobilization; consider greater occipital nerve block if occipital neuralgia suspected.
red_flags: Double vision or slurred speech with the dizziness
Drop attacks with postural change
Dysphagia suggesting posterior fossa or brainstem involvement

user_symptom_description: My newborn is 4 days old and I've noticed the whites of his eyes and his skin have turned yellow today. He's breastfed but I'm not sure if he's getting enough milk and he seems sleepy all the time.
urgency_reason: Neonatal jaundice at day 4 with lethargy requires same-day transcutaneous bilirubin measurement to assess for pathological hyperbilirubinemia and phototherapy threshold.
recommended_action: Go to pediatrician or maternity unit today; transcutaneous bilirubin or serum bilirubin; assess for adequate breastfeeding and wet nappies; lactation consultant review; phototherapy if above treatment line on bilirubin chart.
red_flags: Bilirubin above phototherapy threshold on the hour-specific chart
Extreme lethargy or high-pitched cry suggesting kernicterus risk
Jaundice appearing before 24 hours of age suggesting hemolytic disease

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر بحس بألم في بطني وعندي إمساك شديد من أكتر من أسبوعين. لما بروح الحمام البراز بيكون رفيع جداً. وزني نزل 5 كيلو من غير ما أحاول.
urgency_reason: Pencil-thin stools with weight loss and new constipation meets urgent cancer pathway criteria for colorectal malignancy causing luminal obstruction.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week cancer pathway colonoscopy; CEA, CBC, and iron studies; CT abdomen-pelvis; surgical oncology referral if mass confirmed; bowel preparation for colonoscopy only under specialist guidance.
red_flags: Complete bowel obstruction with distension and vomiting
Rectal bleeding alongside these symptoms
Abdominal mass palpable on examination

user_symptom_description: My son brought me in because I've had a high fever for 4 days, confusion, and a stiff neck that started yesterday. I'm 78 and have never had anything like this. I vomited twice this morning.
urgency_reason: Fever, neck stiffness, and confusion in an elderly patient is bacterial meningitis until excluded — treatment delay increases mortality dramatically.
recommended_action: Call emergency services immediately; go to ER for CT brain before lumbar puncture; start IV ceftriaxone plus dexamethasone and IV acyclovir without waiting if delayed; blood cultures first; monitor airway.
red_flags: Worsening consciousness level
Non-blanching petechial rash
Focal neurological deficit suggesting brain abscess or herpes encephalitis

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أسابيع بيطلع لي طفح أحمر حول خاتم خطوبتي. الجلد تحت الخاتم بيتقشر وبيحكني وبيتورم أحياناً. لما بشيل الخاتم بيتحسن.
extracted_symptoms: clear trigger removal leads to improvement
urgency_reason: Localized eczematous reaction under a metal ring resolving with removal is classic nickel contact dermatitis, managed by allergen avoidance and topical steroids.
recommended_action: Remove ring until skin heals; apply topical hydrocortisone 1% twice daily for 2 weeks; switch to platinum, titanium, or 18-carat gold ring; patch testing at dermatology to confirm nickel allergy.
red_flags: Spreading rash beyond ring contact area
Blistering or weeping suggesting severe acute dermatitis
Secondary infection with increased warmth and pus

user_symptom_description: I've been having intermittent bloody urine with no pain for the past month. I'm 50 years old, I smoke, and I work in a rubber factory. I've had two episodes where the urine was completely red.
urgency_reason: Painless macroscopic hematuria in a male smoker with industrial chemical exposure is bladder cancer until proven otherwise — cystoscopy is mandatory.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week cancer referral to urology; flexible cystoscopy; CT urogram; urine cytology; CBC and renal function; smoking cessation reinforcement; transurethral resection if bladder tumor confirmed.
red_flags: Clot retention causing urinary obstruction
Pelvic mass on examination
Bone pain suggesting metastatic disease

user_symptom_description: بنتي عمرها 2 سنة وبقالها يومين بتلف في أوضتها مش قادرة توقف وإيدها الشمال بتتعبها. صحيت الصبح إيدها مرفوعة ومش بتحركها. مفيش حمى.
extracted_symptoms: inability to move arm on waking
urgency_reason: Acute focal limb weakness in a toddler without fever or injury is childhood arterial ischemic stroke until proven otherwise, requiring emergency MRI and pediatric neurology.
recommended_action: Call emergency services immediately; go to pediatric ER for urgent MRI DWI brain and MRA; echocardiogram and thrombophilia screen; hematology for sickle cell screen; aspirin after hemorrhage excluded.
red_flags: Seizure with the arm weakness
Altered consciousness
Both limbs affected or gaze deviation suggesting large vessel stroke

user_symptom_description: I've been feeling a lump in my neck that I can feel when I swallow — it moves up and down as I swallow. It's been there for 3 months and has slowly grown. I don't have pain but I feel like something is pressing on my throat.
urgency_reason: A growing thyroid nodule with compressive symptoms requires urgent ultrasound and fine-needle aspiration cytology to exclude malignancy.
recommended_action: Urgent thyroid ultrasound; FNAC of dominant nodule; TSH, free T4, and thyroglobulin; ENT or endocrine surgery referral if FNAC suspicious; vocal cord assessment if voice changes present.
red_flags: Hoarseness of voice from recurrent laryngeal nerve involvement
Dysphagia or stridor from rapid growth
Hard fixed nodule with cervical lymphadenopathy suggesting carcinoma

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 30 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين بحس بوخز وتنميل في إيديا الإتنين وقدميا مع ضعف في رجليا وبعد ما كان عندي عدوى في المعدة قبل 3 أسابيع.
urgency_reason: Ascending bilateral weakness with paresthesia following a GI infection is Guillain-Barré syndrome until proven otherwise; respiratory failure can develop within hours.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; nerve conduction studies and lumbar puncture showing albuminocytological dissociation; admit to neurology ward; serial FVC monitoring for respiratory failure; IVIG or plasmapheresis.
red_flags: FVC below 20ml/kg requiring intubation
Dysautonomia with BP swings and arrhythmia
Facial weakness or dysphagia suggesting severe ascending involvement

user_symptom_description: I've had a large, open sore on my left ankle for 3 months that won't heal. My leg is swollen and the skin around it is brown and leathery. I've had varicose veins for years and my legs ache at the end of the day.
urgency_reason: Chronic venous ulcer requires multidisciplinary wound care with compression bandaging after ABI to exclude arterial disease before compression is applied.
recommended_action: Ankle-brachial index measurement before compression; if ABI above 0.8, start 4-layer compression bandaging; wound swab; refer to tissue viability nurse; consider pentoxifylline; venous duplex for surgical treatment planning.
red_flags: ABI below 0.8 contraindicating compression
Rapidly spreading cellulitis around the ulcer
Wound probing to bone suggesting osteomyelitis

user_symptom_description: عندي 47 سنة وبقالي شهرين بحس بتعب وإرهاق غير طبيعي وبلاقي كدمات سهلة على جسمي من غير سبب ونزيف لثتي لما باتفرش. وزني نزل كمان.
urgency_reason: Easy bruising with spontaneous bleeding and weight loss requires urgent FBC to exclude acute leukemia, which can present with thrombocytopenia from marrow infiltration.
recommended_action: Urgent FBC with differential and blood film today; coagulation screen; LFTs; if blast cells or severe thrombocytopenia, refer immediately to hematology; bone marrow biopsy if leukemia suspected.
red_flags: Platelet count below 20 with active bleeding
Fever with neutropenia suggesting sepsis in a leukemic patient
CNS symptoms suggesting leukemic infiltration

user_symptom_description: I've had a very painful mouth for the past week with multiple blisters inside on my gums and cheeks. My lips are crusted and cracked and I can barely eat or drink. I also have a fever and I feel very unwell.
urgency_reason: Severe primary herpes stomatitis with inability to maintain oral hydration requires antiviral treatment and assessment for IV fluids to prevent dehydration.
recommended_action: Go to GP or urgent care today; start oral acyclovir 400mg 5 times daily for 7 days within 72 hours; IV fluids if unable to drink; chlorhexidine mouthwash; lidocaine gel before eating; isolate from immunocompromised contacts.
red_flags: Signs of dehydration requiring hospital admission for IV fluids
Eye involvement suggesting herpetic keratitis
Skin blistering beyond mouth area raising concern for SJS

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر بحس بألم في رأس قضيبي وبيصعب عليا أسحب الجلدة لخلف. مش متألم في الراحة بس بيزيد لما بحاول أتنظف وعند العلاقة الجنسية.
urgency_reason: Symptomatic phimosis impairing hygiene and sexual function warrants urological assessment to determine if topical steroid stretching or circumcision is appropriate.
recommended_action: Urology referral; trial of betamethasone 0.05% cream twice daily with gentle stretching for 6-8 weeks; if lichen sclerosus confirmed, circumcision preferred; exclude diabetes as predisposing cause.
red_flags: Paraphimosis — retracted foreskin that cannot be reduced forward — this is an emergency
Penile ulceration or white plaques suggesting lichen sclerosus with malignant transformation risk
Difficulty passing urine from severe meatal stenosis

user_symptom_description: My 81-year-old mother has been taking about 11 different medications and recently started getting very confused, falling, and has become very unsteady on her feet. Her new doctor thinks her medications might be the problem.
urgency_reason: Polypharmacy with new delirium and falls in an elderly woman requires urgent medication review and deprescribing to prevent further fall injuries and cognitive deterioration.
recommended_action: Urgent GP medication review using Beers criteria; stop or taper benzodiazepines, anticholinergics, and unnecessary medications; check electrolytes and renal function; falls assessment and physiotherapy; caregiver education.
red_flags: Fall with head injury requiring CT
Extreme confusion with aspiration risk
Anticholinergic syndrome with urinary retention, dry mouth, fever

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بكاء وحزن شديد وما قدرتش أنام وما عندي نفس أكل. بعد ما فضلت حامل 8 أسابيع وخسرت الحمل مفاجأة. أنا حاسة بذنب شديد وخواجة إني مش قادرة أعدي في يومي.
urgency_reason: Intense grief after miscarriage with functional impairment at 2 weeks warrants clinical assessment to monitor for progression to major depression requiring treatment.
recommended_action: GP appointment within 1 week; validate and normalize grief; provide miscarriage support resources; assess for depressive episode with PHQ-9; refer to perinatal bereavement counseling; follow up at 4 and 8 weeks.
red_flags: Suicidal ideation
Complete inability to eat or care for herself
Psychotic symptoms such as believing the pregnancy is still ongoing

user_symptom_description: I was hiking and got hit on the head by a falling branch. I was briefly unconscious for about a minute. I have a headache now and felt confused right after but seem OK. That was 3 hours ago.
urgency_reason: Loss of consciousness after head trauma requires CT head to exclude intracranial hemorrhage, particularly extradural hematoma which can deteriorate after a lucid interval.
recommended_action: Go to ER for CT head; observe for 4 hours after scan; concussion education if CT normal; no alcohol, driving, or strenuous activity for 1 week; discharge with head injury advice card; return if headache worsens suddenly.
red_flags: Deteriorating consciousness or GCS drop — lucid interval followed by drowsiness is a neurosurgical emergency
Unequal pupils
Repeated vomiting more than 3 times after injury

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أشهر بلعابي بيزيد وبيصعب عليا ابتلعه وبيجيلي تشنجات في إيديا ووجهي من تلقاءهم. في ناس في العيلة عندهم نفس المشكلة من صغرهم.
urgency_reason: Neurological symptoms with involuntary movements in a young adult with family history warrants copper metabolism testing and slit-lamp examination to exclude Wilson's disease before irreversible liver and brain damage.
recommended_action: Urgent neurology referral; serum ceruloplasmin, 24-hour urine copper, and LFTs; slit-lamp examination for Kayser-Fleischer rings; MRI brain; liver biopsy if ceruloplasmin low; start D-penicillamine if confirmed.
red_flags: Acute liver failure presentation
Psychiatric symptoms emerging with neurological ones
Hemolytic anemia accompanying the neurological features

user_symptom_description: I've had severe pain in my tailbone area for 2 weeks since I fell hard on a hard floor while ice skating. The pain is excruciating when I sit and slightly better when I stand or lie down.
urgency_reason: Coccyx injury after a direct impact with positional pain is almost certainly a contusion or fracture, managed conservatively as surgical intervention is rarely needed.
recommended_action: Lateral coccyx X-ray; donut-shaped coccyx cushion for sitting; NSAIDs for 7-10 days; most coccyx injuries resolve in 4-12 weeks; steroid injection into coccygeal joint if pain persists at 6 weeks.
red_flags: Bowel or bladder dysfunction suggesting sacral nerve involvement
Inability to walk at all
Swelling and redness suggesting abscess rather than fracture

user_symptom_description: عندي 77 سنة وبقالي أسبوع بسمع صوت ويز في أذني اليمين طول الوقت وبحس بإن أذني بتحس بثقل. فجأة سمعي في الأذن دي نزل بشكل واضح.
urgency_reason: Sudden sensorineural hearing loss is a time-sensitive emergency — high-dose oral steroids within 72 hours of onset dramatically improve recovery rates.
