red_flags: Rapid cognitive decline suggesting Lewy body dementia requiring medication precautions against antipsychotics
Autonomic failure with hypotension and syncope
Dementia onset before motor symptoms

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 21 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين بحس بمزاج عالي جداً، مش محتاج نوم خالص، بتكلم بسرعة كبيرة وبفكر في مشاريع كتير في نفس الوقت. صرفت كل مصروفي في أسبوع على حاجات مش محتاجها. أهلي قلقانين عليا.
urgency_reason: Full manic episode with impulsive spending, decreased sleep, and pressured speech in a young adult requires urgent psychiatric assessment as mania carries risk of financial ruin, dangerous behavior, and psychosis.
recommended_action: Same-day psychiatric evaluation; toxicology screen to exclude substance-induced mania; thyroid function tests; start lithium carbonate or valproate for mood stabilization; olanzapine if psychotic features or severe agitation; benzodiazepine for sleep; hospitalization if insight poor or safety risk; family psychoeducation; do not give antidepressant alone as it can worsen cycling.
red_flags: Psychotic features with grandiose delusions requiring immediate antipsychotic
Self-harm or aggression toward others requiring inpatient admission
Lack of insight with refusal of treatment requiring mental health legislation

user_symptom_description: I'm 43 and for three months I've had spots and breakouts mostly along my jawline and chin that are deep, painful, and cystic. They don't respond to over-the-counter creams. I also have irregular periods and noticed some hair on my upper lip and chin. I've put on weight around my middle.
extracted_symptoms: facial hirsutism upper lip and chin
urgency_reason: Hormonal acne with hirsutism, menstrual irregularity, and central obesity in a middle-aged woman fulfils Rotterdam criteria for PCOS requiring hormonal workup and multidisciplinary management.
recommended_action: Total and free testosterone, DHEAS, LH, FSH ratio, 17-OHP to exclude CAH; fasting glucose and insulin; pelvic ultrasound for polycystic ovaries; combined oral contraceptive for acne and menstrual regulation; spironolactone 50-100mg for hirsutism; metformin for insulin resistance; dermatology referral for isotretinoin if COCP insufficient for acne; weight loss counseling.
red_flags: Rapidly progressive virilization with very high testosterone suggesting ovarian tumor
Cushing's features with moon face and striae requiring dexamethasone suppression test
Infertility requiring ovulation induction with clomifene

user_symptom_description: عندي 72 سنة وبقالي 3 أيام بحس بألم حاد ومستمر في بطني من الجنب الأيسر في الأسفل. عندي حمى 38.5 وكمان إمساك. الألم بيزيد بالضغط على المنطقة دي. عندي تاريخ مع أكياس في القولون.
urgency_reason: Left iliac fossa pain with fever and localized peritonism in an elderly woman with known diverticular disease is acute diverticulitis requiring CT abdomen to grade severity and guide antibiotic or surgical management.
recommended_action: CT abdomen and pelvis with contrast urgently; CBC and CRP; urine culture to exclude UTI; modified Hinchey classification to guide management; oral antibiotics amoxicillin-clavulanate for uncomplicated Hinchey Ia; admit for IV antibiotics if Hinchey Ib or above; liquid diet during acute phase; colonoscopy 6-8 weeks after resolution to exclude colorectal cancer.
red_flags: Peritoneal signs suggesting perforated diverticulitis requiring emergency laparotomy
CT showing free air or pus suggesting Hinchey III or IV requiring surgery
Fistula formation to bladder showing pneumaturia or fecaluria

user_symptom_description: I'm 29 and I've been feeling really sad and low for the past 6 months. I used to love going to the gym but I've completely stopped. I sleep 11-12 hours a day and still feel tired. I've been eating a lot more than usual and have gained weight. I can't enjoy anything anymore.
possible_conditions: Major Depressive Disorder with Atypical Features
primary_condition: Major Depressive Disorder with Atypical Features
urgency_reason: Six-month major depression with atypical features of hypersomnia and hyperphagia requires antidepressant therapy and psychological treatment as atypical depression responds better to MAOIs or SSRIs than TCAs.
recommended_action: PHQ-9 and bipolar screening with MDQ; TSH and CBC to exclude organic cause; SSRI such as sertraline 50mg titrating to 100-200mg; CBT or behavioural activation therapy; structured exercise program; light therapy if seasonal pattern; check for bipolar II before antidepressant prescription to avoid cycling; review at 2 and 4 weeks.
red_flags: Suicidal ideation requiring immediate risk assessment
Mood episodes of elevated energy in past suggesting bipolar II
Psychotic symptoms with nihilistic delusions suggesting psychotic depression

user_symptom_description: ابني عنده 18 شهر وبقاله يومين عنده حرارة 40 درجة. الأمبارح بدأت جسمه بيرتجف لمدة دقيقتين وعينيه بقت بيضاء. وقفت لوحدها بس دوحتني. ما وقعش وكان صاحي بعدها.
urgency_reason: First febrile seizure in a toddler requires emergency assessment to exclude bacterial meningitis and provide parental education on management of recurrence, even though simple febrile seizures are benign.
recommended_action: Emergency pediatric assessment; check glucose immediately; lumbar puncture if meningism, prolonged seizure, or age below 18 months; CBC, CRP, urine culture, and blood culture; identify and treat source of fever; parental education on recovery position and buccal midazolam for seizures lasting above 5 minutes; reassure that risk of epilepsy is only 2 to 3 percent; follow-up in 48 hours.
red_flags: Seizure lasting more than 15 minutes suggesting complex febrile seizure or status epilepticus
Focal seizure suggesting structural brain pathology
Neck stiffness or petechial rash requiring immediate lumbar puncture and antibiotics

user_symptom_description: I'm 36 and I developed a very painful blistering rash on my right side that goes from my back around to my abdomen, following a band-like strip. It started as tingling 4 days ago and now the blisters are fully formed. The pain is burning and severe even at rest.
urgency_reason: Active herpes zoster within 72 hours of rash onset requires antiviral therapy to reduce viral replication, shorten episode duration, and most importantly prevent postherpetic neuralgia.
recommended_action: Start oral valacyclovir 1000mg three times daily or famciclovir for 7 days if within 72 hours of rash onset; amitriptyline 10-25mg at night for neuropathic pain prevention; consider short course prednisolone in severe cases; calamine lotion for lesions; avoid contact with pregnant women, neonates, and immunocompromised individuals; shingles vaccine for future prevention.
red_flags: Periocular involvement suggesting herpes zoster ophthalmicus requiring urgent ophthalmology
Severe immunocompromise requiring IV acyclovir
Ramsay Hunt syndrome with facial palsy and ear involvement

user_symptom_description: عندي 67 سنة وبقالي شهرين بلاحظ إن أنا بتبول بتعب، البول بيجي بطيء وعندي تدفق ضعيف، وبيوقف ويرجع أكتر من مرة. بصحى 4 مرات الليل. أحياناً بحس إن المثانة مش بتفضي خالص.
urgency_reason: Lower urinary tract symptoms with high nocturia and incomplete emptying in an elderly man require PSA, DRE, and post-void residual measurement to exclude prostate cancer and assess obstruction severity.
recommended_action: IPSS symptom score; PSA and digital rectal examination; post-void residual urine by ultrasound; urine flow rate; renal ultrasound; if post-void residual above 300ml start alpha-blocker tamsulosin 0.4mg; 5-alpha-reductase inhibitor finasteride if prostate volume large; TURP if medical treatment fails; biopsy if PSA elevated and DRE abnormal.
red_flags: Acute urinary retention requiring emergency catheterization
Bilateral hydronephrosis suggesting chronic retention with renal failure
PSA above 10 with hard nodule on DRE requiring urgent biopsy

user_symptom_description: I'm 23 and last night after dinner I suddenly had the worst pain I've ever felt in my lower right abdomen. It came on so quickly. I feel sick and I've vomited once. When I press on my right side and let go quickly, the pain is much worse. I have a fever of 38.8.
extracted_symptoms: sudden onset severe right iliac fossa pain
urgency_reason: Acute RIF pain with rebound tenderness, fever, and vomiting in a young woman is acute appendicitis with risk of perforation within 24 to 72 hours requiring emergency surgical consultation.
recommended_action: Emergency assessment; urine pregnancy test immediately to exclude ectopic; CBC for leukocytosis and CRP; CT abdomen pelvis or ultrasound if pregnant; Alvarado score calculation; keep nil by mouth; IV access and analgesia; urgent general surgery review for emergency laparoscopic appendicectomy; IV antibiotics as adjunct or primary in uncomplicated cases in selected patients.
red_flags: Positive pregnancy test suggesting ectopic requiring immediate gynecology
Rigid abdomen suggesting perforation with peritonitis requiring immediate surgery
Hemodynamic instability suggesting ruptured ectopic or perforated appendix

user_symptom_description: بقالي 4 أيام بحس بصداع خلف عيني الاتنين وحمى 38.5 وآلام في جسمي كله. أحساسي بالريحة والطعم اتأثر كتير. عندي سعال جاف وتعبان من أي مجهود بسيط. بدأت الأعراض بعد رحلة عمل.
urgency_reason: Anosmia with fever, dry cough, and post-travel myalgia is highly specific for COVID-19 requiring isolation, PCR confirmation, and monitoring for hypoxia development.
recommended_action: SARS-CoV-2 PCR or rapid antigen test; pulse oximetry monitoring at home; home isolation for 5 days from symptom onset; paracetamol for fever and myalgia; adequate hydration; daily oxygen saturation monitoring with alert to seek emergency care if below 94 percent; antiviral nirmatrelvir-ritonavir if high risk patient within 5 days of symptom onset.
red_flags: Oxygen saturation below 94 percent on room air requiring emergency assessment
Chest pain with tachycardia suggesting pulmonary embolism as COVID complication
Altered consciousness suggesting COVID encephalopathy

user_symptom_description: I'm 77 and I've had a sore that won't heal on my lower lip for three months. It started as a small lump and now it's got a raised hard edge. I smoked for 40 years and I've spent a lot of time in the sun working in the garden for decades. It doesn't hurt.
urgency_reason: A non-healing indurated lower lip lesion in an elderly patient with significant UV and tobacco exposure is lip SCC until proven otherwise requiring urgent biopsy as lip SCC has 10 percent metastatic risk to cervical nodes.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week wait dermatology and maxillofacial referral; incisional biopsy for histology; CT neck and chest for nodal staging; wide local excision with 5-10mm margins if SCC confirmed; sentinel lymph node biopsy; radiotherapy if positive nodes or margins; sun protection advice; smoking cessation; oral cancer screening for synchronous lesions.
red_flags: Palpable cervical lymphadenopathy suggesting nodal metastasis
Fixation to underlying structures suggesting deep invasion
Rapid increase in size suggesting aggressive histology

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 28 سنة وبحس بعدم ارتياح وتضايق مستمر من أسبوعين. بخاف من مواقف اجتماعية وبتجنبها. بحس إن الناس بتحكم عليا وبضايقني لما بكون في جمع. بدأت أترك الشغل من وقت لوقت من الخجل.
urgency_reason: Social anxiety causing occupational avoidance requires CBT and SSRI intervention to prevent progressive disability and social isolation but is not immediately medically dangerous.
recommended_action: Liebowitz Social Anxiety Scale assessment; CBT with social exposure hierarchy as first-line treatment; SSRI sertraline 50mg titrating to 200mg as pharmacological adjunct; avoid benzodiazepines for chronic anxiety; social skills training group; occupational therapy for workplace reintegration; mindfulness-based stress reduction; review at 8 weeks.
red_flags: Suicidal ideation linked to shame and social failure
Complete social isolation and inability to leave home
Substance use as self-medication for anxiety

user_symptom_description: I'm 41 and I've had increasing difficulty swallowing solids for four months. It started with just bread and meat but now I struggle with soft food too. I've lost 6kg. The food feels like it stops in the middle of my chest. I've never had heartburn.
extracted_symptoms: progressive dysphagia solids then soft food
urgency_reason: Progressive dysphagia with significant weight loss is esophageal cancer until proven otherwise and requires urgent upper GI endoscopy with biopsy as early-stage disease is potentially curable.
recommended_action: Urgent upper GI endoscopy with multiple biopsies within 2 weeks; if malignancy confirmed CT chest abdomen pelvis for staging; PET-CT and EUS for T and N staging; nutritional support with NG feeding if unable to maintain weight; MDT discussion involving upper GI surgery, oncology, and dietetics; curative surgery or chemoradiotherapy depending on stage.
red_flags: Complete dysphagia to fluids suggesting near-total obstruction requiring stenting
Aspiration pneumonia from food overspill
Tracheoesophageal fistula with coughing on swallowing

user_symptom_description: عندي 69 سنة وبقالي 3 أشهر بلاحظ إن الكلام بتاعي بقى أبطأ وصوتي بقى أخفت وأحياناً بيطلع من تلقاء نفسه كلمات مش قاصدها. بدأت كمان أتعثر في الأكل وبيراق عليا من الأكل أحياناً.
urgency_reason: Bulbar symptoms of dysarthria, dysphagia, and drooling in an elderly man require urgent neurological assessment to establish diagnosis as MND and PSP have rapid progression and affect respiratory function.
recommended_action: Urgent neurology referral; MRI brain and brainstem; nerve conduction studies and EMG to exclude MND; VFSS swallowing assessment to guide safe feeding; speech and language therapy immediately for communication and swallowing; adjust levodopa if Parkinson's confirmed; PEG consideration early if MND confirmed; carer assessment and advance care planning.
red_flags: Respiratory muscle weakness with FVC declining suggesting MND requiring ventilatory support
Complete dysphagia requiring NG feeding or PEG
Cognitive decline with psychosis suggesting DLB

user_symptom_description: I'm 27 and I've been having joint pain and swelling in my fingers, wrists, and knees for two months. I also get a red rash across my cheeks and nose that gets worse in the sun. I'm exhausted all the time and my hair has been falling out a lot.
urgency_reason: Malar rash with photosensitivity, polyarthritis, hair loss, and fatigue in a young woman fulfils ACR/EULAR criteria for SLE requiring urgent renal function assessment as lupus nephritis occurs in 50 percent of patients.
recommended_action: ANA, anti-dsDNA, anti-Sm, complement C3 and C4, antiphospholipid antibodies; urinalysis for hematuria and proteinuria; urine albumin-creatinine ratio; CBC for cytopenias; start hydroxychloroquine 200-400mg as baseline therapy; sun protection SPF 50 and protective clothing; corticosteroids for acute flare; renal biopsy if proteinuria above 500mg daily; rheumatology follow-up every 3 months.
red_flags: Proteinuria above 500mg/day or cellular casts suggesting lupus nephritis requiring urgent biopsy
Pleuritis or pericarditis suggesting serositis requiring systemic steroids
Neuropsychiatric symptoms suggesting CNS lupus requiring MRI and aggressive immunosuppression

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع بحس بألم في صدري من الجنب الأيسر بيزيد لما بتنفس عميق أو بكعس. الألم بدأ بعد ما كنت عيان بزكام قبلها بأسبوع. مفيش ضيق في التنفس بس الألم بيأذيني جداً.
extracted_symptoms: worsened by deep breathing and coughing
preceded by viral upper respiratory tract infection
urgency_reason: Post-viral pleuritic chest pain requires clinical and radiological assessment to exclude pulmonary embolism and pneumonia before attributing to benign viral pleuritis.
recommended_action: Chest X-ray; D-dimer if Wells score suggests PE risk; ECG; CBC and CRP; NSAIDs such as ibuprofen 400mg three times daily with food for 2 weeks; if pleuritis confirmed and improving no further investigation needed; CT pulmonary angiography if D-dimer positive; repeat X-ray at 6 weeks if abnormality on initial film.
red_flags: Dyspnea or tachycardia suggesting pulmonary embolism
Fever with dullness to percussion suggesting pleural effusion from empyema
Hemoptysis requiring urgent investigation

user_symptom_description: My 11-year-old has been complaining that everything looks blurry at the whiteboard at school for the last few months. She sits in the front row now and still squints. She has no headaches or eye pain. Her father and I both wear glasses for the same problem.
urgency_reason: Progressive myopia in a school-age child requires refraction and prescription spectacles to optimize learning and may benefit from myopia control interventions to reduce progression rate.
recommended_action: Optometrist refraction with cycloplegic drops for accurate measurement in children; prescription spectacles or contact lenses; discuss myopia control options including atropine 0.01 percent nightly eye drops or orthokeratology lenses; limit screen time and encourage outdoor activity 2 hours daily; annual review for progression monitoring.
red_flags: Unilateral myopia with large asymmetry suggesting amblyopia requiring occlusion therapy
Floaters or flashing lights in a highly myopic child suggesting retinal tear
Visual acuity not fully correctable with glasses suggesting amblyopia or structural pathology

user_symptom_description: بقالي 4 أيام بشوف وميض ونجوم كتيرة فجأة في عيني الأيمن، ومن امبارح بقيت أشوف ستارة سودا من تحت بتتحرك لفوق في رؤيتي. مفيش ألم. عيني شديدة القصر من صغري.
urgency_reason: Visual field loss described as an ascending curtain following photopsia in a highly myopic patient is rhegmatogenous retinal detachment threatening macular vision and requiring emergency surgical repair within hours.
recommended_action: Emergency ophthalmology assessment same day; fundoscopy and slit-lamp examination; B-scan ultrasound if media opaque; pneumatic retinopexy or scleral buckle or vitrectomy urgently; if macula still attached surgery within 24 hours gives 90 percent visual recovery; if macula off surgery within 24-72 hours; strict nil by mouth for surgery; do not delay.
red_flags: Macular involvement giving central visual loss requiring immediate surgery
Bilateral detachment in Marfan syndrome or trauma
Complete loss of light perception suggesting advanced detachment with poor prognosis

user_symptom_description: I'm 71 and I've been having blood in my stool for 6 weeks. The blood is dark and mixed in with the stool, not just on the surface. I've lost about 5kg and I feel wiped out all the time. My bowel habit has changed and I now have looser stools more frequently.
extracted_symptoms: change in bowel habit to looser stools
urgency_reason: Dark rectal bleeding mixed in stool with change in bowel habit and weight loss in an elderly man is colorectal cancer until proven otherwise requiring urgent colonoscopy as part of the 2-week wait cancer pathway.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week wait colonoscopy with biopsy; FBC for anemia; CEA tumor marker; CT chest abdomen pelvis for staging if cancer confirmed; iron supplementation for anemia; colorectal MDT discussion; curative resection for localized disease; adjuvant chemotherapy with capecitabine or FOLFOX for node-positive disease.
red_flags: Complete large bowel obstruction from tumor requiring emergency surgery
Peritonitis from perforated tumor
Severe anemia with Hb below 70 requiring blood transfusion before surgery

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 23 سنة وبقالي شهر بحس بألم في ركبتي اليمين بيزيد لما بنزل سلم أو بقعد زمان. أنا بعدو ماراثونات وزدت التمرين من شهرين. في طقطقة في الركبة وتورم خفيف بعد التمرين.
extracted_symptoms: worsened by descending stairs and prolonged sitting
urgency_reason: Anterior knee pain worsened by stairs and prolonged flexion in a runner who increased training load is classic patellofemoral pain syndrome requiring activity modification and physiotherapy rather than imaging.
recommended_action: Clinical diagnosis with patellar grind test; MRI only if mechanical symptoms like locking or giving way; reduce running mileage by 50 percent for 4 weeks; vastus medialis oblique strengthening exercises; hip abductor strengthening; correct foot pronation with orthotics; anti-pronation running shoes; ice after exercise; gradual return to marathon training over 8-12 weeks.
red_flags: Joint locking suggesting meniscal tear or loose body requiring MRI
Significant effusion after acute injury suggesting ligament tear
Night pain or bone pain suggesting stress fracture or tumor

user_symptom_description: I'm 52 and I've been having chest tightness and difficulty breathing when I exercise or walk upstairs. It's been getting worse over two months. I also have a cough that produces yellowish phlegm most mornings. I've smoked 20 cigarettes a day for 25 years.
extracted_symptoms: productive morning cough with yellow phlegm
urgency_reason: Exertional dyspnea with productive cough in a heavy smoker requires spirometry to confirm airflow obstruction and chest X-ray to exclude concurrent lung malignancy.
recommended_action: Spirometry post-bronchodilator to confirm FEV1/FVC below 0.7; chest X-ray; CT thorax low-dose for lung cancer screening given smoking history; ABG if severe; GOLD staging; urgent smoking cessation with varenicline; SABA and LAMA bronchodilators; annual influenza and pneumococcal vaccination; pulmonary rehabilitation; check alpha-1-antitrypsin if age below 45 or basal emphysema.
red_flags: Hemoptysis or unexplained weight loss suggesting concurrent lung cancer
FEV1 below 30 percent predicted suggesting severe COPD requiring specialist management
Hypoxia with oxygen saturation below 88 percent requiring long-term oxygen assessment

user_symptom_description: عندي 73 سنة وبقالي يومين بحس بألم مفاجئ وشديد في بطني حواليه السرة والأسفل مع تورم. مش قادرة أتبرز ومش طالع ريح. بدأت بقيئ ومحتاجة أتقوء كتير. عندي عملية في بطني من سنتين.
extracted_symptoms: acute central and lower abdominal pain
urgency_reason: Acute small bowel obstruction with complete obstipation and vomiting in a patient with prior abdominal surgery is a surgical emergency with risk of strangulation and bowel infarction within hours.
recommended_action: Emergency admission immediately; nil by mouth; IV access and fluid resuscitation with electrolyte correction; NG tube decompression; CT abdomen pelvis with contrast to identify obstruction level and exclude strangulation; urgent surgical consult; conservative management with NG drainage for 24-48 hours if no peritoneal signs; emergency laparotomy if strangulation, perforation, or failed conservative management.
red_flags: Peritoneal signs with rebound and guarding suggesting strangulated bowel requiring emergency surgery
Fever with leukocytosis suggesting ischemic or infarcted segment
Hemodynamic instability requiring immediate resuscitation and surgery

user_symptom_description: I've had a burning pain in my upper stomach for weeks that gets worse when I'm stressed at work. Sometimes I wake up at night with it. I take antacids constantly but they only help for an hour or two.
urgency_reason: Chronic epigastric pain with nocturnal waking requires H. pylori testing and upper endoscopy to exclude ulcer before long-term acid suppression.
recommended_action: H. pylori breath test or stool antigen; upper endoscopy if alarm features or age above 55; PPI omeprazole 20mg twice daily for 4 weeks; stop NSAIDs; eradication therapy if H. pylori positive: omeprazole plus clarithromycin plus amoxicillin for 7 days; retest for eradication after 4 weeks.
red_flags: Haematemesis or melaena suggesting bleeding ulcer
Unintentional weight loss suggesting malignancy
Dysphagia suggesting gastric outlet obstruction

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وعندي إسهال مستمر وبطني بيتقلص جداً، وفقدت 4 كيلو. عندي 68 سنة وبخد أدوية للسرطان من 3 شهور. الإسهال بيجيلي أكتر من 8 مرات في اليوم.
urgency_reason: Grade 3 diarrhoea during chemotherapy requires dose modification and hospital assessment to prevent life-threatening dehydration and electrolyte disturbance.
recommended_action: Contact oncology team immediately; stool culture to exclude C. difficile; FBC, U&E, CRP; loperamide 4mg initially then 2mg after each loose stool up to 16mg/day; IV fluids if unable to maintain oral intake; hold chemotherapy until resolved; octreotide if loperamide fails; nutritional support.
red_flags: Bloody diarrhoea suggesting bowel ischaemia or C. difficile
Neutropenic enterocolitis with fever and right iliac fossa pain
Haemodynamic instability from severe dehydration

user_symptom_description: I've been having really bad pain in my lower back for two days after I helped a friend move furniture. The pain is only in my back and doesn't go down my legs. I can barely stand straight and any movement makes it worse.
urgency_reason: Acute mechanical back pain after lifting without neurological features is self-limiting in 90% of cases and requires analgesia and early mobilisation rather than imaging.
recommended_action: Regular ibuprofen 400mg three times daily with food for 5 days; paracetamol alternating for breakthrough pain; diazepam 2mg three times daily if severe muscle spasm for maximum 3 days; encourage early mobilisation; avoid bed rest; hot pack for comfort; physiotherapy if not improving at 6 weeks; no imaging unless red flags present.
red_flags: Saddle anaesthesia or bladder/bowel dysfunction suggesting cauda equina requiring emergency MRI
Night pain or fever suggesting discitis or malignancy
History of cancer with new back pain suggesting metastasis

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 35 سنة وبقالي أسبوع عندي طفح أحمر على صدري وظهري، حبوب صغيرة مليانة ميه وبتحرق. وكنت تعبانة من زكام خفيف قبلها بأسبوع. ما عنديش ألم.
urgency_reason: Primary chickenpox in an adult can cause severe pneumonia and encephalitis requiring antiviral treatment unlike in children.
recommended_action: Oral acyclovir 800mg five times daily for 7 days if within 24 hours of rash — adults require treatment unlike children; calamine lotion and antihistamine for itch; avoid scratching to prevent secondary infection; isolate from immunocompromised, pregnant women, and neonates; check varicella immune status of contacts; review pregnancy status.
red_flags: Respiratory symptoms suggesting varicella pneumonia requiring hospital admission
Neurological symptoms from encephalitis
Immunocompromised patient with rapidly spreading rash

user_symptom_description: My daughter noticed I've been losing my balance when I walk, especially in the dark, and I've had two near-falls this month. My feet feel numb and I've been on metformin for diabetes for 12 years. I'm 76.
possible_conditions: Diabetic Peripheral Neuropathy with Proprioception Loss
urgency_reason: Long-term metformin depletes B12 causing subacute combined degeneration mimicking or compounding diabetic neuropathy — B12 replacement can reverse deficits if caught early.
recommended_action: Urgent serum B12 and folate; FBC for macrocytosis; methylmalonic acid if borderline B12; neurological examination for dorsal column signs; IM hydroxocobalamin 1mg alternate days for 2 weeks then monthly; falls risk assessment; physiotherapy for balance; home safety review; walking aid; Romberg test.
red_flags: Rapidly progressive weakness in legs suggesting myelopathy requiring MRI spine
Cognitive decline from B12 deficiency dementia
Fall with head injury requiring CT head

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر وعندي ألم في الكتف الأيمن بيشتد لما بحاول أرفع إيدي فوق رأسي أو أمسك حاجة من ورا ظهري. الألم بيصحيني الليل وحاسس بضعف في الكتف.
urgency_reason: Shoulder pain with weakness and restricted active range of motion suggests rotator cuff pathology requiring MRI to differentiate tear from impingement.
recommended_action: MRI shoulder to characterise tear size and location; physiotherapy with rotator cuff strengthening and scapular stabilisation; subacromial corticosteroid injection for impingement; arthroscopic repair for full-thickness tear in active patients; ultrasound-guided injection as alternative; reassess at 8 weeks.
red_flags: Complete inability to initiate abduction suggesting massive tear requiring urgent surgical referral
Acute onset after fall suggesting traumatic full-thickness tear
Axillary nerve injury with deltoid weakness and reduced lateral shoulder sensation

user_symptom_description: My 4-year-old has been complaining of pain around her belly button that comes and goes. She says it hurts when she goes to the toilet and her stools have been really hard like little pebbles for weeks.
urgency_reason: Chronic constipation with hard stools in a preschooler is functional in the vast majority and responds to laxative therapy and dietary modification.
recommended_action: Macrogol 0.5g/kg daily as maintenance laxative; increase fibre and fluid intake; regular toilet sitting routine after meals; reward chart for positive reinforcement; exclude overflow incontinence; coeliac serology if growth concerns; review in 4 weeks; expect treatment for minimum 3-6 months.
red_flags: Failure to pass meconium in first 48 hours of life suggesting Hirschsprung's
Ribbon stools or abdominal distension suggesting anatomical abnormality
Blood in stool or weight loss suggesting inflammatory pathology

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام وعندي ألم شديد في بطني من الجهة اليمين السفلانية وأنا حامل في الأسبوع السابع. وعندي تقطير في المهبل وألم في الكتف الأيمن. وحرارتي ارتفعت.
urgency_reason: RIF pain with shoulder tip pain and vaginal bleeding at 7 weeks gestation is ruptured ectopic pregnancy until proven otherwise — a life-threatening emergency.
recommended_action: Call 999 immediately; IV access with two large-bore cannulae; FBC, group and crossmatch, serum beta-hCG; transvaginal ultrasound urgently; if haemodynamically unstable go directly to theatre; laparoscopic salpingectomy or salpingotomy; anti-D prophylaxis if Rhesus negative; psychological support post-procedure.
red_flags: Haemodynamic shock from haemoperitoneum requiring emergency laparotomy
Syncope from peritoneal irritation
Dropping haemoglobin confirming active haemorrhage

user_symptom_description: I'm a 47-year-old teacher and I've had a hoarse voice for 8 weeks. It started gradually and hasn't got better. I don't smoke but I use my voice a lot at work. I also have some throat clearing I can't stop.
urgency_reason: Hoarseness persisting more than 3 weeks requires laryngoscopy to exclude laryngeal malignancy before attributing to benign vocal pathology.
recommended_action: Urgent ENT referral for flexible nasolaryngoscopy within 2 weeks; if nodules confirmed voice therapy with speech and language therapist; vocal hygiene advice; treat LPR with PPI if suspected; microsurgery for nodules not responding to conservative treatment; 2-week wait cancer referral if any suspicion of malignancy.
red_flags: Dysphagia or odynophagia accompanying hoarseness suggesting laryngeal or hypopharyngeal cancer
Stridor indicating subglottic airway compromise
Unilateral vocal cord palsy from recurrent laryngeal nerve compression by lung or mediastinal tumour

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وعندي دم في البول مش بيوجعني خالص. الدم واضح وبيجيلي في بداية البول وآخره. عندي 74 سنة وبدخن من زمان.
extracted_symptoms: blood at start and end of stream
urgency_reason: Painless frank haematuria in an elderly male smoker is bladder cancer until proven otherwise requiring urgent 2-week wait urology referral.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week wait urology referral; urine cytology and MSU; CT urogram to image entire urothelium; flexible cystoscopy; biopsy if lesion found; TURBT for superficial disease; intravesical BCG or mitomycin for Ta/T1 tumours; cystectomy for muscle-invasive disease; PSA to assess prostate.
red_flags: Clot retention causing urinary obstruction requiring emergency catheterisation
Flank pain with haematuria suggesting upper tract urothelial cancer
Anaemia from chronic bleeding requiring transfusion

user_symptom_description: I've been having pain during my periods for years but the last 6 months it's become unbearable — I miss work every month. I also have pain during sex and my periods are getting heavier. Nothing from the pharmacy touches the pain.
urgency_reason: Progressive dysmenorrhoea with dyspareunia and NSAID resistance suggests endometriosis requiring laparoscopic diagnosis and hormonal or surgical management.
recommended_action: Pelvic ultrasound to assess for ovarian endometrioma; COCP or progestogen as empirical treatment; MRI pelvis if deep infiltrating endometriosis suspected; laparoscopy for definitive diagnosis and surgical ablation; GnRH agonist with add-back HRT for severe disease; fertility assessment if pregnancy desired; referral to endometriosis specialist centre.
red_flags: Acute pelvic pain suggesting ruptured endometrioma requiring emergency surgery
Urinary or bowel symptoms from infiltrating disease
Rapidly enlarging endometrioma suggesting malignant transformation

user_symptom_description: بقالي 4 أيام وعندي صداع شديد جداً من الجهة الشمال فوق الأذن، وعيني الشمال محمرة وبتدمع ومفيش ضغط في الصدر. الصداع بيجيلي كل يوم وبيدوم ساعة بالظبط.
urgency_reason: Daily stereotyped unilateral headache with ipsilateral autonomic features is cluster headache requiring acute abortive therapy and prophylaxis.
recommended_action: High-flow oxygen 100% at 12-15L/min for 15 minutes aborts attack; subcutaneous sumatriptan 6mg for acute attack; verapamil 240-960mg daily as prophylaxis during cluster period; short course prednisolone as bridge; MRI brain with pituitary views to exclude secondary cause; avoid alcohol triggers.
red_flags: New severe headache in a patient over 50 suggesting giant cell arteritis
Horner syndrome suggesting carotid dissection
Failure to respond to oxygen or triptans suggesting secondary headache

user_symptom_description: I'm 78 and I've been having pain and swelling in my right knee for months. It's worse after walking and by the end of the day. My knee makes a crunching noise and I've had to start using a walking stick. X-ray last year showed some wear.
urgency_reason: Symptomatic knee osteoarthritis with functional limitation requires stepped care management including physiotherapy, analgesia, and eventual surgical assessment.
recommended_action: Weight loss if BMI elevated; physiotherapy with quadriceps strengthening; regular paracetamol; topical diclofenac gel; intra-articular corticosteroid injection for flares; intra-articular hyaluronic acid; knee replacement assessment if WOMAC score severely impaired and conservative measures exhausted; avoid opioids long-term.
red_flags: Acute severe pain with hot swollen joint suggesting septic arthritis or gout flare
Rapidly worsening mechanical instability
Weight loss or night pain suggesting malignant bone lesion

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 29 سنة وبقالي 6 أشهر وبحس بتعب غير عادي وعيني بتصفر أحياناً وبولي بيبقى غامق بعد ما بعمل تمرين رياضي. وأهلي بيقولوا إن جدي مات من مشكلة في الكبد.
urgency_reason: Young patient with episodic jaundice, dark urine after exertion, and family hepatic history requires Wilson's disease exclusion as it is treatable and fatal if missed.
recommended_action: Serum ceruloplasmin and 24-hour urinary copper; slit-lamp examination for Kayser-Fleischer rings; LFTs, FBC, blood film for haemolysis; liver biopsy for quantitative copper if diagnostic uncertainty; D-penicillamine or trientine chelation therapy; zinc maintenance; genetic counselling for siblings; liver transplant if acute liver failure.
red_flags: Acute liver failure from haemolytic crisis requiring emergency transplant assessment
Neuropsychiatric features including dysarthria and tremor from basal ganglia copper deposition
Coombs-negative haemolytic anaemia as presenting feature

user_symptom_description: I've had terrible pain in my jaw for 3 weeks. It clicks when I open my mouth and I can't open it all the way without pain. I've been very stressed at work and my dentist noticed I've been grinding my teeth at night.
urgency_reason: TMJ dysfunction with bruxism in a stressed patient responds well to conservative management with splint and physiotherapy.
recommended_action: Soft diet for 4-6 weeks; jaw physiotherapy and stretching exercises; dental occlusal splint for nocturnal bruxism; NSAIDs or paracetamol for pain; stress management including CBT; botulinum toxin injection into masseter for severe bruxism; MRI TMJ only if locking or suspected disc displacement requiring surgery.
red_flags: Trismus with fever suggesting peritonsillar or submandibular abscess requiring emergency drainage
Acute locked jaw from disc displacement requiring manipulation
Weight loss with jaw pain in elderly suggesting giant cell arteritis

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهرين وبحس بألم شديد في ظهري بيزيد لما أتحرك أو أقوم من الكرسي. عندي 75 سنة وبخد كورتيزون من سنتين لمرض في الرئة. وبقيت أقصر بوضوح.
urgency_reason: Acute back pain with height loss in an elderly patient on long-term corticosteroids is osteoporotic vertebral fracture until proven otherwise requiring imaging and bone protection.
recommended_action: Urgent X-ray spine; MRI if neurological symptoms or to date fracture acuity; DEXA scan for bone density; analgesia with paracetamol and weak opioid; calcium 1200mg and vitamin D 800IU daily; bisphosphonate such as alendronate or zoledronic acid; vertebroplasty for severe persistent pain; review corticosteroid dose; myeloma screen with SPEP.
red_flags: Neurological deficit from cord compression requiring emergency decompression
Multiple fractures suggesting myeloma requiring haematology workup
Pathological fracture from metastatic disease

user_symptom_description: I eat very little and when I do I feel disgusting about myself. I've lost 14kg in 4 months and my periods stopped. My family is very worried. My BMI is now 15.5. I know I'm thin but I still feel fat when I look in the mirror.
urgency_reason: BMI of 15.5 with secondary amenorrhoea and rapid weight loss indicates severe anorexia nervosa with risk of cardiac arrhythmia and multi-organ failure requiring urgent specialist assessment.
recommended_action: Urgent eating disorder team assessment; ECG for QTc prolongation and bradycardia; U&E for hypokalaemia; FBC, LFTs, glucose; BMI below 13 or rapid decline requires inpatient medical stabilisation before psychiatric treatment; family-based therapy for adolescents; Maudsley method; avoid rapid refeeding to prevent refeeding syndrome; dietitian involvement.
red_flags: Bradycardia below 40 bpm or QTc prolongation indicating cardiac risk
Syncope or muscle weakness from hypokalaemia
Refeeding syndrome with phosphate below 0.5 mmol/L after nutritional support begins

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وعندي صوت أبحّ ومش قادر أبتلع السوائل بسهولة، وبحس بكتلة في حلقي من الجانب الأيمن. مش عندي ألم. عندي 50 سنة وبدخن 30 سنة.
urgency_reason: Painless neck mass with hoarseness and dysphagia in a heavy smoker is head and neck malignancy requiring urgent 2-week wait ENT assessment.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week wait head and neck cancer referral; flexible nasolaryngoscopy; CT neck and thorax with contrast; PET-CT for nodal staging; fine-needle aspiration of neck mass; panendoscopy under general anaesthesia for biopsy; MDT discussion; primary radiotherapy or surgery depending on T stage; PEG tube assessment for nutritional support.
red_flags: Stridor from supraglottic tumour obstructing airway requiring emergency tracheostomy
Rapid increase in neck mass size suggesting aggressive lymphoma
Carotid artery involvement on imaging suggesting unresectable disease

user_symptom_description: My 2-year-old swallowed a small button battery from the TV remote about an hour ago. He seems okay right now but I'm panicking. He's drooling a bit more than usual.
urgency_reason: Button battery lodged in the oesophagus causes liquefactive necrosis and full-thickness perforation within 2 hours requiring immediate endoscopic removal.
recommended_action: Call 999 or go to A&E immediately — do not wait for symptoms; urgent CXR and AXR to locate battery; if in oesophagus emergency endoscopic removal within 2 hours; if in stomach and asymptomatic repeat X-ray in 24 hours; do not give anything by mouth; honey can be given en route to reduce injury if available — 10ml every 10 minutes for children over 1 year; no ipecac.
red_flags: Respiratory distress from tracheal compression
Haematemesis indicating oesophageal perforation
Fever from mediastinitis after delayed presentation

user_symptom_description: بقالي 6 أشهر وبصحى الليل وأنا عرقانة جداً وبيجيلي هبّات ساخنة فجأة في أي وقت. وشعري بدأ يخف ومزاجي بيتغير. عندي 49 سنة وحيضتي بدأت تتأخر وتتقل.
urgency_reason: Classic vasomotor symptoms with menstrual irregularity at age 49 are perimenopausal and warrant discussion of HRT benefits and risks.
recommended_action: FSH and oestradiol; TFTs to exclude hyperthyroidism; discuss HRT — oestrogen plus progestogen for women with uterus; transdermal oestrogen preferred; assess cardiovascular and breast cancer risk before prescribing; cognitive behavioural therapy for mood; calcium and vitamin D for bone protection; DEXA if early menopause or risk factors.
red_flags: Hot flushes with diarrhoea, flushing and wheezing suggesting carcinoid syndrome
Age below 40 suggesting premature ovarian insufficiency requiring urgent fertility discussion
Breakthrough bleeding on HRT requiring endometrial biopsy

user_symptom_description: I've had a high fever of 39.5°C, shaking chills, and pain in my lower back and right side for 2 days. It hurts a lot when I press on my right flank. I feel nauseous and vomited once. I had a UTI last month.
urgency_reason: Systemic sepsis signs with loin pain and tenderness after recent UTI is acute pyelonephritis requiring IV antibiotics and imaging to exclude obstructed infected kidney.
recommended_action: Go to A&E; urine MC&S and blood cultures before antibiotics; FBC, CRP, U&E; CT KUB to exclude obstruction; IV gentamicin or co-amoxiclav; if obstructed infected kidney emergency nephrostomy insertion; switch to oral ciprofloxacin when apyrexial for 48 hours; total 2-week course; imaging in 6 weeks to exclude underlying abnormality.
red_flags: Haemodynamic instability from urosepsis requiring ITU
Obstructed infected kidney — surgical emergency requiring immediate drainage
Failure to defervesce within 72 hours suggesting abscess or resistant organism

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع وعيني الأيمن بتوجعني ومش شايفة بيها كويس، زي ما في ضباب. والألم بيزيد لما أحرك العين. أنا عندي 27 سنة وما عنديش مشاكل في العين قبل كده.
extracted_symptoms: young woman no prior eye disease
urgency_reason: Pain on eye movement with unilateral visual loss in a young woman is optic neuritis — a frequent first presentation of multiple sclerosis requiring urgent MRI.
recommended_action: Urgent ophthalmology review; visual acuity, colour vision, relative afferent pupillary defect assessment; MRI brain and orbits with gadolinium; VEP studies; IV methylprednisolone 1g daily for 3 days accelerates recovery but does not change long-term outcome; neurology referral for MS risk assessment; commence disease-modifying therapy if MS criteria met.
red_flags: Complete visual loss within 24 hours
Bilateral optic neuritis suggesting neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder
MRI showing multiple demyelinating lesions confirming MS requiring immediate disease-modifying therapy

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وعندي ألم شديد وتورم في رجلي الأيمن من تحت الركبة. المنطقة سخنة وحمرا وما قدرتش أمشي. ما كانش عندي إصابة. عندي 71 سنة وقاعدة في البيت من شهر بعد عملية في ورك.
urgency_reason: Unilateral calf swelling with warmth after prolonged post-operative immobility is DVT until proven otherwise requiring urgent Doppler and anticoagulation.
recommended_action: Go to hospital urgently; Wells DVT score; duplex venous ultrasound; start DOAC rivaroxaban 15mg twice daily with food immediately if Wells score 2 or above without waiting for ultrasound result; minimum 3 months anticoagulation; VTE prophylaxis review; early mobilisation; compression stockings after acute phase; thrombophilia screen if unprovoked.
red_flags: Sudden dyspnoea or pleuritic chest pain suggesting PE
Phlegmasia cerulea dolens — entire leg swollen blue with venous gangrene risk
Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia if heparin used

user_symptom_description: I've been having episodes where I suddenly feel my heart racing out of nowhere, lasting about 20 minutes, then it stops as suddenly as it started. It's happened 5 times in the last month. I feel fine between episodes but dizzy during them.
urgency_reason: Paroxysmal regular tachycardia with abrupt onset and offset suggests SVT requiring ECG during episode and electrophysiology assessment.
recommended_action: 12-lead ECG during episode if possible — ask patient to attend A&E during next attack; 7-day event monitor or implantable loop recorder; echocardiogram; adenosine 6mg IV for acute termination; Valsalva manoeuvre self-management; beta-blocker or flecainide prophylaxis; electrophysiology study and radiofrequency ablation for definitive cure.
red_flags: Syncope during tachycardia suggesting haemodynamic compromise or WPW
Wide complex tachycardia on ECG suggesting VT requiring emergency cardioversion
Pre-excitation delta wave on resting ECG confirming WPW requiring urgent ablation

user_symptom_description: بنتي عندها 8 سنين وبقالها أسبوعين بتشكي من صداع بيجيلي كل يوم تقريباً بعد الظهر ومش عارفين ليه. مش عندها حرارة. واتكلمنا مع أصحابها وقالوا إن في مشاكل في المدرسة مع صاحبة.
urgency_reason: Daily headaches in a child with an identified school stressor are most likely tension-type or functional and respond to addressing the psychosocial cause.
recommended_action: Detailed headache diary; visual acuity and refraction check to exclude refractive error; address school bullying with parents and school; paracetamol for acute episodes; avoid analgesic overuse above 15 days per month; sleep hygiene and screen time review; relaxation techniques; school counsellor involvement; review in 4 weeks.
red_flags: Morning headache with vomiting suggesting raised ICP
Headache waking child from sleep
Neurological signs on examination

user_symptom_description: I had a really bad migraine yesterday with a strange aura where I saw zigzag lines and half my vision went. Then 30 minutes later my right arm and hand went numb and weak for about an hour before everything came back to normal. I'm on the pill.
urgency_reason: Migraine with aura plus motor weakness on the combined pill is high risk for stroke — the COCP is absolutely contraindicated and urgent neurological assessment is required.
recommended_action: Stop combined oral contraceptive immediately — absolute contraindication with migraine with aura; switch to progestogen-only contraception; urgent MRI brain; stroke risk factor assessment; aspirin 75mg if TIA cannot be excluded; calcium channel blocker flunarizine for hemiplegic migraine prophylaxis; avoid triptans in hemiplegic migraine.
red_flags: Permanent motor deficit from migrainous infarction
Worsening or new neurological deficit during attack
CADASIL family history suggesting genetic cerebrovascular disease

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام وعندي ألم شديد في بطني من الناحية اليسار السفلانية وعندي حمى وإمساك. مش قادر أعدي من الألم وفيه حاجة زي التورم في نفس المنطقة. عندي 70 سنة.
urgency_reason: LIF pain with fever and localised mass in an elderly patient is acute diverticulitis requiring CT abdomen and antibiotic therapy.
recommended_action: Go to A&E; CT abdomen pelvis with contrast to confirm diverticulitis and exclude perforation or abscess; FBC, CRP; IV co-amoxiclav and metronidazole; analgesia; if uncomplicated and well can manage with oral antibiotics as outpatient; colonoscopy 6-8 weeks after recovery to exclude malignancy; surgical referral if Hinchey III/IV perforation.
red_flags: Free air on CT indicating perforation requiring emergency laparotomy
Diverticular abscess requiring CT-guided drainage
Faecal peritonitis with haemodynamic collapse

user_symptom_description: I've been having chest pain that comes on when I'm running but goes away completely within a few minutes of stopping. It's a tight squeezing feeling across my chest. I'm 32 and had Kawasaki disease as a child.
possible_conditions: Coronary Artery Aneurysm from Kawasaki Disease
primary_condition: Coronary Artery Aneurysm from Kawasaki Disease
urgency_reason: Exertional chest pain in a young patient with Kawasaki disease history is coronary artery aneurysm complication with ischaemia requiring urgent cardiology assessment.
recommended_action: Urgent cardiology referral; coronary CT angiography or invasive coronary angiography to assess aneurysm size and stenosis; stress testing with imaging; low-dose aspirin lifelong; anticoagulation if giant aneurysm; beta-blocker for symptom control; restrict strenuous exercise pending assessment; PCI or CABG if significant stenosis.
red_flags: Symptoms at rest suggesting acute coronary syndrome from thrombosis within aneurysm
Giant aneurysm above 8mm with high thrombosis risk
Sudden cardiac death risk from ventricular arrhythmia

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر وأنا حاسة بوخز وتنميل في وجهي من الجهة اليسار، وبيجيلي أحياناً ضعف في إيدي اليسار لساعات ثم يرجع. أنا عندي 38 سنة وبدأت بعد الولادة بشهرين.
urgency_reason: Episodic unilateral sensorimotor symptoms in a postpartum young woman requires urgent MRI to distinguish MS relapse from cerebral venous thrombosis.
recommended_action: Urgent MRI brain and spine with gadolinium; MR venography to exclude cerebral venous thrombosis; D-dimer; CSF analysis for oligoclonal bands; if MS confirmed with previous episode: IV methylprednisolone for acute relapse; commence disease-modifying therapy; postpartum thrombosis risk assessment; anticoagulation if CVT confirmed.
red_flags: Headache with papilloedema suggesting raised ICP from cerebral venous thrombosis
Rapid progression of motor deficit
Optic neuritis concurrent with motor symptoms strengthening MS diagnosis

user_symptom_description: I work in a mine and I've had a cough that's been getting worse over 2 years. It produces black phlegm sometimes. I've lost weight and I'm short of breath even walking on flat ground. I'm 55.
urgency_reason: Progressive respiratory symptoms with black sputum in a miner requires HRCT and spirometry for occupational lung disease assessment and malignancy exclusion.
recommended_action: HRCT chest; spirometry; FBC and ESR; sputum cytology and culture including TB; DEXA for bone density; report to occupational health and regulatory authority; remove from dust exposure; treat COPD component with inhalers; pulmonary rehabilitation; disability assessment and occupational compensation claim; annual surveillance.
red_flags: Rapidly progressive massive fibrosis
Haemoptysis suggesting concurrent lung cancer or cavitation
Rheumatoid factor positive with pneumoconiosis suggesting Caplan syndrome

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع وعندي ألم شديد في معصمي الأيمن بعد ما وقعت على إيدي. المعصم منتفخ ومشوّه ومش قادرة أحركه. عندي 72 سنة ومعروف إن عندي هشاشة.
extracted_symptoms: right wrist pain after fall on outstretched hand
urgency_reason: Wrist deformity with swelling after fall in an osteoporotic elderly woman is Colles fracture requiring X-ray, reduction, and immobilisation.
recommended_action: Go to A&E; AP and lateral wrist X-ray; haematoma block and Bier's block for closed reduction; below-elbow plaster or surgical fixation with volar locking plate if unstable or intra-articular; neurovascular assessment before and after reduction; DEXA scan and bisphosphonate therapy; osteoporosis management to prevent further fragility fractures; falls assessment.
red_flags: Median nerve compression causing acute carpal tunnel after fracture
Vascular injury to radial artery
Open fracture requiring emergency surgery

user_symptom_description: أنا لاعب كرة قدم وأمبارح ضربت ضربة في رأسي بشدة. صحيت النهارده وعندي صداع وغثيان وبحس بطنخة وصعب أركز. مش فاقد وعيي خالص.
urgency_reason: Post-concussion symptoms require clinical assessment using SCAT5 tool to stratify need for CT head and guide return-to-play protocol.
recommended_action: Apply SCAT5 concussion assessment; CT head if GCS below 15, seizure, repeated vomiting, amnesia, or anticoagulated; physical and cognitive rest for 24-48 hours then graded return; no return to play until symptom-free at rest; graduated return-to-play protocol minimum 6 steps; avoid alcohol and screen time acutely; review at 7-10 days.
red_flags: Deteriorating consciousness or GCS drop requiring emergency CT
Unequal pupils suggesting herniation
Neck pain with neurology suggesting cervical spine injury requiring immobilisation

user_symptom_description: I've had joint pain and skin tightening on my fingers and hands for 4 months. My fingers go white and blue in the cold, and I've been having difficulty swallowing solid food. I also have heartburn all the time.
extracted_symptoms: skin tightening over fingers and hands
urgency_reason: Skin sclerosis with Raynaud's and oesophageal dysmotility is systemic sclerosis requiring anti-Scl70 and anti-centromere antibodies to assess subtype and organ involvement.
recommended_action: ANA, anti-Scl70, anti-centromere antibodies; HRCT chest for interstitial lung disease; echocardiogram for pulmonary hypertension; nailfold capillaroscopy; PPI for reflux; nifedipine for Raynaud's; cyclophosphamide or mycophenolate for lung disease; prostacyclin infusion for digital ulcers; bosentan for PAH; physiotherapy for hand contractures.
red_flags: Pulmonary hypertension with exertional syncope requiring urgent treatment
Renal crisis with rapidly rising creatinine requiring ACE inhibitor urgently
Rapidly progressing skin thickening spreading proximally

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وابني عنده 6 سنين بيبلل فراشه الليل وكمان بيجيلي حوادث في النهار. كان بيعدي من السنة اللي فاتت وبعدين رجعت المشكلة. مش عنده ألم لما يبول.
urgency_reason: Secondary enuresis with daytime accidents requires urine culture to exclude UTI and blood glucose to exclude diabetes before behavioural management.
recommended_action: MSU for culture; blood glucose; abdominal X-ray if constipation suspected; review for recent stressors including new sibling or school change; enuresis alarm as most effective long-term treatment; fluid restriction in evenings; desmopressin for short-term use; treat constipation if present; reassure parents; no punishment.
red_flags: Polyuria and polydipsia with glucose in urine suggesting new diabetes
Daytime accidents with abnormal urinary stream suggesting anatomical abnormality
Regression with behavioural change suggesting abuse or significant psychological stressor

user_symptom_description: I've been having terrible anxiety attacks where my heart pounds, I sweat, I feel like I'm dying, and I shake uncontrollably. They come without warning and last about 10 minutes. I've ended up in A&E twice this month. All the heart tests were normal.
urgency_reason: Recurrent panic attacks with normal cardiac workup meets panic disorder criteria requiring CBT rather than further cardiac investigations.
recommended_action: Urinary metanephrines to exclude phaeochromocytoma; TFTs; CBT with panic-focused exposure therapy as most effective treatment; SSRI sertraline or paroxetine if CBT unavailable or insufficient; breathing retraining; avoid benzodiazepines for long-term use; psychoeducation about panic cycle; avoid caffeine; safety behaviours addressed in therapy.
red_flags: Hypertension during attacks suggesting phaeochromocytoma requiring 24-hour urinary catecholamines
Cardiac arrhythmia on Holter during symptom diary
Agoraphobia so severe patient is housebound requiring intensive outreach treatment

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر وعندي صداع شديد فوق هيكتي وعلى فودي، وحسيت بألم في فكي لما باكل. وعندي ضعف في كتفايا من قبل كده. عندي 72 سنة وأنا شايفة إن جسمي تقيل ومتعب.
possible_conditions: Giant Cell Arteritis with Polymyalgia Rheumatica
primary_condition: Giant Cell Arteritis with Polymyalgia Rheumatica
urgency_reason: Jaw claudication with temporal headache in an elderly patient is giant cell arteritis — a vascular emergency as permanent blindness can occur without same-day high-dose steroids.
recommended_action: Start prednisolone 60mg immediately before investigations — do not wait; urgent ESR and CRP; temporal artery biopsy within 2 weeks does not need to be done before starting steroids; ophthalmology review; tocilizumab as steroid-sparing agent; taper prednisolone slowly over 2 years; bone protection with bisphosphonate and calcium.
red_flags: Sudden visual loss from anterior ischaemic optic neuropathy requiring emergency IV methylprednisolone
Diplopia from cranial nerve ischaemia
Aortic aneurysm from large vessel involvement

user_symptom_description: I've been having difficulty initiating urine stream, feeling like my bladder never fully empties, and I've been going to the toilet 4 times at night. I'm 48 and recently started a new antidepressant 2 months ago.
possible_conditions: Drug-Induced Urinary Retention from Anticholinergic Antidepressant
primary_condition: Drug-Induced Urinary Retention from Anticholinergic Antidepressant
urgency_reason: LUTS temporally related to tricyclic antidepressant start in a 48-year-old requires medication review before prostatic investigations.
recommended_action: Review antidepressant — switch from TCA to SSRI with lower anticholinergic burden; PSA and DRE to exclude prostatic pathology; post-void residual ultrasound; if urinary retention persists after medication change refer to urology; avoid other anticholinergic drugs; reassess LUTS 4 weeks after medication switch.
red_flags: Acute urinary retention with inability to void requiring emergency catheterisation
PSA above 10 suggesting prostate cancer
Bilateral hydronephrosis from obstructive uropathy on ultrasound

user_symptom_description: بقالي 4 أشهر وبحس بكتلة في رقبتي من الجهة الأيمن، ما بتوجعنيش وبيجيلي تعب وحرارة خفيفة الليل مع عرق. عندي 25 سنة وما عنديش مشاكل صحية.
urgency_reason: Painless cervical lymphadenopathy with B symptoms in a 25-year-old is Hodgkin lymphoma until proven otherwise — a 2-week wait cancer referral is mandatory.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week wait lymphoma referral; FBC, ESR, LDH, LFTs, HIV, EBV, TB screen; CT chest, abdomen, pelvis; excisional lymph node biopsy — not FNAC alone; PET-CT for staging after histological confirmation; ABVD chemotherapy for classical Hodgkin; fertility preservation before chemotherapy in young women; discuss treatment and prognosis openly.
red_flags: Mediastinal mass causing SVC obstruction or airway compression
Lymph node growing rapidly suggesting aggressive lymphoma transformation
Bone marrow failure from extensive infiltration

user_symptom_description: I'm 44 and had my appendix out 3 years ago. Over the last 2 months I've had intermittent cramping and bloating. Yesterday my belly swelled up rapidly and I haven't been able to pass gas or have a bowel movement for 24 hours and I'm vomiting.
urgency_reason: Complete bowel obstruction with absolute constipation after prior abdominal surgery is adhesional SBO with strangulation risk requiring emergency surgical assessment.
recommended_action: Go to A&E immediately; nil by mouth; IV access and fluid resuscitation; NG tube insertion for decompression; CT abdomen pelvis with contrast to assess for strangulation, closed loop, or transition point; FBC, U&E, lactate; surgical review urgently; water-soluble contrast follow-through if no strangulation; laparoscopic adhesiolysis if conservative management fails or strangulation suspected.
red_flags: Raised lactate suggesting bowel ischaemia requiring emergency laparotomy
Peritonism with rebound tenderness
Sepsis from strangulated bowel with necrosis

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع وعندي ألم خفيف في بطني من الجانب الأيمن وعيوني اصفرت، وبولي بقى غامق وبطني بدأ ينتفخ. عندي 69 سنة وعندي تليف كبدي من 5 سنين.
urgency_reason: New jaundice with ascites in a cirrhotic patient requires urgent AFP and liver imaging to exclude hepatocellular carcinoma as a treatable complication.
recommended_action: Urgent AFP, LFTs, FBC, coagulation screen; triphasic CT liver or MRI with gadolinium; diagnostic ascitic tap to exclude SBP; IV cefotaxime if SBP confirmed; spironolactone and furosemide for ascites; MDT assessment for hepatic resection, ablation, TACE, or sorafenib; liver transplant assessment if within Milan criteria; MELD score assessment.
red_flags: Haematemesis from variceal bleeding requiring emergency endoscopic band ligation
Hepatic encephalopathy from acute decompensation
Rapid ascites accumulation suggesting portal vein thrombosis

user_symptom_description: I was in a car accident 3 months ago and since then I can't stop thinking about it. I feel jumpy, can't concentrate at university, and I drink a lot more alcohol now to help me sleep. I've had two blackouts from drinking.
urgency_reason: Comorbid PTSD and alcohol misuse is a high-risk combination requiring integrated dual diagnosis treatment as alcohol use perpetuates avoidance and worsens trauma symptoms.
recommended_action: AUDIT-C for alcohol use severity; PCL-5 for PTSD; both conditions must be treated concurrently — not sequentially; trauma-focused CBT or EMDR; motivational interviewing for alcohol reduction; acamprosate or naltrexone for alcohol craving; liver function tests; safeguarding assessment at university; mentor or peer support.
red_flags: Alcohol withdrawal seizure from abrupt cessation requiring inpatient detoxification
Active suicidal ideation from combined PTSD and alcohol depression
Blackouts with head injury risk

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهرين وعندي ألم مستمر في عظام حوضي وظهري السفلاني، وبحس بتعب غير طبيعي. الحيض بقى غزير جداً وبينزل جلطات. عندي 41 سنة وما أنجبتش قبل كده.
urgency_reason: Heavy menstrual bleeding with pelvic pain and fatigue in a nulliparous woman requires pelvic ultrasound and endometrial biopsy to exclude malignancy.
recommended_action: Pelvic ultrasound; endometrial biopsy if over 40 or abnormal bleeding pattern; FBC for anaemia and ferritin; LNG-IUS as first-line for menorrhagia with fibroids; GnRH agonist for 3 months pre-surgery; myomectomy to preserve uterus in nulliparous patient desiring fertility; uterine artery embolisation as alternative; iron supplementation.
red_flags: Post-coital bleeding or irregular bleeding suggesting endometrial cancer
Rapid fibroid growth suggesting sarcomatous change
Haemoglobin below 7 requiring transfusion

user_symptom_description: My 13-year-old has been refusing to eat for 6 months and she's lost a lot of weight. She says she's not hungry but I can see her counting calories and refusing meals. She exercises excessively every day even when sick. Her BMI is 16.
urgency_reason: BMI 16 with food restriction and excessive exercise in an adolescent is anorexia nervosa requiring urgent CAMHS eating disorder team referral.
recommended_action: Urgent CAMHS eating disorder referral; ECG for QTc and bradycardia; blood glucose, electrolytes, phosphate; FBC, LFTs; family-based therapy as first line for adolescents; inpatient medical admission if bradycardia, BMI below 13, or rapid weight loss; refeeding protocol with phosphate monitoring; school liaison; no punitive approaches to food.
red_flags: Bradycardia below 50 bpm or hypotension indicating medical emergency
Refeeding syndrome risk with severe malnutrition
Suicidal ideation related to body image distress

user_symptom_description: بقالي يومين وعندي صعوبة في المضغ وفتح فمي صعب جداً، وإيدي وأقدامي بدأت تتصلب وتتشنج. وعندي جرح في قدمي من أسبوع من مسمار صدي في الأرض.
extracted_symptoms: wound from rusty nail 1 week ago
urgency_reason: Trismus with generalised rigidity following a puncture wound from a rusty object is tetanus — a life-threatening emergency with high mortality without ICU care.
recommended_action: Call 999 immediately; ICU admission; human tetanus immunoglobulin 3000-6000 IU IM immediately; metronidazole IV for 10 days; wound debridement; diazepam IV for spasm control; magnesium sulphate infusion; mechanical ventilation if respiratory muscle involvement; tetanus toxoid vaccination; quiet dark environment to minimise stimulus-triggered spasms.
red_flags: Respiratory muscle spasm causing asphyxia requiring emergency intubation
Autonomic instability with hypertensive crises
Opisthotonos indicating severe generalised tetanus with high mortality

user_symptom_description: I'm 80 and I've been taking 12 different tablets a day. My daughter noticed I've become very confused and sleepy since my GP added two new medications last week. I fell yesterday.
urgency_reason: Acute confusion with fall after adding medications to a polypharmacy patient requires urgent medication review and assessment for drug-drug interactions.
recommended_action: Admit for assessment; medication reconciliation using STOPP/START criteria; stop or reduce sedatives, anticholinergics, and inappropriate medications; U&E, glucose, renal function; CT head if fall with head injury; ECG for drug-induced arrhythmia; pharmacist medication review; Beers criteria assessment; aim to reduce to minimum essential medications.
red_flags: Head injury with intracranial haemorrhage from fall
Respiratory depression from opioid or benzodiazepine over-dose
Severe hyponatraemia from diuretic or SSRI addition causing seizure

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وعندي ألم في ضرسي الأسفل من الجهة اليمين، وبيجيلي ألم عند البلع وورم في خدي. وعندي حمى وما قدرتش أفتح فمي صح. البنج الموضعي ما اشتغلش عند الدكتور.
urgency_reason: Dental abscess with trismus, fever, and failed anaesthesia spreading to submandibular space risks Ludwig's angina with fatal airway compromise requiring emergency surgical drainage.
recommended_action: Go to A&E immediately; assess airway urgently — anaesthetics alert if trismus worsening; CT neck with contrast to map spread; IV broad-spectrum antibiotics co-amoxiclav plus metronidazole; surgical drainage under GA; ENT and oral maxillofacial surgery emergency involvement; airway management plan including fibreoptic intubation or surgical airway; blood cultures.
red_flags: Stridor or drooling indicating impending airway obstruction
Extension to parapharyngeal or retropharyngeal space on CT
Descending mediastinitis from untreated Ludwig's angina — mortality above 40%

user_symptom_description: I've had a productive cough for 3 weeks with dark brown blood in the phlegm. I've lost 6kg in 2 months and I sweat through my clothes at night. I came from Somalia 6 months ago.
urgency_reason: Classic TB presentation with B symptoms and haemoptysis in a recent immigrant from a high-prevalence country requires immediate isolation and investigation.
recommended_action: Isolate in negative-pressure room; 3 sputum samples for AFB smear and culture; CXR; IGRA and Mantoux; HIV test; notify public health; contact tracing of household contacts; standard four-drug therapy HRZE for 2 months then HR for 4 months; DOT if adherence concern; LTBI treatment for contacts; DST for drug resistance.
red_flags: Massive haemoptysis above 200ml requiring bronchial artery embolisation
MDR-TB or XDR-TB requiring specialist second-line drugs
Miliary TB with haematogenous dissemination requiring ITU

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أيام وعندي حكة شديدة في جسمي وخاصة في بطني وإيدي ومش شايفة أي طفح. أنا حامل في الأسبوع 34. وما عنديش مشاكل كبدية قبل كده.
urgency_reason: Generalised pruritus without rash in the third trimester is intrahepatic cholestasis of pregnancy until proven otherwise — associated with stillbirth risk requiring urgent bile acid measurement.
recommended_action: Urgent serum bile acids and LFTs; bile acids above 40 micromol/L indicates high-risk — involve obstetric team immediately; ursodeoxycholic acid 500mg twice daily to reduce bile acids and itch; weekly bile acid monitoring; increased foetal surveillance with CTG; elective delivery at 37-38 weeks; repeat LFTs 10 days postpartum to confirm resolution.
red_flags: Bile acids above 100 micromol/L associated with very high stillbirth risk requiring immediate delivery planning
Jaundice suggesting severe hepatic impairment
Abnormal foetal movements or CTG requiring emergency delivery

user_symptom_description: I'm 77 and I've been noticing that I can't hear in my right ear properly for 6 months. I have to ask people to repeat themselves and I can't follow conversations in noisy places. My wife says I have the TV too loud.
urgency_reason: Progressive bilateral hearing loss in an elderly patient is presbycusis until proven otherwise requiring audiometry for hearing aid assessment.
recommended_action: Otoscopy to exclude cerumen impaction — first-line is ear irrigation; pure tone audiometry; MRI internal auditory meati if asymmetric loss to exclude acoustic neuroma; hearing aid fitting — bilateral digital hearing aids are most effective; cochlear implant assessment if profound loss; communication strategies; lip-reading classes; loop system at home.
red_flags: Unilateral sudden hearing loss requiring emergency treatment with steroids
Asymmetric loss with tinnitus suggesting acoustic neuroma
Rapid progression over weeks suggesting immune-mediated hearing loss

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين وعندي حرارة عالية وصداع وألم خلف عيني، وعندي طفح أحمر على جلدي بدأ من 4 أيام. أنا رجعت من رحلة في تايلاند من أسبوعين. وعندي ألم في عضلاتي ومفاصلي بيوجعني جداً.
extracted_symptoms: return from Southeast Asia 2 weeks ago
urgency_reason: Classic dengue triad of retro-orbital headache, rash, and severe musculoskeletal pain after travel to endemic area requires urgent dengue NS1 antigen and serology.
recommended_action: Dengue NS1 antigen and IgM/IgG; FBC for thrombocytopenia and leucopenia; LFTs; admission if platelets below 100 or warning signs; paracetamol only for fever — NO aspirin or NSAIDs due to bleeding risk; IV fluids if unable to maintain oral intake; monitor for dengue haemorrhagic fever; notify public health.
red_flags: Platelets below 20 indicating dengue haemorrhagic fever with bleeding risk
Abdominal pain with ascites suggesting plasma leakage
Altered consciousness indicating dengue encephalitis

user_symptom_description: I've been having a strange metallic taste in my mouth for 3 weeks and I keep drooling at night. My hands shake when I reach for things and my writing has become very small. I feel really stiff in the mornings. I'm 62.
urgency_reason: Kinetic tremor with micrographia, drooling, and morning rigidity in a 62-year-old requires neurological assessment to confirm Parkinson's and exclude drug-induced or secondary causes.
recommended_action: Urgent neurology referral; medication history for dopamine-blocking drugs; MRI brain to exclude structural cause; DaTscan if diagnosis uncertain; serum ceruloplasmin and slit-lamp if age below 50 to exclude Wilson's; levodopa/carbidopa trial; physiotherapy, speech therapy, occupational therapy; Parkinson's UK nurse specialist referral; driving assessment.
red_flags: Early falls suggesting Parkinson's plus syndrome such as PSP
Autonomic failure with orthostatic hypotension suggesting MSA
Rapid cognitive decline in first year suggesting DLB

user_symptom_description: بقالي يومين وعندي ألم شديد في ظهري ويمتد للبطن ومن امبارح عندي حرارة شديدة وقشعريرة. أنا حامل في الأسبوع 20 وعندي سكر حمل.
urgency_reason: Pyelonephritis in pregnancy with gestational diabetes is a high-risk obstetric emergency as it can precipitate preterm labour and maternal sepsis.
recommended_action: Emergency obstetric admission; IV cefuroxime empirically — avoid quinolones in pregnancy; blood and urine cultures; FBC, CRP, U&E; CTG for foetal wellbeing; renal ultrasound to exclude obstruction; IV fluids; antipyretics with paracetamol only; switch to oral antibiotics when apyrexial; two-week oral course total; urine culture after completion.
red_flags: Preterm contractions precipitated by infection
Septic shock requiring ITU admission
Obstructed infected kidney requiring nephrostomy despite pregnancy

user_symptom_description: I've had a deep ulcer on my right foot that won't heal for 3 months. It doesn't hurt at all which worries me more. The skin around it is dark and the ulcer is deep — I can see yellowish tissue. I have type 2 diabetes for 18 years.
urgency_reason: Non-healing diabetic foot ulcer with necrotic tissue in longstanding diabetes requires urgent multidisciplinary diabetic foot team assessment as osteomyelitis risk is high.
recommended_action: Urgent diabetic foot team referral; ABPI and vascular assessment; probe-to-bone test; plain X-ray and MRI foot for osteomyelitis; tissue biopsy for deep culture; IV antibiotics targeting Staphylococcus and polymicrobial organisms; surgical debridement; offloading total contact cast; vascular revascularisation if PAD; HbA1c optimisation.
red_flags: Gas on X-ray suggesting necrotising fasciitis requiring emergency surgical debridement
Systemic sepsis from ascending infection
Bone involvement on MRI requiring prolonged IV antibiotics or amputation consideration

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع وعندي ضيق شديد في التنفس حتى وأنا قاعدة، وبحس بضغط في صدري وعندي سعال جاف. كمان بقيت أصحى من النوم عشان ما أقدرش أتنفس. عندي 75 سنة وعملت قسطرة قلبية من 3 شهور.
urgency_reason: New rest dyspnoea with chest symptoms 3 months after PCI is in-stent restenosis or stent thrombosis — an acute coronary syndrome requiring emergency catheterisation.
recommended_action: Call 999 immediately; ECG and troponin on arrival; aspirin 300mg if not already on; dual antiplatelet therapy review; CXR and BNP; urgent cardiology review; repeat coronary angiography; IV heparin; possible repeat PCI for restenosis; echocardiogram for LV function; ICU if haemodynamically compromised.
red_flags: ST elevation on ECG indicating stent thrombosis requiring emergency PCI within 90 minutes
Cardiogenic shock requiring IABP or mechanical support
Ventricular arrhythmia from acute ischaemia

user_symptom_description: I've had severe pain in my left eye socket that feels like someone is stabbing me, it's been happening every night for the past 2 weeks at exactly 2am. My eyelid droops and my eye is red. Each attack lasts about 45 minutes.
urgency_reason: Strictly nocturnal unilateral orbital pain at the same time each night with autonomic features is a classic cluster headache bout requiring abortive and prophylactic treatment.
recommended_action: Subcutaneous sumatriptan 6mg or 100% oxygen 15L/min via non-rebreather mask for acute attack; intranasal zolmitriptan as alternative; verapamil 240mg daily increasing to 480mg for prophylaxis during cluster period; short prednisolone course as bridge; MRI brain to exclude pituitary tumour; lithium if verapamil ineffective; sleep regularity.
red_flags: New neurological deficit accompanying pain suggesting carotid aneurysm or cavernous sinus lesion
Persistent Horner syndrome between attacks suggesting carotid dissection
First-ever severe headache in patient over 50 requiring CT to exclude SAH

user_symptom_description: ابني عنده 12 سنة وبقاله شهر وعنده تبول لا إرادي في أثناء النهار فجأة وبيقول إن مش حاسس إنه محتاج يبول إلا لما بيكون متأخر جداً. وعنده التهابات في البول كتيرة. ما عنده سكر.
urgency_reason: Daytime urgency incontinence with recurrent UTIs in a child requires urological assessment to exclude structural cause before anticholinergic treatment.
recommended_action: Urine MC&S; renal and bladder ultrasound with post-void residual; urodynamic studies; voiding diary; timed voiding and fluid management; oxybutynin if confirmed OAB; treat concurrent UTI; VCUG if recurrent UTI to exclude VUR; pelvic floor physiotherapy; review in 6 weeks.
red_flags: Duplex kidney or ectopic ureter on ultrasound explaining continuous dribble
Raised creatinine from reflux nephropathy
Spinal dysraphism on examination with sacral dimple and urological symptoms

user_symptom_description: I've been feeling disconnected from my body for the past month like I'm watching myself from outside. I feel like the world around me isn't real. I've had a lot of stress and some panic attacks but this feeling is different and really frightening.
urgency_reason: Chronic depersonalisation and derealisation in the context of anxiety requires neurological exclusion and specific psychological treatment.
recommended_action: EEG to exclude temporal lobe epilepsy; MRI brain; trauma history assessment; CBT focused on acceptance and reducing safety behaviours which perpetuate dissociation; treat underlying panic or PTSD; SSRIs have modest evidence; psychoeducation is key — the experience is distressing but not dangerous; mindfulness-based techniques; avoid cannabis which worsens symptoms.
red_flags: Temporal lobe seizure features with déjà vu and automatisms
Command hallucinations or thought insertion suggesting psychosis
Inability to maintain daily function from severity of dissociation

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع وعندي ألم مفاجئ شديد في بطني والأيمن والأيسر، وبشوف إن بطني منتفخ. وحسيت بالدوخة لما وقفت. عندي 76 سنة وعندي ضغط دم عالي من 20 سنة.
urgency_reason: Sudden severe abdominal pain with distension and haemodynamic instability in a long-term hypertensive elderly man is ruptured AAA — mortality exceeds 80% without immediate surgery.
recommended_action: Call 999 immediately; do not delay for investigations if haemodynamically unstable — direct to vascular surgery; permissive hypotension targeting SBP 50-70 mmHg; two large-bore IV cannulae and rapid transfusion; bedside ultrasound to identify AAA if stable; CT angiography if stable for EVAR assessment; emergency open repair if EVAR not feasible; massive transfusion protocol.
red_flags: Loss of consciousness from haemorrhagic shock — imminent death without surgery
Pulsatile abdominal mass on examination
Extension to iliac vessels causing leg ischaemia

user_symptom_description: I noticed my testicle has been getting gradually larger over the last 2 months. It doesn't really hurt much — just feels heavy. I'm 24 and I felt a hard lump on it when I examined myself.
urgency_reason: Painless progressive testicular enlargement with hard lump in a young male is testicular cancer until proven otherwise requiring urgent scrotal ultrasound and tumour markers.
recommended_action: Urgent scrotal ultrasound — most sensitive first investigation; tumour markers AFP, beta-hCG, LDH; CT chest, abdomen, pelvis for staging; do not biopsy — risk of seeding; radical inguinal orchidectomy is diagnostic and therapeutic; sperm banking before any treatment; staging determines surveillance versus adjuvant BEP chemotherapy or radiotherapy; cure rate above 95% for stage 1.
red_flags: Retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy on CT suggesting advanced stage
Very high HCG suggesting choriocarcinoma with aggressive behaviour
Contralateral testis abnormality requiring biopsy

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهرين وعندي إرهاق شديد وقلبي بيدق بسرعة وبنزل وزني من غير ما أعمل ريجيم. وبحس بحرارة شديدة دايماً وإيدي بترتجف وأنا كاتبة. وعندي عيون بارزة بدأت أحسها.
urgency_reason: Classic Graves' disease with proptosis requires thyroid function tests and TRAb antibodies to confirm autoimmune hyperthyroidism and ophthalmology referral for thyroid eye disease.
