urgency_reason: Classic tinea pedis with concomitant onychomycosis requires nail clipping for mycology confirmation before systemic terbinafine prescription for 12 weeks.
recommended_action: Nail clipping and skin scrapings for KOH microscopy and fungal culture; if Trichophyton rubrum confirmed oral terbinafine 250mg daily for 12 weeks for toenails; topical amorolfine nail lacquer as adjunct; interdigital tinea treated with topical clotrimazole; change socks daily; dry between toes; antifungal powder in shoes; cure rates 70-80% with oral terbinafine; monitor LFTs.
red_flags: Secondary bacterial infection in diabetic feet requiring systemic antibiotics
Failure to respond to terbinafine suggesting non-dermatophyte mold requiring different antifungal
Extensive palmoplantar involvement with blistering suggesting autoimmune disease requiring biopsy

user_symptom_description: بنتي عندها 10 سنين وبقالها 3 أسابيع بتشكي من ألم في قدمها الأيمن تحت الكعب ومش قادرة تمشي كويس. بيزيد لما بتقوم الصبح أو بعد ما تقعد. بتلعب كرة قدم 4 أيام في الأسبوع.
extracted_symptoms: worse on rising and after sitting
urgency_reason: Heel pain with squeeze test tenderness at calcaneal apophysis in a pre-adolescent active girl is Sever's disease, a self-limiting traction apophysitis requiring activity modification and heel cups.
recommended_action: Clinical diagnosis with medial-lateral calcaneal squeeze test; X-ray only if atypical; reduce football to alternate days; heel cups in both shoes; gastrocnemius and Achilles stretching twice daily; ibuprofen for pain flares; ice after exercise; reassure self-limiting with skeletal maturity by age 14; full return to sport when pain resolves.
red_flags: Night pain or constitutional symptoms suggesting osteoid osteoma
Unilateral limb length difference suggesting growth plate disturbance
Persistent pain despite 8 weeks of rest requiring MRI to exclude stress fracture

user_symptom_description: I'm 35 and I've had recurring hives all over my body for three months. They come and go, each patch lasts less than 24 hours but new ones keep appearing. There's no obvious trigger. They're incredibly itchy. I've tried antihistamines from the chemist but they only help a bit.
extracted_symptoms: individual wheals lasting less than 24 hours
urgency_reason: Chronic spontaneous urticaria lasting over 6 weeks with no identifiable trigger requires non-sedating antihistamine at licensed dose and investigation for autoimmune associations.
recommended_action: Urticaria Activity Score UAS7; CBC, CRP, TFTs, anti-TPO antibodies, ANA; non-sedating antihistamine cetirizine 10mg or loratadine 10mg; if inadequate response double dose to 20mg; step up to omalizumab 300mg monthly injection for antihistamine-refractory CSU; skin biopsy only if wheals lasting above 24 hours or purpura to exclude vasculitis; avoid aspirin and NSAIDs.
red_flags: Individual wheals lasting above 24 hours with bruising suggesting urticarial vasculitis requiring biopsy
Angioedema with throat tightness suggesting risk of anaphylaxis requiring adrenaline auto-injector
Weight loss or lymphadenopathy suggesting underlying lymphoma

user_symptom_description: عندي 74 سنة وبقالي 3 أسابيع بحس بألم في منطقة الحوض والفخذين وبيصعب علي القيام من الكرسي أو طلوع السلم. مش عندي ألم في المفاصل بس العضلات ضعيفة ومؤلمة. عندي سكري وعندي ارتفاع في الكوليسترول وبآخد ستاتين.
extracted_symptoms: proximal hip and thigh muscle weakness and pain
difficulty rising from chair and climbing stairs
urgency_reason: Proximal muscle weakness and pain in a patient on statin therapy requires urgent CK measurement as statin myopathy can progress to rhabdomyolysis with renal failure if not identified.
recommended_action: Urgent CK level; TSH to exclude hypothyroidism potentiating statin myopathy; aldolase and LDH; stop statin immediately; repeat CK at 2 weeks; if CK above 10 times normal admit for IV hydration and monitor renal function; switch to pravastatin or rosuvastatin at lower dose when muscle symptoms resolve; consider ezetimibe as statin alternative if intolerant.
red_flags: CK above 10 times normal with myoglobinuria or dark urine suggesting rhabdomyolysis requiring emergency IV fluids
Rapidly ascending weakness suggesting inflammatory myopathy requiring muscle biopsy
Acute kidney injury from myoglobin requiring dialysis

user_symptom_description: I've had a painful red swollen finger for 4 days — the tip of my index finger is throbbing, shiny, and warm. There's a small collection of pus under the skin at the tip. I work as a nurse and had a needle stick last week.
urgency_reason: A felon is a closed-space infection of the fingertip pulp that can cause pressure necrosis of bone and tendon if not surgically drained urgently.
recommended_action: Go to hand surgery or ER today; surgical incision and drainage under digital block; wound culture; oral co-amoxiclav; ensure tetanus is up to date; occupational health review for needle stick HIV and hepatitis risk.
red_flags: Tracking erythema up the hand suggesting spread
Inability to bend the finger suggesting flexor sheath involvement
Signs of osteomyelitis on X-ray

user_symptom_description: عندي 80 سنة وبقالي شهرين بحس بصعوبة في المشي وأنا بابص في الأرض وبستحملش أمشي في الضلمة. كمان بحس إن ثيابي بتلمسني من غير ما أحس بيها كويس.
urgency_reason: Sensory ataxia with proprioception loss and tactile sensory loss in an elderly man is subacute combined degeneration from B12 deficiency until proven otherwise — reversible if treated early.
recommended_action: Urgent serum B12, methylmalonic acid, and homocysteine; MRI cervical and thoracic spine; start IM hydroxocobalamin injections immediately without waiting for results; neurology referral; peripheral nerve conduction studies.
red_flags: Rapidly ascending paralysis
Bladder and bowel incontinence from cord involvement
Dementia symptoms suggesting cerebral B12 deficiency

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوع بحس بألم في بطني من الجانب الأيمن أسفل وبيزيد جداً لو اتحركت أو لو ضغطت عليه. عندي تأخر في دورتي أسبوعين وعمري 22 سنة.
urgency_reason: Right lower quadrant pain with missed period in a woman of childbearing age is ectopic pregnancy until proven otherwise — rupture causes fatal hemorrhage within minutes.
recommended_action: Call emergency services or go to ER immediately; urine and serum beta-hCG; transvaginal ultrasound; IV access and crossmatch; gynecology surgical team on standby; laparoscopy if ectopic confirmed.
red_flags: Sudden severe pain with hemodynamic collapse indicating rupture
Shoulder tip pain from diaphragmatic irritation by blood
Falling beta-hCG without intrauterine sac on ultrasound

user_symptom_description: I've been on a construction site for years and my hearing has been getting worse gradually. I find it hard to follow conversations in noisy places and my wife says I have the TV volume too loud. I have constant ringing in both ears.
urgency_reason: Chronic noise-induced hearing loss is irreversible but progressive worsening can be halted with hearing protection — audiological assessment and hearing aids are the priority.
recommended_action: Audiometry and pure tone audiogram; ENT referral; fitted hearing aids if moderate loss confirmed; mandatory ear protection at work; occupational health referral for workplace noise risk assessment; tinnitus retraining therapy.
red_flags: Sudden unilateral worsening suggesting superimposed SSNHL
Asymmetric hearing loss requiring MRI to exclude acoustic neuroma
Balance problems developing alongside hearing loss

user_symptom_description: ابني عمره 6 سنين وبقاله 3 أيام عنده ورم في رقبته من جانب الأيسر وحرارة 38.5 وبيشكي من وجع في بلعه. أنا لاحظت إن وشه بقى منتفخ شوية من الجانب ده.
urgency_reason: Parotid swelling with fever in a child requires evaluation to distinguish viral mumps from bacterial parotitis and exclude complications such as abscess or orchitis.
recommended_action: Same-day pediatric appointment; check MMR vaccination status; serum amylase and mumps IgM; if bacterial, start co-amoxiclav; ultrasound if abscess suspected; isolate from unvaccinated contacts if mumps confirmed.
red_flags: Trismus with deviation of uvula suggesting parapharyngeal abscess
Scrotal pain in a post-pubertal boy suggesting orchitis
Severe headache and neck stiffness suggesting meningeal involvement

user_symptom_description: I've had a painless non-healing sore on my lower lip for 6 weeks. It started as a small bump and has grown into a crater-like ulcer with raised hard edges. I'm 58 and I smoked for 25 years.
urgency_reason: Indurated non-healing lip ulcer lasting over 3 weeks in an elderly smoker is oral cancer until proven otherwise requiring urgent biopsy.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week cancer pathway referral to oral and maxillofacial surgery; incisional biopsy; CT neck for lymphadenopathy staging; avoid further sun exposure to lips; SYPHILIS serology to exclude chancre.
red_flags: Palpable submandibular or cervical lymph nodes
Jaw involvement or loosening of teeth
Trismus suggesting deep tissue invasion

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أشهر بحس بضعف وتعب وبشوف أصفر في كل حاجة. قلبي بيرفرف أحياناً وبيجيلي دوخة. بيجيلي صفرة في جلدي وعيناي بعض الأوقات.
urgency_reason: Yellow vision with jaundice and palpitations in an elderly woman on cardiac medications raises digoxin toxicity as a dangerous possibility requiring urgent drug level testing.
recommended_action: Go to ER today; digoxin level, ECG, potassium, and renal function urgently; FBC and reticulocyte count; LFTs and bilirubin; B12 and folate; Coombs test; withhold digoxin if toxic level confirmed.
red_flags: ECG showing bidirectional ventricular tachycardia from digoxin toxicity
Hyperkalemia from digoxin toxicity
Hemoglobin below 7 requiring transfusion

user_symptom_description: I've been feeling extremely grandiose and energetic for the past 10 days. I've been sleeping only 2 hours a night and feel fine. I've spent $15,000 on a business idea and I feel like I can achieve anything. My family thinks something is very wrong.
urgency_reason: Acute mania with dangerous impulsivity, reckless spending, and severely reduced sleep requires urgent psychiatric assessment and mood stabilizer initiation to prevent further harm.
recommended_action: Urgent same-day psychiatry assessment; consider voluntary or involuntary admission if insight absent; start lithium or valproate; urine toxicology to exclude stimulant use; thyroid function tests; family support and psychoeducation.
red_flags: Psychotic features such as delusions of grandeur or hallucinations
Aggressive or dangerous behavior
Complete sleep deprivation exceeding 72 hours causing medical risk

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بحس بألم في ضهري أسفل وعلى جنب أيمن وبيزيد لما أضغط على منطقة الكلية. عندي حمى وبولي عكر وبيوجع. عندي كلية وحدة بس من عملية.
urgency_reason: Pyelonephritis in a patient with a single kidney is a renal emergency — loss of this kidney means dialysis dependence, requiring immediate IV antibiotics and urgent urological imaging.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; IV ceftriaxone before cultures; urine and blood cultures; urgent renal ultrasound and CT KUB; urology emergency consultation; monitor eGFR every 6 hours; nephrology co-management.
red_flags: Rising creatinine indicating acute kidney injury in the only kidney
Obstruction requiring emergency nephrostomy
Septic shock with hypotension requiring ICU

user_symptom_description: I've had a painful swollen knee for 2 days with no injury. The joint is hot, very red, and so painful I can't put any weight on it. I have a fever of 102°F. I had a knee replacement 2 years ago.
urgency_reason: Acute hot joint with fever in a patient with a prosthetic knee is infected prosthesis until proven otherwise — delayed washout risks losing the implant and systemic sepsis.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; blood cultures before antibiotics; urgent orthopedic surgery consultation; joint aspiration for cell count, Gram stain, and culture; IV vancomycin empirically; surgical washout or implant revision as indicated.
red_flags: Sinus tract or discharge from the wound overlying the prosthesis
Hemodynamic instability from sepsis
Rapid joint destruction on X-ray

user_symptom_description: عندي 77 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين بحس بصعوبة في البلع والأكل بيوقف في الحنجرة وبرجع يطلع لوحده أحياناً. وزني نزل 7 كيلو. مش عندي ألم.
urgency_reason: Regurgitation of undigested food with weight loss requires barium swallow to distinguish Zenker's diverticulum from esophageal malignancy — both require urgent specialist management.
recommended_action: Urgent barium swallow; never perform rigid endoscopy without knowing the diagnosis due to perforation risk; CT neck and chest; ENT and upper GI surgical referral; endoscopic stapling if Zenker's confirmed; nutritional support.
red_flags: Aspiration pneumonia from overnight regurgitation
Rapid weight loss suggesting malignancy
Neck swelling suggesting pouch enlargement or spread

user_symptom_description: I've been having episodes where my face, lips, and tongue swell suddenly for no reason. It's happened 5 times in the past 3 months. No hives or rash. It resolves on its own in about 24 hours. I'm not on any medications.
urgency_reason: Recurrent non-histaminergic angioedema without urticaria requires C1-inhibitor level and function testing to exclude hereditary angioedema, which can cause fatal laryngeal edema.
recommended_action: Immunology referral; C4, C1-inhibitor level and function, and C1q; family history assessment; carry icatibant auto-injector as emergency treatment; avoid triggers like trauma, stress, and estrogen-containing contraceptives.
red_flags: Laryngeal edema with stridor requiring emergency icatibant or C1-INH concentrate
Abdominal swelling episode mimicking surgical abdomen
Family history of deaths from angioedema

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 28 سنة ولاعب كرة قدم. أمس خلال المباراة حسيت بفرقعة في ركبتي اليسرى وانتفخت على طول وما قدرتش أكمل. الركبة مش ثابتة لما بحاول أمشي.
urgency_reason: Acute hemarthrosis with instability after a non-contact pivot injury is ACL rupture in over 70% of cases, requiring MRI confirmation and early orthopedic planning.
recommended_action: Go to orthopedic or sports medicine clinic within 48 hours; knee X-ray to exclude fracture; MRI knee for soft tissue assessment; RICE protocol; knee brace; physiotherapy; ACL reconstruction discussion for young active patients.
red_flags: Locked knee indicating displaced meniscal tear requiring urgent arthroscopy
Vascular injury with cold pale foot
Multi-ligament injury suggesting knee dislocation

user_symptom_description: I've had a itchy, scaly, red rash in a ring pattern on my inner thigh for 3 weeks. It's spreading outward with a clearer center. I go to the gym daily and use a shared towel sometimes.
urgency_reason: Ring-shaped scaly rash with central clearing in the groin after gym exposure is classic tinea cruris responding to topical antifungal treatment.
recommended_action: Topical terbinafine cream twice daily for 2-4 weeks; keep area dry; wear loose breathable underwear; use personal towels; treat concurrent tinea pedis to prevent reinfection; Wood's lamp if erythrasma suspected.
red_flags: Rash spreading to genitals or perianal area
No response to topical antifungal at 4 weeks requiring skin scraping for culture
Pustular or nodular lesions suggesting Majocchi's granuloma

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر كل ما بنام، نفسي بيقطع وبصحى مذعورة وزوجي قالي إني بوقف أتنفس فترات وبيصحيني. بقيت متعبة طول اليوم وصداعي بالصبح.
urgency_reason: Witnessed apneas with morning headache and daytime sleepiness is OSA requiring polysomnography and CPAP therapy to prevent cardiovascular complications.
recommended_action: Refer for overnight polysomnography or home sleep apnea test; Epworth Sleepiness Scale; CPAP titration if AHI above 15; weight loss counseling; avoid alcohol and sedatives; lateral sleeping position; ECG and BP check for cardiovascular complications.
red_flags: Witnessed apneas lasting over 30 seconds
Nocturnal arrhythmias on Holter monitoring
Cor pulmonale with peripheral edema from chronic hypoxia

user_symptom_description: I was doing DIY at home and a metal shard from grinding hit my right eye at high speed. I can feel something in there, my vision is blurred, and the eye is very painful and tearing constantly.
urgency_reason: High-velocity metal particle from grinding has a high probability of penetrating the globe and entering the eye — open globe injury requires emergency surgical exploration.
recommended_action: Go to ophthalmology emergency immediately; do not rub or patch with pressure; shield the eye with a rigid cup; CT orbits to locate metal fragment; surgical exploration and removal; tetanus booster; IV antibiotics to prevent endophthalmitis.
red_flags: Visible uveal tissue prolapse — open globe confirmed
Hypotony of the eye on examination
Vitreous hemorrhage obscuring fundal view

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بيجيلي رعشة في إيديا وأنا بشرب أو بآكل، مش في الراحة. وبيجيلي كمان رعشة في صوتي لما بتكلم. بتحسنت لما شربت كوباية نبيذ في حفلة.
urgency_reason: Action tremor with voice involvement improving with alcohol is classic essential tremor; Wilson's disease must be excluded in young adults before starting propranolol.
recommended_action: Neurology appointment; serum ceruloplasmin and 24-hour urine copper to exclude Wilson's disease in young adults; start propranolol 40mg BD; primidone if propranolol fails; deep brain stimulation for severe refractory cases.
red_flags: Liver disease signs such as jaundice or hepatomegaly
Kayser-Fleischer rings on slit-lamp examination
Psychiatric symptoms alongside the tremor

user_symptom_description: I have HIV and I've been on antiretrovirals for 5 years. For the past month I've been having severe headaches, confusion, and last week I had a seizure at home. My CD4 count was 85 at my last visit.
urgency_reason: Seizure with confusion in an HIV patient with CD4 below 100 is a CNS opportunistic infection emergency requiring immediate MRI and empirical anti-toxoplasma therapy.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; MRI brain with gadolinium urgently; Toxoplasma IgG, cryptococcal antigen, and lumbar puncture if no mass effect; start pyrimethamine plus sulfadiazine empirically; seizure prophylaxis; ID and neurology co-management.
red_flags: Multiple ring-enhancing lesions on MRI suggesting toxoplasmosis or lymphoma
Herniation signs from mass effect
CSF India ink positive indicating cryptococcal meningitis

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 69 سنة وبقالي شهر ووجهي من الجانبين بيحس بتيبس وألم صباحي لمدة ساعتين وعيون جافة وفم جاف. الأعراض بتتحسن في اليوم بعد ما أتحرك.
extracted_symptoms: morning stiffness 2 hours improving with activity
urgency_reason: Bilateral sicca symptoms with inflammatory morning stiffness in an elderly woman requires autoimmune panel to diagnose Sjögren's and differentiate from PMR.
recommended_action: Rheumatology referral; ANA, anti-Ro, anti-La, RF, ESR, and CRP; Schirmer's test; minor salivary gland biopsy; dental review for caries risk from dry mouth; hydroxychloroquine; pilocarpine for sicca symptoms.
red_flags: Parotid enlargement suggesting lymphoma transformation
Renal involvement with proteinuria
Peripheral neuropathy or vasculitic skin lesions

user_symptom_description: I've been waking up with a very stiff and painful lower back every morning for the past 4 months. The stiffness lasts about 2 hours but eases with exercise. I'm 32, and the pain gets worse when I rest and better when I'm active. My right eye has also been red and painful twice.
urgency_reason: Inflammatory back pain with uveitis in a young male meets clinical criteria for ankylosing spondylitis requiring MRI sacroiliac joints and HLA-B27 testing to prevent irreversible spinal fusion.
recommended_action: Rheumatology referral; HLA-B27; MRI sacroiliac joints; CRP and ESR; start NSAIDs as first-line; TNF inhibitors such as adalimumab or etanercept for refractory disease; ophthalmology for uveitis management; physiotherapy.
red_flags: Bamboo spine on X-ray indicating advanced fusion
Spinal fracture from minor trauma through fused vertebrae
Acute uveitis with vision loss requiring steroid drops

user_symptom_description: بنتي عمرها 4 سنين ومن يومين بطنها منتفخ جداً وبتبكي من الألم وما عندهاش براز من 4 أيام. بشيل كلها وهي بتبكي من الوجع.
urgency_reason: Severe abdominal distension with persistent pain and 4-day constipation in a toddler requires urgent evaluation to exclude volvulus or intussusception before treating fecal impaction.
recommended_action: Go to pediatric ER or urgent care today; abdominal X-ray to assess stool burden and exclude obstruction; if impaction, polyethylene glycol or rectal disimpaction; if volvulus or intussusception suspected, urgent surgical consult; rectal examination by pediatrician.
red_flags: Bilious vomiting suggesting complete obstruction
Absent bowel sounds suggesting ileus
Peritoneal signs with rigid abdomen

user_symptom_description: I have a known peanut allergy and accidentally ate something with peanuts at a restaurant 20 minutes ago. I already have hives on my trunk and my lips are tingling. I have my EpiPen with me.
urgency_reason: Urticaria with lip tingling 20 minutes after peanut ingestion in a known allergic patient is early anaphylaxis — symptoms can progress to airway involvement within minutes.
recommended_action: Use EpiPen into outer thigh NOW without delay; call emergency services; lie flat with legs raised unless breathing is difficult; second EpiPen at 5 minutes if no improvement; go to ER for 4-hour observation for biphasic reaction.
red_flags: Throat tightening or change in voice
Wheezing or stridor
Pale clammy skin with rapid weak pulse indicating shock

user_symptom_description: عندي 81 سنة وبقالي أسبوع بحس إن الأشياء بتتحرك من تلقاءها وبيجيلي رعشة في إيديا وأنا مش متحركهم. ابني لاحظ إن مشيتي بقت صغيرة الخطوات وبنني تعبت.
urgency_reason: Visual hallucinations combined with Parkinsonism in an elderly man is Lewy body dementia until proven otherwise — neuroleptic medication is dangerous in this condition and must be avoided.
recommended_action: Urgent neurology referral; DaTSCAN; MRI brain; full medication review to exclude drug-induced cause; NEVER give haloperidol or typical antipsychotics; rivastigmine for cognition; low-dose quetiapine only if hallucinations distressing.
red_flags: Administration of haloperidol causing severe neuroleptic sensitivity reaction
Rapid cognitive decline over weeks suggesting CJD
Falls from postural instability requiring home safety assessment

user_symptom_description: I've been having episodes of uncontrollable shaking and convulsing that look like seizures for 6 months. They happen when I'm stressed. My EEG and MRI were both completely normal and the neurologist says it's not epilepsy.
urgency_reason: Recurrent dissociative seizures with normal neurological investigations require specialist psychological treatment — ongoing misdiagnosis leads to unnecessary antiepileptic drugs and harm.
recommended_action: Referral to neuropsychiatry or specialist FND service; video-EEG monitoring to capture an episode; trauma-focused CBT and physiotherapy; explain diagnosis compassionately; do not give antiepileptic drugs; address underlying psychological trauma.
red_flags: Tongue biting during episodes — rare in dissociative seizures, reconsider epilepsy
Incontinence in every episode
Postictal neurological deficit requiring urgent re-evaluation

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهر بحس بألم خلف عظمة العقب بيزيد لما بركض أو بقوم على رؤوس أصابعي. الألم بيجيلي في أول خطواتي الصبح وبيتحسن. بقيت أركض 15 كيلو في الأسبوع من شهرين.
extracted_symptoms: pain with running and toe standing
urgency_reason: Insertional Achilles pain from rapid training volume increase responds to load management and eccentric exercises but not traditional drop heel stretches which worsen insertional tendinopathy.
recommended_action: Reduce running volume by 50%; isometric heel raises for pain relief; sports physiotherapy with Alfredson modified protocol avoiding full drop off step; heel lift in shoes; extracorporeal shock wave therapy at 12 weeks if no improvement; avoid corticosteroid injection into Achilles.
red_flags: Sudden severe pain with audible pop suggesting complete Achilles rupture
Swelling proximal to insertion suggesting mid-portion rupture
Inability to perform any plantarflexion

user_symptom_description: My daughter brought me in because I have been wetting myself during the day without warning for the past 2 months. I had a stroke 6 months ago affecting my right side and I've been using a walking frame since.
urgency_reason: New incontinence after a stroke is a recognized neurogenic complication requiring continence assessment and management to restore dignity and prevent skin breakdown.
recommended_action: Urine culture to exclude infection; continence nurse assessment; prompted voiding schedule every 2 hours; pelvic floor exercises adapted for mobility; mirabegron preferred over anticholinergics to avoid cognitive side effects post-stroke; bladder diary.
red_flags: Complete urinary retention from spinal cord involvement
Recurrent UTIs from incomplete emptying
Skin breakdown from persistent moisture

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 45 سنة ومن 6 أشهر بيجيلي صداع كل يوم تقريباً وبآخد مسكنات كتير جداً، أكتر من 15 يوم في الشهر. الصداع مش بيروح تماماً حتى بالمسكنات.
extracted_symptoms: analgesic use over 15 days per month
urgency_reason: Daily headache with analgesic overuse over 15 days monthly is medication overuse headache — the only cure is supervised analgesic withdrawal which temporarily worsens headache before improving.
recommended_action: Neurology referral; headache diary; supervised analgesic withdrawal over 4-8 weeks; start topiramate or amitriptyline prophylaxis; bridge therapy with naproxen or prednisolone during withdrawal; patient education that overuse perpetuates headache.
red_flags: Thunderclap onset headache during analgesic withdrawal
Neurological symptoms accompanying headache requiring MRI
Failure of multiple prophylactics requiring specialist inpatient detoxification

user_symptom_description: My 5-year-old woke up this morning and his whole face is swollen, especially around his eyes so much he can barely open them. His belly also looks puffy. He had a sore throat 2 weeks ago. There's no pain or fever.
urgency_reason: Massive periorbital edema with abdominal distension after a throat infection in a child is nephrotic syndrome requiring urgent urine protein testing and pediatric nephrology assessment.
recommended_action: Go to pediatric ER today; urine dipstick for protein 3+ or 4+; serum albumin, cholesterol, and renal function; 24-hour urine protein; start prednisolone 60mg/m2 after nephrology review; restrict salt; monitor BP; low-molecular-weight heparin for thrombosis risk.
red_flags: Severe hypoalbuminemia below 15 g/L with respiratory distress from pleural effusion
Peritonitis from spontaneous bacterial peritonitis
Thromboembolism from hypercoagulable state

user_symptom_description: I've been experiencing episodes where my skin suddenly turns very pale then blue then red on my fingers when I go outside in cold weather. It's painful and the fingers go numb during the attack. I've had this for a year.
possible_conditions: Secondary Raynaud's from Connective Tissue Disease
urgency_reason: Classic triphasic color change with cold trigger is Raynaud's phenomenon; autoimmune screening is necessary to distinguish primary from secondary Raynaud's in connective tissue disease.
recommended_action: ANA, anti-Scl-70, anti-centromere, and nailfold capillaroscopy; warm layered clothing and gloves; avoid cold and smoking; nifedipine 10mg extended-release if frequent episodes; topical glyceryl trinitrate paste for severe attacks.
red_flags: Digital ulcers suggesting secondary Raynaud's from systemic sclerosis
Gangrene of fingertips
Sclerodactyly or CREST features requiring scleroderma workup

user_symptom_description: عندي 76 سنة وبقالي 3 أسابيع بحس بألم حاد في بطني الأيسر وبيجيلي عرق ليلي وحرارة خفيفة. الألم بيزيد لو ضغطت على جنبي الأيسر.
urgency_reason: Left abdominal pain with fever and night sweats in an elderly man requires CT abdomen to exclude complicated diverticulitis, splenic abscess, or malignancy.
recommended_action: Go to ER or urgent care today; CT abdomen-pelvis with contrast; CBC, CRP, and blood cultures; IV antibiotics if diverticulitis or abscess confirmed; interventional radiology drainage for abscess; surgical review; colonoscopy after 6-week recovery.
red_flags: Peritoneal signs suggesting free perforation
Septic shock from abscess rupture
Weight loss suggesting underlying colonic malignancy

user_symptom_description: I've had a high fever of 104°F, severe sore throat, and complete exhaustion for the past week. My neck glands are massively swollen and my spleen feels tender when I press on the left side of my abdomen.
urgency_reason: Splenomegaly with fever and lymphadenopathy in a young adult is infectious mononucleosis with splenic enlargement — there is a risk of splenic rupture with any abdominal trauma.
recommended_action: Monospot test and EBV IgM; CBC with differential for atypical lymphocytes; LFTs for hepatitis; abdominal ultrasound to quantify spleen size; avoid all contact sports and heavy lifting for 4 weeks minimum; no amoxicillin — causes rash; rest and paracetamol.
red_flags: Sudden severe left upper quadrant pain suggesting splenic rupture — surgical emergency
Airway compromise from massive tonsillar enlargement
Jaundice from hepatic involvement

user_symptom_description: عندي 50 سنة وبقالي سنة وأنا بحس بوخز وتنميل في قدمي الشمال في القدم وأحياناً بيطلع لأعلى. الألم زي الحرقان. عندي قدم مسطحة وبيزيد لو وقفت ساعات.
urgency_reason: Medial plantar burning with flat foot and prolonged standing suggests tarsal tunnel nerve entrapment requiring nerve conduction studies to confirm before orthotic intervention.
recommended_action: Nerve conduction studies of posterior tibial nerve; medial arch support orthotics for flat foot; anti-inflammatory medication; corticosteroid injection into tarsal tunnel; surgical decompression if conservative management fails at 6 months.
red_flags: Rapidly progressive weakness of intrinsic foot muscles
Bilateral symptoms suggesting systemic neuropathy requiring metabolic workup
Mass in tarsal tunnel on ultrasound requiring excision

user_symptom_description: My 13-year-old daughter has had a swollen painful right knee for 3 days with no injury. She has a fever of 102°F and is refusing to walk. She had a throat infection last month that was treated with antibiotics.
urgency_reason: Hot migratory joint with fever following a streptococcal infection meets Jones criteria for rheumatic fever requiring cardiac assessment to exclude valvular damage.
recommended_action: Go to pediatric ER; ASO titer, anti-DNase B, throat swab, and CRP; ECG for prolonged PR interval; echocardiogram to assess valves; benzylpenicillin IV; aspirin for arthritis; cardiology review; long-term penicillin prophylaxis if cardiac involvement.
red_flags: New cardiac murmur suggesting mitral regurgitation from carditis
Joint aspiration showing turbid fluid with high WBC suggesting septic arthritis
Chorea involuntary movements indicating Sydenham's chorea

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بيجيلي نوبات من الخفقان والتعرق الشديد والقلق والصداع وارتفاع الضغط اللي بقيسه في الصيدلية. الضغط بيطلع 200/110 وقت النوبة.
urgency_reason: Paroxysmal hypertension with the triad of headache, sweating, and palpitations is pheochromocytoma until proven otherwise — a hypertensive crisis can cause stroke or myocardial infarction.
recommended_action: Urgent 24-hour urine metanephrines and catecholamines; plasma free metanephrines; CT or MRI adrenal glands; avoid beta-blockers before alpha-blockade; start phenoxybenzamine; surgical adrenalectomy after adequate alpha-blockade.
red_flags: Hypertensive emergency above 180/120 with end-organ damage requiring IV labetalol after alpha-blockade
Hypertensive crisis during surgical manipulation of tumor
Bilateral adrenal masses suggesting MEN2 syndrome

user_symptom_description: I've had a very itchy rash all over my body for 3 weeks. Looking closely I can see tiny burrow lines between my fingers and on my wrists. My wife has also started itching in the last week and my grandson visited recently.
extracted_symptoms: burrow lines between fingers and wrists
urgency_reason: Classic scabies with burrows and household contact spread requires simultaneous treatment of all household members to break the transmission cycle.
recommended_action: Treat all household contacts simultaneously even if asymptomatic; permethrin 5% cream from neck to toes overnight for 8 hours, repeat in 1 week; wash all bedding and clothing at 60°C; chlorphenamine for itch at night; itching may persist 4-6 weeks after treatment.
red_flags: Crusted Norwegian scabies in immunocompromised patient requiring oral ivermectin
Secondary impetiginization with cellulitis requiring antibiotics
No improvement after two permethrin treatments suggesting resistance

user_symptom_description: بقالي 4 أيام ألم شديد في بطني من فوق في المنتصف وإقياء مستمر وما أقدرش أكل. بيجيلي كتير بعد الأكل. عندي حصوات مرارة عارفاها من أشعة من قبل.
urgency_reason: Persistent epigastric pain with vomiting in a patient with known gallstones is biliary pancreatitis until proven otherwise — requires immediate hospitalization and serum lipase.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; nil by mouth; serum amylase and lipase; LFTs and bilirubin; abdominal ultrasound and CT with contrast; IV fluids and morphine; ERCP if CBD stone; cholecystectomy during same admission for gallstone pancreatitis.
red_flags: Cullen's or Grey Turner's sign indicating hemorrhagic pancreatitis
Fever with rigors suggesting infected necrosis
Ranson's criteria score above 3 requiring ICU admission

user_symptom_description: I've been having a persistent cough for 3 months that produces small amounts of blood. I've also lost about 7kg without trying and wake up drenched in night sweats. I recently moved from a country with high TB rates.
urgency_reason: Constitutional symptoms with hemoptysis and TB risk factors requires immediate sputum AFB smear and chest X-ray — TB is a notifiable disease and infection control is essential.
recommended_action: Respiratory isolation immediately; chest X-ray; sputum AFB smear x3 and culture; IGRA blood test; notify public health authority; start HRZE quadruple therapy if smear positive; directly observed therapy; contact tracing for household members.
red_flags: Massive hemoptysis over 200ml requiring bronchial artery embolization
MDR-TB on culture requiring specialist treatment
Miliary TB pattern on chest X-ray indicating disseminated disease

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهرين بيجيلي إحساس بحرقة وألم في عيني الإتنين وبيدمعوا كتير وبيحسوا جافين في نفس الوقت. الشاشة والضو بيزيدوا الألم. بشتغل 10 ساعات على الكمبيوتر.
urgency_reason: Chronic dry eye with screen work is common and manageable with lubricants and screen hygiene but autoimmune causes should be excluded given bilateral nature.
recommended_action: Preservative-free artificial tears every 2-3 hours; 20-20-20 rule: look 20 feet away every 20 minutes for 20 seconds; screen brightness reduction and anti-glare filter; warm lid compress for blepharitis; ANA and anti-Ro to exclude Sjögren's; consider punctal plugs if severe.
red_flags: Corneal ulceration from severe dry eye
Reduced visual acuity
Systemic sicca symptoms suggesting Sjögren's

user_symptom_description: My 80-year-old mother has not passed urine for the past 12 hours and her lower abdomen looks very swollen and hard. She is in obvious discomfort and keeps holding her lower abdomen. She has dementia and can't tell us what's wrong.
urgency_reason: Acute urinary retention with bladder distension is a urological emergency causing severe pain even in dementia — catheterization must not be delayed.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; bedside bladder scan to confirm retention; urethral catheterization and measure residual volume; review all anticholinergic medications; renal function test; pelvic ultrasound to exclude mass; urology review if recurrent.
red_flags: Urine output resuming with very high residual over 1000ml suggesting overflow
Post-obstructive diuresis requiring close fluid balance monitoring
Rising creatinine from bilateral hydronephrosis

user_symptom_description: أنا عندي 36 سنة وبقالي 3 أشهر بحس بألم في صدري زي الحزام يلف حوالين جسمي من الظهر لقدام وبيزيد بالأكل. وزني نزل ومش عندي شهية.
urgency_reason: Band-like back to front pain with weight loss and anorexia in a middle-aged man is pancreatic malignancy until excluded by urgent CT and tumor marker assessment.
recommended_action: Urgent CT abdomen-pelvis with contrast and MRCP; CA 19-9 and CEA; LFTs; new-onset diabetes assessment; urgent gastroenterology referral on cancer pathway; ERCP for biliary stenting if obstructed; nutritional support.
red_flags: Painless jaundice developing
New onset diabetes in a thin middle-aged person
Migratory thrombophlebitis suggesting Trousseau syndrome from pancreatic cancer

user_symptom_description: My 4-year-old has been crying and holding his right ear for 2 hours. He suddenly started screaming and I can see something small and round in his ear canal that looks like a bead from his toy set.
urgency_reason: Aural foreign body in a toddler requires urgent ENT removal under proper visualization — home removal attempts can push the object deeper or perforate the eardrum.
recommended_action: Go to pediatric ER or ENT urgently; do NOT attempt home removal with cotton buds; if button battery suspected go immediately as it causes chemical burns within hours; ENT removal under direct vision with hook or suction; check tympanic membrane integrity after removal.
red_flags: Button battery — chemical necrosis begins within 1 hour
Otorrhea or blood suggesting tympanic membrane perforation
Child in severe distress suggesting object pressing on eardrum

user_symptom_description: I've had a painful, swollen, red area on my left shin that has been spreading over the past 3 days. The redness has clear advancing edges. I have no open wound there. I'm diabetic and the area feels warm and hard.
extracted_symptoms: left shin erythema with advancing edges
possible_conditions: Deep Vein Thrombosis with Skin Changes
urgency_reason: Rapidly advancing erysipelas in a diabetic patient requires systemic antibiotics and close monitoring for necrotizing fasciitis, which can be fatal if missed.
recommended_action: Same-day GP or urgent care; mark the erythema borders with pen; oral penicillin V or co-amoxiclav; if not improving in 24 hours, go to ER for IV benzylpenicillin; elevate leg; check glucose control; Doppler ultrasound to exclude DVT.
red_flags: Skin blistering, necrosis, or crepitus suggesting necrotizing fasciitis requiring emergency surgery
Fever above 39°C with systemic sepsis
Spreading faster than one centimeter per hour despite antibiotics

user_symptom_description: عندي 73 سنة وبقالي شهر بحس بضعف شديد وإرهاق ولون وجهي اتصفر. أكلي عادي وما عنديش نزيف. بعد فحص الدم قالوا إن هيموجلوبين بتاعي 7.
urgency_reason: Unexplained severe anemia with jaundice tinge in an elderly man without bleeding requires urgent blood film and bone marrow assessment to exclude myelodysplastic syndrome or leukemia.
recommended_action: Urgent hematology referral; blood film morphology; reticulocyte count; B12, folate, LDH, and Coombs test; bone marrow biopsy if MDS suspected; consider transfusion if symptomatic; erythropoietin-stimulating agents or azacitidine depending on IPSS risk score.
red_flags: Neutropenia with infection susceptibility from bone marrow failure
Transformation to acute myeloid leukemia
Platelet count below 20 with bleeding risk

user_symptom_description: I've had a high fever, severe sore throat, and a widespread red rash for 2 days. The rash feels rough like sandpaper, started on my trunk, and my tongue looks like a strawberry. I have no cough.
urgency_reason: Scarlet fever in an adult requires antibiotic treatment to prevent post-streptococcal complications including rheumatic fever and glomerulonephritis.
recommended_action: Same-day GP appointment; throat swab; start penicillin V 500mg four times daily for 10 days; paracetamol for fever; rest; work exclusion until 24 hours on antibiotics; contact notify close contacts.
red_flags: Toxic shock features with hypotension and multi-organ failure
Peritonsillar abscess with muffled voice and trismus
Swallowing such pain as to prevent medication intake

user_symptom_description: بقالي 6 أشهر بحس بحرقة وألم في مؤخرتي وبيجيلي دم في البراز أحمر فاتح على ورق الحمام. الألم بيزيد بعد التبرز وبيفضل ساعات. عمري 35 سنة.
extracted_symptoms: bright red rectal bleeding on paper
urgency_reason: Chronic anal fissure with post-defecation burning is a benign condition but chronic fissures require topical treatment to relax the internal sphincter and allow healing.
recommended_action: Topical glyceryl trinitrate 0.4% ointment twice daily for 8 weeks; stool softener and high-fibre diet; warm sitz baths after defecation; topical lidocaine before defecation; botulinum toxin injection if topical therapy fails; lateral internal sphincterotomy for chronic fissures.
red_flags: Pain with perianal swelling and fever suggesting abscess requiring drainage
Dark blood mixed in stool rather than on paper suggesting higher bleeding source
Fissure in unusual position suggesting Crohn's or anal cancer

user_symptom_description: I've been having episodes where I suddenly see flashing lights and then completely lose vision in my left eye for about 10 minutes before it returns to normal. This has happened 3 times in the last 2 weeks. I'm 72 with high blood pressure.
urgency_reason: Transient monocular vision loss is a TIA equivalent with the highest stroke risk in the next 48-72 hours — this is a neurological emergency even though vision has returned.
recommended_action: Go to ER or stroke unit immediately; carotid Doppler ultrasound urgently; MRI DWI brain; start aspirin 300mg now; ECG for AF; ESR and CRP to exclude giant cell arteritis; carotid endarterectomy within 2 weeks if stenosis above 70%.
red_flags: Permanent vision loss from central retinal artery occlusion requiring emergency ophthalmology
Scalp tenderness and jaw claudication from GCA requiring immediate high-dose steroids
Concurrent limb weakness indicating anterior circulation TIA

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أسابيع عندي إفرازات من حلمتي الأيسر من غير حمل أو رضاعة. الإفراز بني داكن وبيجي لوحده. عمري 38 سنة.
urgency_reason: Spontaneous unilateral blood-stained nipple discharge requires urgent triple assessment to exclude breast malignancy regardless of the patient's age.
recommended_action: Urgent 2-week cancer pathway breast clinic referral; mammogram and breast ultrasound; nipple discharge cytology; ductoscopy or microdochectomy if papilloma confirmed; prolactin level to exclude prolactinoma; MRI breast if inconclusive.
red_flags: Associated breast lump or skin change
Bilateral bloody discharge requiring pituitary MRI
Nipple retraction developing alongside discharge

user_symptom_description: My 2-year-old has been limping and refusing to walk on his right leg for a day. He doesn't seem to be in severe pain but just won't use the leg. He had a minor fall yesterday from a low couch and cried briefly. No swelling.
urgency_reason: An undisplaced spiral tibial fracture from minor twisting trauma in a toddler can be invisible on initial X-ray but must be treated to prevent ongoing injury.
recommended_action: Pediatric ER visit; X-ray tibia AP and lateral — may appear normal initially; if high clinical suspicion, below-knee cast for 3 weeks and repeat X-ray; check for other unexplained injuries; safeguarding assessment if mechanism inconsistent.
red_flags: Mechanism inconsistent with injury pattern suggesting non-accidental injury
Multiple fractures at different healing stages
Fever with complete refusal to move leg suggesting septic arthritis

user_symptom_description: I've had trouble getting or maintaining an erection for the past year. It happens about 80% of the time. I'm 52, I smoke, have type 2 diabetes, and high blood pressure. My relationship is fine and I'm not stressed.
urgency_reason: Erectile dysfunction in a diabetic smoker with cardiovascular risk factors is often the first symptom of generalized atherosclerosis and warrants cardiovascular risk assessment alongside treatment.
recommended_action: Testosterone, LH, prolactin, HbA1c, and lipids; review antihypertensives for alpha-blockers; sildenafil 50mg or tadalafil 10mg as first-line; lifestyle modification including smoking cessation; cardiovascular risk stratification; penile Doppler if no response to PDE5 inhibitors.
red_flags: Exertional chest pain suggesting cardiac ischemia — treat before using PDE5 inhibitors
Rapidly progressive ED with mass on testosterone suggesting pituitary tumor
Priapism if PDE5 inhibitor dose is too high

user_symptom_description: بقالي أسبوعين بيجيلي ألم في رأسي وأنا باتكأ لقدام وبحس بامتلاء في أذني الإتنين وطنين. بقالي 3 أيام وأنا مصابة بزكام قوي.
urgency_reason: Post-viral Eustachian tube dysfunction causing ear fullness and tinnitus typically resolves spontaneously but requires ENT review if persisting beyond 3 months.
recommended_action: Nasal saline irrigation; intranasal steroid spray for 4-6 weeks; decongestant for 3 days only; Valsalva maneuver; avoid flying until resolved; reassess in 4-6 weeks; ENT referral if persisting for glue ear assessment.
red_flags: Unilateral conductive hearing loss not resolving suggesting effusion needing grommets
Facial palsy developing
Symptoms persisting over 3 months in an adult requiring nasopharyngoscopy to exclude NPC

user_symptom_description: I've been having severe burning pain in my feet and lower legs at night that prevents sleep. The pain is like fire or electric shocks. I was treated for tuberculosis 3 months ago and finished the course last week.
urgency_reason: Painful peripheral neuropathy following isoniazid-based TB treatment is a well-known side effect from pyridoxine depletion, requiring B6 supplementation and neuropathic pain management.
recommended_action: Start pyridoxine 50mg daily; pregabalin or amitriptyline for neuropathic pain; check fasting glucose to exclude co-existing diabetic neuropathy; nerve conduction studies; reassess in 6 weeks; symptoms often partially reversible with B6.
red_flags: Ascending weakness suggesting motor involvement
No improvement at 8 weeks requiring re-evaluation for alternative diagnosis
Severe ataxia from proprioceptive loss

user_symptom_description: بنتي لاحظت عليا إني باتكلم مع ناس مش موجودين وبحسب إن في ناس بيسرقوا أغراضي من البيت. بقيت مش قادرة أنام بالليل وبقرفت من اكل. عمري 78 سنة.
urgency_reason: New-onset psychosis with insomnia and anorexia in an elderly woman requires urgent exclusion of organic causes including infection, medication toxicity, and metabolic disturbance before psychiatric diagnosis.
recommended_action: Go to ER for full delirium workup; FBC, U&E, LFTs, TFTs, blood glucose, urine culture, chest X-ray, and ECG; review all medications for anticholinergic burden; treat underlying cause; low-dose haloperidol only if agitated and distressed; old-age psychiatry referral.
red_flags: Fever suggesting infectious encephalitis requiring LP
Rapid fluctuation in consciousness suggesting delirium from sepsis
Focal neurology requiring urgent CT brain

user_symptom_description: I sustained a burn to my right forearm from boiling water 30 minutes ago. The skin is white and leathery, there is no pain in the center, but the edges are very painful and blistered. The burned area is about the size of my hand.
urgency_reason: Central painless white leathery skin indicates full-thickness burn requiring burn unit assessment, surgical debridement, and possible skin grafting.
recommended_action: Go to ER or burns unit immediately; cool the burn with running tepid water for 20 minutes; do not use ice or butter; cover with cling film loosely; IV morphine for pain at edges; burns unit referral for full-thickness areas; fluid resuscitation if large burn; tetanus update.
red_flags: Burns to face, hands, genitalia, or circumferential limb requiring immediate burns unit transfer
Inhalation injury with singed nasal hairs or hoarse voice
Total body surface area above 10% requiring Parkland formula fluid resuscitation

user_symptom_description: بقالي سنة وأنا بيجيلي عرق ليلي شديد وأضطر أبدل هدومي كذا مرة في الليل. أنا عمري 24 سنة ومش في سن اليأس وما عنديش حمى. نزلت 5 كيلو.
urgency_reason: Drenching night sweats with weight loss in a young woman without menopausal cause are B symptoms requiring urgent hematological evaluation to exclude lymphoma.
recommended_action: Urgent GP appointment; FBC with differential, LDH, ESR, CRP, and TSH; chest X-ray for mediastinal mass; CT chest-abdomen-pelvis; if lymphadenopathy present refer to haematology urgently on cancer pathway.
red_flags: Mediastinal mass causing superior vena cava syndrome with facial swelling
Painless lymphadenopathy in neck, axilla, or groin
Pruritus without rash suggesting Hodgkin's lymphoma

user_symptom_description: I've had pain and swelling in my left ankle that came on suddenly last night after a long flight. The ankle is warm and moderately swollen. I've had a similar episode in my right foot a year ago that was diagnosed as gout.
urgency_reason: Acute monoarthritis with previous gout history is almost certainly a gout flare; DVT must also be considered given the recent long-haul flight.
recommended_action: GP or urgent care within 24 hours; ankle Doppler to exclude DVT given flight history; colchicine 500mcg twice daily or naproxen for gout; serum uric acid after acute attack resolves; discuss allopurinol prevention after 4-6 weeks; urate-lowering diet.
red_flags: Calf swelling extending above ankle suggesting DVT
Fever suggesting septic arthritis requiring joint aspiration
Multiple joint involvement suggesting systemic disease

user_symptom_description: بقالها أسبوع ألم في عينيها الإتنين وأصبحت شبه ما بتشوفش بالليل وبتشوف هالة حوالين الأنوار. الألم خفيف مش حاد. عندها 75 سنة وعندها سكر من 15 سنة.
urgency_reason: Bilateral progressive visual decline with night blindness and halos in a long-standing diabetic requires urgent ophthalmology assessment for diabetic cataracts and diabetic macular edema.
recommended_action: Ophthalmology appointment within 1 week; dilated fundus examination; slit-lamp for cataracts; OCT for macular edema; IOP measurement for glaucoma; HbA1c optimization; referral for cataract surgery when vision significantly impaired.
red_flags: Sudden complete vision loss from vitreous hemorrhage in diabetic retinopathy
IOP above 30 suggesting acute glaucoma
New floaters suggesting proliferative diabetic retinopathy requiring urgent laser

user_symptom_description: I've been having severe wrist pain and weakness for the past 3 months. I dropped a cup of coffee last week because my grip suddenly failed. My two friends who work with me at the same car assembly plant have similar symptoms.
urgency_reason: Multiple workers with similar symptoms from the same assembly line suggests ergonomic work-related upper limb disorder requiring occupational health assessment alongside clinical management.
recommended_action: Nerve conduction studies; occupational health referral for workstation ergonomic assessment; wrist splints; physiotherapy; NSAIDs; modified duties; if CTS confirmed, corticosteroid injection and surgical referral; report to occupational health for workplace inspection.
red_flags: Complete thenar wasting suggesting advanced median nerve damage
Bilateral rapidly progressive weakness requiring urgent MRI cervical spine
Vasculitic features suggesting systemic cause

user_symptom_description: بنتي عمرها 8 سنين وبقالها أسبوعين بتقول إنها مش شايفة كويس من عينها الشمال وبتغمز عينها الصح. المدرسة قالت إنها دايماً قاعدة جنب السبورة.
extracted_symptoms: sitting close to board at school
urgency_reason: Unilateral visual impairment in an 8-year-old requires urgent ophthalmological assessment as the critical period for amblyopia treatment is closing — delays worsen prognosis.
recommended_action: Urgent ophthalmology appointment within 1 week; full refraction under cycloplegia; spectacle correction; occlusion therapy of the better eye for 2-6 hours daily; patching compliance monitoring; review every 6 weeks; treatment less effective after age 10.
red_flags: Whitish reflex in the eye suggesting retinoblastoma — urgent
Sudden complete vision loss suggesting optic neuritis
Squint with limited eye movement suggesting orbital disease

user_symptom_description: I've noticed my skin has been getting darker and more pigmented over the past 4 months, especially in skin folds, my knuckles, and my lips. I feel very tired and dizzy when I stand up and have been craving salty foods.
extracted_symptoms: hyperpigmentation in skin folds and knuckles
urgency_reason: Hyperpigmentation in skin folds with salt craving and orthostatic hypotension is Addison's disease until proven otherwise — an Addisonian crisis with severe stress can be fatal without steroids.
recommended_action: Urgent 9am cortisol and ACTH levels; short synacthen test; electrolytes for hyponatremia and hyperkalemia; start hydrocortisone 20mg morning and 10mg afternoon if diagnosis confirmed; carry emergency steroid card and injection kit; sick day rules education.
red_flags: Vomiting with hypotension and confusion indicating Addisonian crisis requiring IV hydrocortisone 100mg immediately
Hyperkalemia causing arrhythmia
Hypoglycemia from cortisol deficiency

user_symptom_description: عندي 78 سنة وبقالي أسبوعين ما بقدرش أحرك عيني لأعلى وبيصعب علي أحافظ على توازني وبوقع للخلف. وصوتي بقى خافت ومكتنز.
extracted_symptoms: axial postural instability with backward falls
urgency_reason: Vertical gaze palsy with axial rigidity and backward falls is pathognomonic for PSP — unlike Parkinson's it does not respond well to levodopa and has a distinct management pathway.
recommended_action: Neurology referral; MRI brain showing midbrain atrophy hummingbird sign; levodopa trial but typically poor response; physiotherapy for gait and fall prevention; speech and language therapy for dysarthria and dysphagia; advance care planning given prognosis.
red_flags: Aspiration pneumonia from dysphagia — leading cause of death in PSP
Severe falls causing subdural hematoma
Rapid cognitive decline requiring dementia care planning

user_symptom_description: I've had severe crampy diarrhea with blood and mucus for 3 days. I have a fever and feel very unwell. I ate at a street food stall 2 days before the symptoms started. My husband who ate the same food has similar symptoms.
urgency_reason: Bloody diarrhea with high fever from a suspected food source requires stool cultures and consideration of antibiotic therapy to prevent complications including reactive arthritis.
recommended_action: Urgent GP appointment; stool culture for Campylobacter, Salmonella, Shigella, E. coli O157; ORS for hydration; azithromycin for Campylobacter if severely ill; avoid antimotility agents in bloody diarrhea; notify public health if food stall implicated; go to ER if unable to tolerate fluids.
red_flags: Bloody diarrhea with E. coli O157 suggesting HUS — avoid antibiotics as they increase HUS risk
Hemodynamic instability from blood loss
Oliguria suggesting onset of hemolytic uremic syndrome

user_symptom_description: عندي 29 سنة وبقالي 6 شهور بعد ما صحيت من النوم، جسمي بيفضل مش قادر يتحرك لمدة دقيقتين أو تلاتة وأنا صاحي وشايف كل حاجة. بيجيلي أحياناً كمان تهيؤات وأنا بنام.
urgency_reason: Sleep paralysis with hypnagogic hallucinations is a narcolepsy tetrad feature requiring polysomnography and MSLT to confirm diagnosis and guide treatment.
recommended_action: Neurology referral; overnight polysomnography followed by multiple sleep latency test; HLA-DQB1*06:02 genotyping; modafinil for excessive daytime sleepiness; venlafaxine for cataplexy; strict sleep schedule; safety counseling — no unsupervised driving or swimming.
red_flags: Cataplexy causing falls from sudden complete muscle weakness
Driving incidents from sudden sleep attacks
Severe depression co-occurring with narcolepsy

user_symptom_description: I've had a severely itchy red rash with weeping blisters on my hands for months. It flares badly when I wash dishes or use cleaning products and settles with moisturizer and steroid cream. I'm a hairdresser.
urgency_reason: Occupational hand dermatitis in a hairdresser from chemical exposure is a notifiable occupational disease requiring patch testing and workplace exposure reduction to prevent permanent sensitization.
recommended_action: Dermatology referral for patch testing with hairdressing standard series; emollient barrier cream before work; nitrile gloves; topical mometasone for flares; report to occupational health; career counseling if unresponsive — hairdressers often need career change with severe contact allergy.
red_flags: Spread to forearms and face suggesting airborne sensitizer
Secondary bacterial infection with pustules
Complete inability to work due to severity

user_symptom_description: عندي 72 سنة وبقالي شهر بيجيلي ألم حاد في صدري بيشع لظهري ووصفوه بإنه بيمزق أو بيتشق. جيت من الطوارئ وقالوا إن الألم مش من القلب بس الألم لسه شديد.
extracted_symptoms: tearing or ripping chest and back pain
ER visit with normal cardiac enzymes
urgency_reason: Tearing chest to back pain with normal troponin is aortic dissection until proven otherwise — CT aortogram is mandatory and mortality increases 1-2% per hour without treatment.
recommended_action: Return to ER immediately; CT aortogram urgently; if Type A dissection involving ascending aorta — emergency cardiothoracic surgery; if Type B — IV labetalol for BP control targeting systolic below 120; vascular surgery team involvement.
red_flags: Pulse deficits between arms suggesting involvement of aortic arch
Neurological deficit from carotid involvement
Hemopericardium with cardiac tamponade

user_symptom_description: I'm a 40-year-old nurse and over the past year I've been having difficulty concentrating, feeling emotionally numb, having nightmares about patients dying, and feeling detached during patient care. I dread going to work.
urgency_reason: Burnout with compassion fatigue in a healthcare worker impairing clinical performance and personal wellbeing requires urgent occupational support and psychological intervention to prevent patient harm.
recommended_action: Urgent occupational health referral; PCL-5 for PTSD and Maslach Burnout Inventory; temporary workload reduction or leave; trauma-focused CBT or EMDR; peer support program; discuss sustainability of current role; consider structured return-to-work plan.
red_flags: Suicidal ideation — healthcare workers have elevated suicide risk
Substance use as coping mechanism
Clinical errors at work from impaired concentration

user_symptom_description: ابني عمره 3 سنين وبقاله 5 أيام حرارة مستمرة مش بتروح بالمسكنات ووجعه في مفاصله وأصابعه منتفخة. عيونه محمرة وفيه طفح على جلده.
urgency_reason: Five-day fever with conjunctivitis, rash, and joint swelling in a toddler meets Kawasaki criteria and requires emergency IVIG to prevent coronary artery aneurysm formation.
recommended_action: Go to pediatric ER immediately; echocardiogram for coronary artery assessment; CBC, CRP, ESR, and LFTs; IVIG 2g/kg single infusion plus aspirin; admission for cardiac monitoring; repeat echocardiogram at 2 and 6 weeks.
red_flags: Giant coronary aneurysm requiring anticoagulation
Myocarditis with cardiac failure
Persistent fever after IVIG dose requiring retreatment or infliximab

user_symptom_description: I've been having double vision when I look to the right for the past 3 weeks. The double vision disappears when I close one eye. I also notice my right eyelid has been drooping more than usual lately.
urgency_reason: New ptosis with diplopia from oculomotor palsy may indicate a posterior communicating artery aneurysm — a neurosurgical emergency if pupil is dilated.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; examine pupils — fixed dilated pupil is an aneurysm until proven otherwise; CT and CTA brain urgently; MRI orbit if pupil sparing to assess for ischemic nerve palsy; neurosurgical consultation; Tensilon test if myasthenia considered.
red_flags: Fixed dilated ipsilateral pupil indicating posterior communicating artery aneurysm
Severe headache suggesting subarachnoid hemorrhage
Complete external ophthalmoplegia suggesting cavernous sinus disease

user_symptom_description: بقالي 3 أشهر بيجيلي إحساس بضربات قلب قوية جداً لما بقوم من النوم أو بتحرك بسرعة. بيروح لوحده بعد دقيقة. خليت ECG وطبيعي. مفيش تاريخ مرضي.
urgency_reason: Palpitations triggered specifically by standing suggests POTS, which is underdiagnosed especially in young women and managed with non-pharmacological measures and hydration.
recommended_action: Active stand test: measure HR lying and after 10 minutes standing — rise above 30bpm confirms POTS; increase salt and water intake to 3L daily; compression stockings; regular aerobic exercise; ivabradine or fludrocortisone for refractory cases.
red_flags: Syncope from orthostatic hypotension
Resting tachycardia suggesting thyroid or anemia cause
Underlying autoimmune or neuropathic cause requiring further workup

user_symptom_description: I've had a painless bald patch on my scalp, about 5 centimeters in diameter, that appeared suddenly over a month. The skin on the bald area looks normal and smooth. I've been under a lot of work stress.
urgency_reason: Circumscribed smooth alopecia patch with normal overlying skin is alopecia areata — autoimmune in origin and often recurrent; dermatology referral is warranted for intralesional steroid consideration.
recommended_action: Dermatology appointment; pull test for activity; exclude thyroid disease and check ANA; intralesional triamcinolone injections every 4-6 weeks; topical minoxidil as adjunct; reassure most single patches regrow spontaneously; discuss immunotherapy for extensive disease.
red_flags: Total scalp hair loss suggesting alopecia totalis
Nail pitting developing alongside scalp disease
Eyebrow and eyelash involvement suggesting systemic alopecia universalis

user_symptom_description: عندي 70 سنة وبقالي أسبوع تورم في رجليا الإتنين وصعوبة في النفس لما بنام على ظهري. بيجيلي سعال جاف بالليل وبلاقي نفسي بحاجة لـ 4 وسادات.
urgency_reason: Orthopnea requiring 4 pillows with bilateral edema and nocturnal cough in an elderly woman indicates severe acute decompensated heart failure requiring immediate diuresis.
recommended_action: Go to ER immediately; BNP or NT-proBNP; chest X-ray for pulmonary edema; ECG and echocardiogram; IV furosemide 40-80mg; oxygen supplementation; weigh daily; reduce fluid and salt intake; cardiology admission.
red_flags: Oxygen saturation below 88% requiring BiPAP
Pink frothy sputum from acute pulmonary edema
Cardiogenic shock with hypotension requiring inotropes

user_symptom_description: I've had severe pain in my right hip that started after a long run last week. The pain is in my outer hip and thigh, burning in quality, worse sitting on hard surfaces, and tender when I press on the outer bony prominence.
urgency_reason: Lateral hip pain with trochanteric tenderness after a running injury is gluteal tendinopathy or trochanteric bursitis, managed with load modification and physiotherapy.
recommended_action: Hip ultrasound to assess gluteal tendons; avoid crossing legs and direct pressure on hip; physiotherapy for gluteal strengthening and load management; corticosteroid injection into trochanteric bursa if 6 weeks physiotherapy fails; avoid direct tendon injection.
red_flags: Night pain with constitutional symptoms suggesting bone metastases
Groin pain suggesting intra-articular hip pathology
Neurological symptoms suggesting L4/L5 radiculopathy mimicking hip pain

user_symptom_description: بقاله سنتين ابني عمره 15 سنة بيشرب ويبول كتير جداً، بيصحى 4 مرات في الليل علشان يشرب ميه. وزنه نزل 8 كيلو. بيحس بتعب ومش قادر يركز في المذاكرة.
urgency_reason: Classic triad of polyuria, polydipsia, and weight loss over 2 years with fatigue in an adolescent is type 1 diabetes requiring urgent blood glucose and DKA exclusion.
recommended_action: Go to pediatric urgent care today for random blood glucose; if above 11mmol/L or ketonuria, go to pediatric ER for DKA workup; HbA1c; insulin initiation under pediatric diabetology; CGM device; diabetes education for family; school diabetic care plan.
red_flags: Deep rapid breathing suggesting Kussmaul breathing from ketoacidosis
Fruity breath odor from ketones
Altered consciousness requiring immediate IV insulin and fluids

user_symptom_description: I've had a constant ringing sound in my left ear for 2 months that gets louder when it's quiet and keeps me awake at night. I have no hearing loss. I'm 45 and work in a noisy factory but always wear ear protection.
urgency_reason: Unilateral tinnitus for over 6 weeks requires audiometry and MRI internal auditory meatus to exclude acoustic neuroma before tinnitus retraining therapy.
recommended_action: ENT referral; audiometry; MRI internal auditory meatus; tinnitus retraining therapy; sound enrichment white noise device; CBT for tinnitus distress; avoid silence; avoid caffeine and alcohol which worsen tinnitus.
red_flags: Pulsatile tinnitus synchronous with heartbeat suggesting vascular lesion or glomus tumor
Asymmetric hearing loss on audiometry suggesting acoustic neuroma
Tinnitus with sudden hearing loss suggesting SSNHL

user_symptom_description: بقالي شهرين بيجيلي ألم شديد وتورم في مفصل ركبتي اليمين كل أسبوعين تقريباً وبيروح لوحده في 3-4 أيام. في الأشعة قالوا في ترسبات على الغضروف.
urgency_reason: Episodic acute knee arthritis with chondrocalcinosis is pseudogout — metabolic causes including hyperparathyroidism and hemochromatosis must be excluded in recurrent cases.
recommended_action: Rheumatology referral; joint aspiration during an episode for weakly positive birefringent crystals on polarized microscopy; calcium, PTH, ferritin, and magnesium; treat acute attack with NSAIDs or colchicine; low-dose colchicine prophylaxis for frequent attacks.
red_flags: Fever during attack requiring joint aspiration to exclude septic arthritis
Secondary hyperparathyroidism causing hypercalcemia
Joint destruction from chronic CPPD arthropathy

user_symptom_description: I found a black mole on my back that has been changing. It now has irregular jagged edges, is multiple shades of brown, black, and has a slightly raised area in the center. It's about 9mm. My father had melanoma.
